Unique identifier for the cold chain monitoring event record.
Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Cold‑chain compliance monitoring per batch requires linking temperature events to the batch record.

Unique identifier for the transportation carrier responsible for the cold chain asset during transit.
User identifier of the quality assurance or food safety personnel who reviewed the cold chain event and made the disposition decision.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Temperature excursion reports must reference the product registration to satisfy FDA and FSMA traceability requirements.
Unique identifier for the shipment or load associated with this cold chain monitoring event.
Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Cold‑chain monitoring must associate temperature excursions with the specific SKU to satisfy FSMA/HACCP audit and product recall processes.
Boolean indicator of whether an automated alert notification was sent to responsible personnel when the excursion was detected (True if sent, False if not sent).
Comma-separated list of email addresses or user IDs that received the temperature excursion alert notification.
Unique identifier or code for the specific cold chain asset (e.g., trailer number, zone code, reefer unit serial number).

Sugar content measurement in degrees Brix if applicable to the product being monitored (primarily for beverages and fruit products).
Description of the corrective action implemented in response to a temperature excursion or monitoring alert.
Date and time when corrective action was initiated in response to the temperature excursion.
Date and time when the cold chain event record was first created in the system.
Code identifying the destination location (retail store, distribution center, or customer site) for the cold chain shipment.
Final disposition decision for the product following temperature excursion review (approved for sale, rejected, quarantined, pending review, or destroyed).
Date and time when the temperature monitoring event was recorded in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Total duration in minutes that the temperature remained outside the acceptable range during the excursion event.
Boolean indicator of whether the recorded temperature exceeded the acceptable range (True if excursion occurred, False if within range).
Classification of the temperature excursion severity based on duration and deviation magnitude (minor, moderate, major, or critical).

Geographic longitude coordinate of the cold chain asset at the time of the temperature reading.

Date and time when the cold chain event record was last modified or updated.
Free-text notes or comments regarding the cold chain event, excursion circumstances, or corrective actions taken.
Code identifying the origin location (distribution center, manufacturing plant, or warehouse) where the cold chain monitoring began.

Unique identifier for the temperature monitoring sensor or device that captured the reading.

Unique identifier for the fulfillment order record. Primary key for the fulfillment order entity representing a customer order released to the distribution center for picking, packing, and shipping.

Reference to the transportation carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider assigned to transport this order. Used for carrier performance tracking and freight cost allocation.
Reference to the distribution center or warehouse facility responsible for fulfilling this order. Identifies the source location for inventory picking and shipping operations.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Captures the employee who created the order, supporting auditability and accountability in order processing.

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Required for the Order Pricing Audit report, which tracks which price list version was applied to each fulfillment order for compliance and margin analysis.
Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Traceability report links each fulfillment order to the production order that created the shipped product.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Assigns revenue from each fulfillment order to a profit center for profitability analysis and reporting.
Identifier for the delivery route to which this fulfillment order has been assigned. Used for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) route optimization and last-mile logistics planning.
Reference to the originating sales order that triggered this fulfillment order. Links the distribution execution back to the commercial transaction.
Reference to the destination location where goods will be delivered. May represent a retail store, distribution center, foodservice location, or direct-to-consumer address.
Identifier for the consolidated shipment that includes this fulfillment order. Multiple orders may be combined into a single shipment for transportation efficiency.
Identifier for the picking wave or batch to which this fulfillment order has been assigned. Waves group multiple orders for efficient picking and resource optimization.
The actual date the order was delivered to the customer location. Captured from proof of delivery (POD) or carrier tracking systems. Used for OTIF performance measurement.
The actual date the order shipped from the distribution center. Used for OTIF performance measurement and variance analysis against planned and requested dates.
Advanced Shipping Notice document number transmitted to the customer via Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) prior to shipment arrival. Enables customer receiving preparation and inventory planning.
The date and time the Advanced Shipping Notice was transmitted to the customer. Used for EDI compliance tracking and customer service level measurement.

Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains temperature-sensitive products requiring cold chain compliance throughout storage and transportation. True indicates refrigerated or frozen handling required; false indicates ambient temperature handling.
The date and time this fulfillment order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

Indicates whether this fulfillment order is designated for cross-docking operations, where goods move directly from receiving to shipping without intermediate storage. True indicates cross-dock handling; false indicates standard warehouse flow with putaway and storage.
The precise date and time the order was delivered to the customer. Captured from proof of delivery or carrier tracking. Used for OTIF measurement and service level compliance.

Code identifying the type of exception or disruption encountered during fulfillment execution. Examples include inventory shortage, damaged product, carrier delay, address error, or customer request change. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.
Detailed description of the exception or disruption encountered during fulfillment. Provides context for resolution actions and root cause investigation.

Current status of exception resolution efforts. Open indicates exception has been identified but not yet addressed; in_progress indicates resolution actions are underway; resolved indicates exception has been successfully resolved; escalated indicates exception requires management intervention.

The percentage of the order quantity that was successfully fulfilled and shipped. Calculated as shipped quantity divided by ordered quantity. Used for service level measurement and inventory availability analysis.

The business channel through which this order is being fulfilled. Determines handling requirements, service level expectations, and routing logic. Retail represents traditional grocery and mass merchandiser channels; foodservice represents restaurant and institutional channels; direct-to-consumer (DTC) represents online orders shipped to individual consumers; direct store delivery (DSD) represents orders delivered directly to retail stores bypassing DC consolidation.
Business-readable unique identifier for the fulfillment order. Used for operational tracking, communication with warehouse staff, and customer service inquiries.

Current lifecycle status of the fulfillment order within the distribution execution process. Released indicates order is available for wave assignment; wave_assigned indicates order is assigned to a picking wave; picking indicates order is actively being picked; picked indicates picking is complete; packing indicates order is being packed; packed indicates packing is complete; staged indicates order is staged for loading; loaded indicates order is loaded on carrier vehicle; shipped indicates order has departed the DC; in_transit indicates order is en route to destination; delivered indicates order has been received by customer; exception indicates order has encountered a problem requiring intervention; cancelled indicates order has been cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: released|wave_assigned|picking|picked|packing|packed|staged|loaded|shipped|in_transit|delivered|exception|cancelled — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and transportation compliance. True indicates hazmat present; false indicates no hazmat.
The date and time this fulfillment order record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.
The date and time the fulfillment order was released from the order management system to the warehouse management system for execution. Marks the start of the fulfillment lifecycle.

Classification of the fulfillment order based on handling and processing requirements. Standard represents normal replenishment orders; rush represents expedited orders requiring priority handling; cross-dock represents orders that bypass storage and move directly from receiving to shipping; drop-ship represents orders shipped directly from supplier to customer; sample represents non-commercial product samples; promotional represents orders tied to marketing campaigns or trade promotions.

Indicates whether this fulfillment order is included in On-Time In-Full performance measurement. True indicates order is subject to OTIF service level agreements; false indicates order is excluded from OTIF metrics (e.g., sample orders, internal transfers).
The date and time when all picking operations were completed for this fulfillment order. Used for cycle time analysis and throughput measurement.
The date and time when picking operations began for this fulfillment order. Used for cycle time analysis and warehouse productivity measurement.
The date the distribution center plans to ship the order based on capacity, inventory availability, and carrier schedules. May differ from requested ship date due to operational constraints.

Priority level assigned to the fulfillment order for warehouse resource allocation and sequencing. Critical represents emergency orders requiring immediate processing; high represents time-sensitive orders with tight delivery windows; normal represents standard priority orders; low represents orders that can be delayed if capacity constraints exist.
The date committed to the customer for delivery of the order. Used for On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance measurement and customer service level tracking.
The date the customer or sales order requested the order to ship from the distribution center. Used for planning and scheduling warehouse operations.
The precise date and time the order departed the distribution center. Used for transit time calculation and carrier performance tracking.

description: Current lifecycle status of the religious certification. Active indicates the certification is valid and in effect. Expired indicates the certificate has passed its expiry date. Type of religious dietary certification. Halal certifies compliance with Islamic dietary law. Kosher certifications include Pareve (neutral, contains no meat or dairy), Dairy (contains dairy or dairy equipment), Meat (contains meat or meat equipment), and Passover (meets additional Passover requirements).

description: Indicates whether using the substitute ingredient requires changes to product labeling, including ingredient list, allergen statements, or nutritional panel.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the substitution record. Tracks change history for audit and compliance purposes.
Date of the most recent periodic review of the substitution rule. Used to track compliance with review frequency requirements.
Detailed notes on how the substitution affects manufacturing processes, including equipment settings, processing times, temperature adjustments, and yield impacts.

Maximum allowable percentage of the primary ingredient that can be replaced by the substitute in a formulation. Range 0.00 to 100.00. Critical for maintaining product quality and regulatory compliance.
Username or employee identifier of the person who last modified the substitution record. Supports audit trail and accountability.
reference: Veeva Vault Quality Management Audit Trail

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the substitution rule. Calculated based on last review date and review frequency.
Indicates whether the substitute ingredient maintains the same organic certification status as the primary ingredient. Critical for products with USDA Organic or equivalent certifications.

Ranking of this substitution option when multiple substitutes exist for the same primary ingredient. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by manufacturing execution systems to select optimal substitute.
Reference to the quality specification document that defines acceptance criteria for the substitute ingredient, including Brix, pH, CFU (Colony Forming Units), and other quality parameters.

Indicates whether the substitution requires formal regulatory review and approval before implementation. True when substitution impacts nutritional panel, allergen declaration, or regulatory compliance.
reference: FDA (Food and Drug Administration) Labeling Regulations

description: Number of days between periodic reviews of the substitution rule. Ensures ongoing validation of functional equivalence, cost impact, and regulatory compliance.

Evaluation of the substitutes impact on organoleptic properties (taste, texture, aroma, appearance). None = no detectable difference, Minor = slight variation within acceptable range, Moderate = noticeable but acceptable, Significant = substantial change requiring consumer testing.
Business identifier for the substitution rule. Externally-known code used in manufacturing and procurement systems.

Current lifecycle status of the substitution rule. Tracks approval workflow and operational readiness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_review|approved|active|suspended|expired|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the substitution rule. Full = complete replacement at 100%, Partial = limited percentage replacement, Emergency = crisis-driven short-term use, Temporary = time-bound approval, Permanent = long-term approved alternative.
Status of supplier qualification and approval for the substitute ingredient. Tracks whether the substitute ingredient supplier has passed quality audits and compliance checks.
reference: GFSI (Global Food Safety Initiative) Supplier Approval

description: Business justification for establishing the substitution rule. Examples: primary ingredient supply risk, cost optimization, seasonal availability, supplier diversification, quality improvement.
Indicates whether the substitute ingredient requires lot-level traceability tracking for FSMA compliance and recall readiness. True for high-risk ingredients.
reference: FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) Traceability Rule

description: Number of times the substitution rule has been applied in production batches. Tracks actual utilization and validates business value of maintaining the substitution rule.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Order Management

description: Primary key for fsma_traceability

Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: Regulatory FSMA reports must capture the specific asset generating the traceability lot code, ensuring equipment‑level provenance for ingredient batches.
Reference to the ingredient or raw material subject to FSMA traceability requirements. Links to the raw material master for ingredients on the Food Traceability List (FTL).
Foreign key linking to distribution.shipment. Business justification: Regulatory Traceability Requirement: FSMA records must reference the specific shipment carrying the raw material.
Reference to the immediate previous source (IPS) supplier who provided the ingredient. Required for receiving CTE traceability.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Immediate Previous Source

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FSMA traceability verification is performed by a qualified employee; verifier_employee_id links verification to responsible staff for regulatory reporting.
Current compliance status of this traceability record against FSMA Section 204 requirements. Indicates whether all required KDEs are present and accurate.
Date when the ingredient was cooled (for produce and other temperature-sensitive items). Required KDE for certain FTL items.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Key Data Elements for Specific Foods

description: Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where the ingredient was produced or harvested. Required for import traceability and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3, FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S

description: Timestamp when this traceability record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record creation.
Date when the Critical Tracking Event occurred. Required Key Data Element (KDE) for FSMA 204 compliance. Must enable 24-hour FDA traceability response.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Key Data Elements

description: Precise timestamp when the Critical Tracking Event occurred, including time zone. Provides granular traceability for time-sensitive food safety investigations.

Type of Critical Tracking Event as defined by FSMA Section 204. Receiving CTE is owned by ingredient domain; transformation and shipping CTEs are owned by manufacturing and distribution domains respectively.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Critical Tracking Events

description: Name of the source system from which this traceability record was captured (e.g., SAP Batch Management, TraceGains, FoodLogiQ, Rfxcel). Enables data lineage tracking.
Reason for exemption from FSMA 204 traceability requirements, if applicable. Certain small businesses and specific food types may be exempt.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Exemptions

business_glossary_term: Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Facility Registration Number
description: FDA-assigned facility registration number for the location where the CTE occurred. Required KDE for FSMA 204 compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - FDA Facility Registration

description: Classification of the ingredient according to the FDA Food Traceability List. Identifies which FTL category the ingredient belongs to (e.g., leafy greens, fresh-cut fruit, shell eggs, nut butters, finfish, crustaceans, mollusks, prepared ready-to-eat deli salads, tropical tree fruits, cheeses).
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Food Traceability List

description: Standardized code identifying the specific FTL item. Used for consistent classification across the supply chain.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Food Traceability List

description: GS1 Global Location Number identifying the physical location where the CTE occurred. Provides standardized location identification for supply chain traceability.
Geographic location where the ingredient was grown or harvested. Required KDE for certain FTL items to enable rapid source tracing during outbreaks.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Key Data Elements for Specific Foods

description: Date when the ingredient was harvested (for agricultural products). Required KDE for certain FTL items such as leafy greens and fresh produce.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Key Data Elements for Specific Foods

description: Physical address of the immediate previous source facility. Required for FSMA traceability to enable rapid source identification during food safety events.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Immediate Previous Source

description: Name of the immediate previous source (supplier, grower, processor) who provided the ingredient. Required KDE for receiving CTE.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Immediate Previous Source

description: Contact phone number for the immediate previous source. Enables rapid communication during food safety investigations and recalls.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Immediate Previous Source

description: Timestamp when this traceability record was last modified. Enables tracking of record changes for audit and compliance purposes.
Description of the physical location where the CTE occurred. May include facility name, warehouse, production line, or receiving dock identifier.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Location Information

description: Additional notes or comments related to this traceability event. May include special handling instructions, quality observations, or investigation findings.
Date when the ingredient was initially packed. Required KDE for certain FTL items to track the packing CTE.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Key Data Elements for Specific Foods

description: Description of the ingredient or food product subject to traceability. Required KDE for FSMA 204 compliance to clearly identify the traceable item.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Key Data Elements

description: Quantity of ingredient received, transformed, or shipped in this Critical Tracking Event. Required KDE for FSMA 204 compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Key Data Elements

description: Unique identifier of the source document supporting this traceability record. Links the CTE to the originating business transaction or production event.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Reference Documents

description: Type of source document supporting this traceability record. Examples include Bill of Lading (BOL), Purchase Order (PO), Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN), production record, or Certificate of Analysis (COA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BOL|PO|ASN|production_record|COA|receiving_report|batch_record|shipping_manifest — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Reference Documents

description: Origin of the traceability lot code. Indicates whether the TLC was assigned by the supplier, internally generated, or provided by the original grower/processor.
Reference identifier linking the TLC to the source system or document where it was originally assigned. Enables upstream traceability to the immediate previous source.
Unique lot code assigned to the ingredient batch for FSMA traceability purposes. The TLC is the core identifier linking all CTEs for a specific ingredient lot throughout the supply chain.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Traceability Lot Code

description: Version identifier of the FSMA traceability plan under which this record was created. Enables audit trail of traceability process changes.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Traceability Plan

description: Unit of measure for the quantity field (e.g., kg, lbs, gallons, cases). Required KDE for FSMA 204 compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Key Data Elements

description: Date when this traceability record was last verified for accuracy and completeness. Supports audit readiness for FDA inspections.
Status of data verification for this traceability record. Indicates whether the record has been validated against source documents and supplier data.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: FSMA traceability requires linking each ingredient lot to the equipment that processed it, enabling recall, compliance reporting, and equipment‑level accountability.
Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Inventory valuation: lot balances are reported per legal entity (company code) for financial statements and compliance.
Foreign key linking to regulatory.fsma_record. Business justification: FSMA compliance requires each ingredient lot to be associated with a specific FSMA record for traceability and audit.

Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: FSMA traceability: link ingredient lot to sales order to identify which orders contain the lot for recall and compliance reporting.

Unit of measure for the rated capacity value, indicating how production throughput is quantified for this equipment.
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this equipment is designed for automated clean-in-place sanitation without disassembly, enabling efficient changeover and sanitation cycles.
Date when the equipment was validated, qualified, and approved for production use following installation and testing.
Timestamp when this equipment master record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.
Business criticality classification of the equipment based on its impact to production continuity, safety, and quality. Critical equipment requires highest priority maintenance and monitoring.
Date when the equipment was permanently removed from service and decommissioned, marking the end of its operational lifecycle.
Business justification or reason for decommissioning the equipment (e.g., end of life, obsolescence, facility closure, technology upgrade).
Rated or average energy consumption of the equipment per operating hour, used for sustainability tracking and operational cost analysis.
High-level grouping of equipment by operational category for reporting and analysis purposes.
Human-readable descriptive name of the equipment asset (e.g., 'Line 3 Filling Machine', 'Pasteurizer Unit A').

Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the equipment asset for operational tracking and maintenance purposes. This is the externally-known business identifier used in MES systems, work orders, and maintenance schedules.

Classification of the equipment by its primary production function within the manufacturing process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filling_machine|mixer|blender|pasteurizer|extruder|packaging_line|labeler|metal_detector|checkweigher|cip_system|oven|cooler|conveyor|palletizer|wrapper|sealer — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this equipment has surfaces that come into direct contact with food products, requiring specific sanitation and material compliance standards.
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this equipment is classified as GMP-critical, requiring enhanced documentation, validation, and change control procedures.
Date when the equipment was physically installed at the manufacturing facility.
Date when the equipment was last calibrated or verified for measurement accuracy, applicable to equipment requiring calibration per GMP or quality standards.
Timestamp when this equipment master record was last updated, supporting change tracking and audit trail requirements.
Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) who produced the equipment asset.
Unique identifier used by the MES system (Aveva/Wonderware) to track this equipment in real-time production monitoring, batch execution, and performance analytics.
Manufacturer's model designation for the equipment, used for parts ordering and technical documentation reference.
Scheduled date for the next required calibration or verification activity to maintain equipment qualification status.
Free-text field for additional comments, special handling instructions, or contextual information about the equipment that does not fit structured fields.

business_glossary_term: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) Baseline Target Percent
description: Target OEE percentage established as the performance baseline for this equipment, used for monitoring and continuous improvement initiatives. OEE measures availability, performance, and quality.
Current operational state of the equipment indicating its availability for production use.
Reference code linking this equipment to its preventive maintenance plan schedule defining frequency and tasks for routine maintenance activities.
Maximum production capacity of the equipment as specified by the manufacturer or validated during commissioning, expressed in the unit of measure appropriate to the equipment type.
Reference code or identifier for safety certifications held by this equipment (e.g., UL, CE, CSA), documenting compliance with electrical and mechanical safety standards.
Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific equipment unit for warranty and service tracking.
Reference identifier for the technical specification document containing detailed engineering specifications, operating parameters, and maintenance procedures for this equipment.
Date when the manufacturer's warranty coverage for this equipment expires, after which repairs and parts are no longer covered under warranty.
Unique identifier for the manufacturing plant, co-packing facility, or production site. Primary key for the plant master data product.
Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Each plant must be linked to its regulatory establishment registration for audit and reporting.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Links plant manager to employee record for compliance, audit trails, and hierarchical reporting in plant‑level dashboards.
Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Logistics planning maps each plant to a distribution network node for transportation scheduling and OTIF performance reporting.

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: REGIONAL PRICING: Plant location determines applicable price zone for pricing rules; price zone assignment is required for price list selection and compliance reporting.
Primary street address line for the plant location including street number and name.
Secondary address line for additional location details such as building number, suite, or floor.
Unit of measure for the daily capacity metric (e.g., cases, pallets, kilograms, pounds, liters, gallons) aligned with the primary output of the facility.
City or municipality where the plant is located.
Refrigerated and frozen storage capacity at the plant site measured in standard pallet positions for temperature-sensitive products and ingredients.
Date when the plant was first commissioned and began commercial production operations.
Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where the plant is located (e.g., USA, MEX, CAN, DEU, CHN).
Timestamp when this plant master data record was first created in the system.
Maximum daily production capacity of the plant measured in standard production units per day. Used for capacity planning, demand allocation, and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculations.
Date when the plant was permanently closed and ceased all production operations. Null for active facilities.

Plant code or site identifier used in the primary ERP system (SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Cloud ERP) for transactional processing, inventory management, and financial reporting. May differ from the business plant_code for legacy integration reasons.

business_glossary_term: Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Registration Number
description: FDA facility registration number required for food manufacturing facilities under FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) regulations. Mandatory for US-based facilities and importers.
reference: FDA FSMA Section 102 Food Facility Registration

description: Expiration date of the current GFSI certification requiring renewal to maintain compliance and customer approval status.
reference: GFSI Global Food Safety Initiative Benchmarking Requirements

description: The GFSI-benchmarked food safety certification scheme held by the plant. SQF (Safe Quality Food), BRC (British Retail Consortium), FSSC 22000 (Food Safety System Certification), IFS (International Featured Standards), or none if not certified.
reference: GFSI Global Food Safety Initiative Benchmarking Requirements

description: Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds current GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) certification required for food and beverage manufacturing quality standards.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117 Current Good Manufacturing Practice

description: Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds halal certification from a recognized Islamic authority enabling production of halal-certified products.
reference: Islamic Food and Nutrition Council of America (IFANCA) Standards

description: Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds kosher certification from a recognized rabbinical authority enabling production of kosher-certified products.
Timestamp when this plant master data record was most recently updated or modified.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the plant location in decimal degrees format for mapping and logistics optimization.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the plant location in decimal degrees format for mapping and logistics optimization.
Site or facility identifier used in the Manufacturing Execution System (Aveva/Wonderware MES) for production scheduling, batch management, and OEE tracking.
Total count of active production lines or manufacturing lines within the plant used for production scheduling and line allocation.
Standard number of operating days per week for the plant (typically 5, 6, or 7) used for production planning and capacity calculations.
Standard operating hours per day for the plant used for capacity planning and OEE calculations. Typically 8, 16, or 24 hours depending on shift structure.
Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds organic certification (USDA Organic, EU Organic, or equivalent) enabling production of certified organic products.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205 National Organic Program

description: Name of the accredited organic certification body that issued the organic certification (e.g., QAI, CCOF, Oregon Tilth, Ecocert).
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205 National Organic Program

description: Business identifier code for the plant used across operational systems. Typically a 4-10 character alphanumeric code used in ERP systems (SAP plant code, Oracle site code).
Official business name of the manufacturing plant or production facility.

Current operational status of the plant in its lifecycle. Active indicates full production operations, idle indicates temporarily not producing but maintained, decommissioned indicates permanently closed, under construction indicates new facility being built, and temporarily closed indicates short-term shutdown for maintenance or seasonal operations.

Classification of the facility type indicating ownership and operational model. Owned manufacturing indicates company-operated production facilities, co-packer indicates third-party contract manufacturing, toll manufacturer indicates facilities where the company provides ingredients and the manufacturer provides processing, pilot plant indicates small-scale testing facilities, R&D facility indicates research and development sites, and distribution center indicates warehousing and logistics hubs.
Postal or ZIP code for the plant address used for mail delivery and logistics routing.
Primary product category or product line manufactured at this plant (e.g., beverages, snacks, dairy, bakery, confectionery) used for portfolio planning and specialization tracking.
Business-defined geographic region or market area for reporting and planning purposes (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, Latin America).
State, province, or regional administrative division where the plant is located.
IANA time zone identifier for the plant location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Shanghai) used for production scheduling and shift management.

business_glossary_term: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Establishment Number
description: USDA establishment number assigned to facilities that process meat, poultry, or egg products under USDA inspection. Required for facilities producing USDA-regulated products.
reference: USDA FSIS 9 CFR Part 304 Application for Inspection

description: Total warehouse storage capacity at the plant site measured in standard pallet positions for finished goods and raw material inventory management.
Unique identifier for the production routing master record.
Reference to the manufacturing plant where this routing is executed.
Reference to the employee who approved this routing for production use.
Reference to the employee who created this routing record.
Reference to the production planner or scheduler responsible for maintaining this routing and resolving production issues.
Reference to the employee who last modified this routing record.
Reference to the third-party co-packing supplier if this routing represents externally manufactured production. Null for internal production.

Reference to the toll manufacturing supplier if this routing represents production where the company provides raw materials and the supplier provides processing. Null for internal or co-packed production.
Reference to the finished good SKU that this routing produces.
Reference to the primary production line or work center group where this routing is executed. Used for capacity planning and scheduling.
Indicates whether this routing requires special allergen handling procedures, such as line changeover cleaning or dedicated equipment, to prevent cross-contact.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA)

description: The current approval state of this routing. Approved routings are released for production use; pending routings await engineering or quality approval; rejected routings require rework; expired routings have passed their review date.
The date and time when this routing was approved for production use.
The standard production lot size for which the routing times are defined. Actual production order times are scaled proportionally from this base.
The unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., KG, LB, EA, CS). Aligns with the SKU primary UOM.
The standard time required to change over the production line from the previous SKU to this routing, including cleaning, setup, and first-article inspection.
The date and time when this routing record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether this routing has been validated for GMP compliance. True means all operations meet GMP documentation, training, and control requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 110 - Good Manufacturing Practice

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Flag
description: Indicates whether this routing contains one or more HACCP critical control points (CCPs) that require monitoring and documentation per the food safety plan.
The date when this routing was last reviewed and validated by engineering or production management.
The minimum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Supports lot-size-dependent routing selection.
The maximum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Null indicates no upper limit.
The largest production quantity that can be produced in a single batch using this routing, constrained by equipment capacity or process limits.
The smallest production quantity that can be economically or technically produced using this routing, expressed in the base UOM.
The date and time when this routing record was last modified.
The scheduled date for the next periodic review of this routing to ensure continued accuracy and compliance.
The total number of sequential operations (work center steps) defined in this routing.
Indicates whether this routing is certified for organic production under USDA NOP or equivalent standards, requiring segregation and documentation.
Indicates whether non-conforming product from this routing can be reworked through a defined rework operation sequence.
Free-text description of the routing, typically summarizing the production method or key process characteristics (e.g., 'High-speed aseptic filling line', 'Manual batch mixing and packaging').
Business identifier for the routing. The externally-known unique code used to reference this routing in production planning and scheduling systems.

Current lifecycle status of the routing. Active routings are available for production order creation; draft and under-review routings are in development; inactive and obsolete routings are no longer used.

Classification of the routing. Standard routings are the default production method; alternative routings provide flexibility for capacity or material constraints; trial routings support NPD; co-packing and toll manufacturing routings reflect external production arrangements.
The expected percentage of material loss or waste across all operations in this routing, used for material planning and costing.
The sum of all operation standard times (setup, machine, labor) across the routing, expressed in minutes. Used for capacity planning and production scheduling.

The business purpose for which this routing is maintained. Production routings drive MES execution; costing routings feed standard cost calculations; maintenance and quality inspection routings support non-production work orders.
The date from which this routing version becomes effective for production planning and scheduling.
The date until which this routing version remains effective. Null indicates open-ended validity.
Version identifier for the routing. Allows multiple versions of the same routing to coexist for different time periods or production scenarios.
Unique identifier for the changeover event. Primary key for the changeover data product.
Identifier of the QA (Quality Assurance) operator who signed off on the changeover verification.
Identifier of the production order that was running before the changeover event.
Identifier of the production order that will run after the changeover event.
Identifier of the sanitation crew lead responsible for executing the changeover and cleaning procedures.
Identifier of the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that was being produced before the changeover.
Identifier of the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that will be produced after the changeover.
Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the changeover occurred.
Identifier of the work center or production line where the changeover occurred.
Actual duration of the changeover event in minutes from start to completion.
Actual end date and time when the changeover event was completed in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Actual start date and time when the changeover event began in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Indicates whether allergen clearance was verified and approved. True if verified, False if not applicable or not verified.
Allergen present in the SKU being produced before the changeover. Used for allergen management and clearance verification.
reference: FDA Food and Drug Administration Allergen Labeling

description: Allergen present in the SKU being produced after the changeover. Used for allergen management and clearance verification.
reference: FDA Food and Drug Administration Allergen Labeling

business_glossary_term: Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) Relative Light Units (RLU) Value
description: Numeric RLU (Relative Light Units) value from the ATP swab test. Lower values indicate cleaner surfaces.
Result of the ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) swab test used to detect organic contamination on surfaces.
CFU (Colony Forming Units) count from the microbiological swab test. Indicates the level of bacterial contamination.
Name or code of the chemical cleaning agent used during the sanitation process.
Concentration level of the chemical agent used, expressed as a percentage.
Indicates whether the CIP (Clean-in-Place) cycle was completed successfully. True if completed, False if not applicable or incomplete.
Timestamp when the changeover record was first created in the system in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Deviation from planned duration in minutes. Positive values indicate the changeover took longer than planned; negative values indicate it was faster.
Free-text notes explaining any deviations, issues, or special circumstances encountered during the changeover.
Code indicating the reason for any deviation from the planned changeover duration or procedure.
Current lifecycle status of the changeover event.

Type of changeover event. CIP = Clean-in-Place, COP = Clean-out-of-Place. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flavor_to_flavor|allergen_to_allergen|allergen_to_non_allergen|non_allergen_to_allergen|format_change|cip|cop|dry_clean|full_sanitation|allergen_clean|scheduled_sanitation|unscheduled_sanitation — promote to reference product]
Timestamp when the changeover record was last modified in the system in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Indicates whether the line clearance checklist was completed. True if completed, False if incomplete.
Result of the microbiological swab test to detect bacterial contamination.
reference: HACCP Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points

description: Overall pass or fail status of the changeover event based on all verification tests and procedures.
Planned duration of the changeover event in minutes based on standard operating procedures.
Indicates whether production clearance was granted to resume operations after the changeover. True if granted, False if pending or denied.
Result of the rinse verification test to ensure all chemical residues have been removed.
reference: HACCP Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points

description: Standard operating procedure code for the sanitation method used during the changeover.
Type of sanitation procedure performed during the changeover event. None indicates no sanitation was required.
reference: HACCP Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points

description: Reference code linking the changeover event to the production schedule or planning system.
Planned end date and time for the changeover event in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Planned start date and time for the changeover event in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Unique identifier for the lot consumption transaction record. Primary key for the lot consumption entity.
Reference to the batch record documenting the production run. Links lot consumption to the specific batch manufacturing execution record for GMP compliance and traceability.
reference: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) Batch Documentation Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for Material Consumption Costing and variance analysis; cost_center_code is a denormalized code that should reference finance.cost_center.

Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: REQUIRED: Enables lot‑level traceability for recalls and FDA FSMA reporting by linking each consumption record to the specific ingredient lot used.
Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Lot consumption records occur at a plant; linking to plant provides authoritative plant data and removes the free‑text plant_code.
Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Linking consumption to a specific PO line enables variance analysis and accurate spend tracking.
Reference to the production order against which this lot consumption occurred. Links consumption to the manufacturing order that triggered the material usage.
Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Material consumption is allocated to specific promotions for accurate cost‑of‑goods‑sold and promotional spend reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: REQUIRED: Links each consumption event to the exact inventory lot consumed, supporting lot traceability, regulatory reporting, and OTIF calculations.
Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Identifying the supplier of each consumed lot supports quality audits and allergen compliance.
Reference to the work center or production line where the material was consumed. Links consumption to the specific manufacturing resource.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Master

description: Indicator of whether the consumed material contains or may contain allergens as defined by FDA and FALCPA regulations. Used for allergen control and cross-contamination prevention.
reference: FALCPA (Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act)

description: Flag indicating whether this consumption was automatically backflushed based on production confirmation (True) or manually posted (False). Backflushing automates material consumption based on BOM and production output.
The planned or standard quantity of this material specified in the Bill of Materials for the production order. Used to calculate consumption variance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Bill of Materials

description: The current status of the consumption transaction in the system workflow: confirmed (posted to inventory), pending (awaiting approval), rejected (not accepted), or reversed (cancelled after posting).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement Status

description: The quantity of material consumed from the specified lot during production. Recorded in the unit of measure specified in the unit_of_measure field.
The precise date and time when the material lot was consumed in the production process. Critical for FIFO/FEFO compliance and production timeline tracking.
reference: MES (Manufacturing Execution System) Time Stamping Standard

description: Classification of the consumption event indicating the purpose or nature of material usage: standard production, rework/reprocessing, trial run, quality sample, or waste/scrap.
The user ID or username of the person who created this consumption record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.
The date and time when this lot consumption record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data governance and compliance.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the cost values are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

business_glossary_term: FIFO/FEFO (First In First Out/First Expired First Out) Sequence Number
description: Sequential number indicating the order in which lots were consumed according to FIFO or FEFO inventory management rules. Lower numbers indicate earlier consumption priority.
Classification of the consumed material regarding genetically modified organism content: GMO (contains GMO), non-GMO (verified non-GMO), or unknown (not tested/declared).
reference: Non-GMO Project Standard and FDA GMO Labeling Guidance

description: Indicator of whether the consumed lot is certified halal according to Islamic dietary law. Required for halal product manufacturing and labeling.
Indicator of whether the consumed lot is certified kosher by a recognized kosher certification agency. Required for kosher product manufacturing and labeling.
The expiration or best-before date of the consumed lot. Used to enforce FEFO consumption rules and ensure materials are used before expiration.
reference: FDA Food Labeling and Shelf Life Requirements

description: The unique document number generated by the ERP system for this goods movement transaction. Used for audit trail and transaction traceability.
The fiscal year in which the material document was posted. Combined with material_document_number forms a unique key in the source system.
The user ID or username of the person who last modified this consumption record. Used for accountability and change tracking.
The date and time when this lot consumption record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit and data quality purposes.

The SAP movement type code that classifies the inventory transaction (e.g., 261 for goods issue to production order, 262 for reversal). Determines how the consumption affects inventory and financial accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Movement Type Configuration

description: Indicator of whether the consumed lot is certified organic according to USDA NOP or equivalent organic certification standards. Critical for organic product integrity and labeling compliance.
The date on which the consumption transaction was posted to the financial and inventory ledgers. May differ from consumption_timestamp for backdated postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Posting Date Logic

description: The material reservation document number that allocated this lot for consumption against the production order. Links consumption to the planned material requirement.
The original lot or batch number assigned by the supplier for the consumed material. Critical for traceability back to the raw material source and supplier quality investigations.
reference: FSMA Traceability and Supplier Documentation Requirements

description: The total cost of the consumed quantity, calculated as consumption_quantity * unit_cost. Represents the material cost component of the production order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Production Order Costing

description: The standard or actual cost per unit of measure for the consumed material at the time of consumption. Used for production cost calculation and COGS determination.
The unit in which the consumption quantity is measured (e.g., KG, LB, EA, L, GAL). Must align with the material master unit of measure.
The consumption variance expressed as a percentage of the BOM quantity. Calculated as (variance_quantity / bom_quantity) * 100.
The difference between actual consumption quantity and the BOM planned quantity. Positive values indicate over-consumption; negative values indicate under-consumption.
The storage location or warehouse from which the material lot was withdrawn for consumption. Links to the warehouse management system location master.
Unique identifier for the OEE event record. Primary key for the OEE event entity.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.equipment_master. Business justification: OEE events are tied to a specific equipment asset; linking to equipment_master gives full asset details and eliminates the loosely typed equipment_tag.
Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this OEE event occurred.
Reference to the production operator who was responsible for the work center when the event occurred.
Reference to the production order that was active when this OEE event occurred.
Reference to the production shift during which this OEE event occurred.
Reference to the product (SKU) that was being manufactured when this OEE event occurred.
Reference to the work center (production line or equipment) where this OEE event occurred.
The production batch or lot number that was being processed when the OEE event occurred, enabling full traceability per FIFO/FEFO requirements.
Timestamp when this OEE event record was first created in the MES system, representing when the event was initially captured.
Total duration of the OEE loss event in minutes, calculated as the difference between event_end_timestamp and event_start_timestamp.
Precise timestamp when the OEE loss event ended and normal production resumed, captured from the MES system.
Identifies the source system that captured and reported this OEE event (e.g., Aveva Wonderware MES, SCADA system, manual entry).
Precise timestamp when the OEE loss event began, captured from the MES system in real-time.
Current lifecycle status of the OEE event from occurrence through resolution. Tracks the event from initial detection through maintenance response to final closure.
Classification of the OEE loss event type. Determines which OEE component (availability, performance, quality) is impacted.
Technical fault code or alarm code captured from the equipment control system at the time of the event, used for detailed troubleshooting and maintenance analysis.
Indicates whether this OEE event triggered a GMP compliance review or documentation requirement. True if GMP review is required, False otherwise.

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Impacted Flag
description: Indicates whether this OEE event impacted a HACCP Critical Control Point, requiring food safety review and documentation. True if CCP was impacted, False otherwise.
Timestamp when this OEE event record was last updated, tracking changes to root cause, resolution actions, or status updates.
Indicates whether the loss event was planned (scheduled maintenance, changeover) or unplanned (breakdown, quality issue, material shortage).

Standardized reason code from MES that categorizes the root cause of the loss event (e.g., mechanical failure, electrical fault, material shortage, utility failure, operator error, quality hold, sanitation hold).
The work order number from the maintenance management system if a maintenance work order was raised in response to this event. Null if no work order was created.
Indicates whether a maintenance work order was created in response to this OEE event. True if a work order was raised, False otherwise.
Identifies which of the three OEE components (availability, performance, quality) is primarily impacted by this loss event.
Free-text notes entered by the production operator providing additional context, observations, or details about the OEE event.
Quantity of production units lost or not produced due to this OEE event, measured in the unit of measure of the product being manufactured.
Description of the corrective action taken to resolve the OEE event and restore normal production, entered by operator or maintenance personnel.
Operator-entered or maintenance technician-entered description of the root cause of the OEE loss event, captured for continuous improvement analysis.

Detailed classification of downtime events into categories such as mechanical failure, electrical fault, material shortage, utility failure, operator error, quality hold, or sanitation hold. Applicable only when event_type is downtime.
Unique identifier for the process parameter reading record.
Reference to the corrective action record initiated in response to this out-of-spec parameter reading, if applicable.
reference: Quality Management System, CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) Process

description: Reference to the production operator or technician who was responsible for the process at the time of reading, or who manually entered the value. Required for GMP audit trail.
Reference to the specific production equipment, vessel, or machine from which this parameter was measured.
reference: Equipment Master Data, Maintenance Management System

description: Reference to the production recipe or formulation being executed at the time of this parameter reading.
Reference to the production order during which this parameter was measured.
Reference to the finished good or intermediate product being manufactured when this parameter was captured, enabling product-specific process analysis.
Reference to the work center or production line where the parameter was captured.
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this parameter reading triggered an alarm or alert in the MES system due to exceeding control limits or deviation thresholds.
reference: MES Alarm Management, HACCP Monitoring Procedures

description: Manufacturing batch or lot number associated with this parameter reading, enabling full traceability per FIFO/FEFO and GMP requirements.
reference: GMP Batch Record Requirements, 21 CFR Part 11

description: Next scheduled calibration date for the sensor or instrument at the time of reading, in format yyyy-MM-dd. Ensures measurement validity per GMP and ISO 9001 requirements.
reference: ISO 9001 Calibration Requirements, GMP Equipment Qualification

description: HACCP plan reference number for this Critical Control Point, if applicable (e.g., 'CCP-1', 'CCP-2A'). Used for regulatory audit traceability.
reference: HACCP Plan Documentation, FDA FSMA Compliance

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this out-of-spec reading requires formal corrective action documentation per HACCP or quality management procedures.

System timestamp when this process parameter record was first created in the data platform, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Distinct from reading_timestamp which captures the actual measurement time.
Assessment of the reliability of this parameter reading: valid (normal operation), suspect (potential sensor issue), invalid (known bad data), or estimated (interpolated due to sensor failure).
Origin of the parameter reading: automated_sensor (captured directly from MES historian) or manual_entry (operator-entered value).
Calculated percentage deviation of the measured value from the target value, enabling quick assessment of process variation and capability.
Unit of measure for the parameter value (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit, PSI, bar, pH units, degrees Brix, liters/min, RPM, Nm, percent RH, CFU/ml, percent ABV).
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this parameter is designated as a HACCP Critical Control Point requiring enhanced monitoring and documentation per food safety regulations.
reference: HACCP Principles, FSMA Preventive Controls, Codex Alimentarius

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the measured value falls within the acceptable control limits (LCL to UCL). False indicates an out-of-spec event requiring investigation.
Minimum acceptable value for this parameter; readings below this threshold indicate an out-of-specification condition requiring corrective action per SPC and HACCP protocols.
reference: SPC (Statistical Process Control) Standards, HACCP Critical Limits

description: The actual numeric value recorded for this process parameter at the reading timestamp.
System timestamp when this process parameter record was last updated in the data platform, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records, Data Governance Standards

description: Free-text field for operator or quality technician comments regarding this parameter reading, including context for deviations, corrective actions taken, or special circumstances.

Classification of the parameter type: temperature, pressure, pH (acidity measure), Brix (sugar content), flow rate, speed, torque, humidity, CFU (Colony Forming Units) count, or ABV (Alcohol by Volume). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temperature|pressure|ph|brix|flow_rate|speed|torque|humidity|cfu_count|abv — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: HACCP Critical Control Point Categories, FDA Process Controls

description: Human-readable description of the process parameter being measured (e.g., 'Pasteurization Tank 3 Temperature', 'Filling Line 2 pH Probe').
Unique tag identifier from the MES historian system (Aveva/Wonderware) representing the specific sensor or control point.
reference: Aveva/Wonderware MES Historian Tag Naming Convention

description: Manufacturing facility or plant identifier where this parameter was measured, enabling multi-site process comparison and benchmarking.
reference: SAP Plant Master Data, ERP Organization Structure

description: Stage or phase of the production process during which this parameter was measured (e.g., 'mixing', 'pasteurization', 'cooling', 'filling', 'packaging'). Enables phase-specific process capability analysis.

Quality disposition of the batch or production run based on this parameter reading: approved (within spec), rejected (out of spec, product quarantined), under_review (pending quality review), or conditional (approved with restrictions).

description: Precise date and time when the parameter measurement was captured from the sensor or manually entered, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this parameter reading must be included in regulatory submissions or audit documentation per FDA, USDA, or other governing body requirements.

description: Laboratory or in-process sample identifier if this parameter reading was associated with a physical sample taken for testing or verification.
Unique identifier of the physical sensor or instrument that captured the measurement, enabling calibration traceability and maintenance tracking.

The intended or setpoint value for this parameter as defined in the production recipe or process specification.

description: Maximum acceptable value for this parameter; readings above this threshold indicate an out-of-specification condition requiring corrective action per SPC and HACCP protocols.
reference: SPC (Statistical Process Control) Standards, HACCP Critical Limits

Unique identifier for the work center. Primary key.

Reference to the financial cost center to which production costs and overhead for this work center are allocated.
Foreign key linking to sustainability.environmental_instrument. Business justification: Renewable Energy Certificates are allocated to specific equipment/work centers for compliance and reporting.
Reference to the manufacturing plant where this work center is located.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Work center is part of a production line; adding production_line_id links work_center to its line and provides needed connectivity.
FK to workforce.employee

business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Node ID
description: SCADA system node identifier for real-time monitoring and control of this work center's automation equipment.
Date when this work center became operational and available for production scheduling.
Date when this work center was decommissioned or taken out of service. Null for currently active work centers.

Indicates whether this work center is capable of processing products containing major food allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans). Critical for allergen control and production scheduling.

Comma-separated list of specific allergen types that this work center is approved to handle (e.g., milk, peanuts, tree nuts, soy). Empty if allergen_capable_flag is false. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: milk|eggs|fish|shellfish|tree_nuts|peanuts|wheat|soybeans|sesame|sulfites — promote to reference product]
Total available production hours per day for this work center based on shift pattern and planned maintenance windows.

Unit of measure for the rated capacity (e.g., cases per hour, bottles per minute, pounds per hour). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cases|bottles|cans|pounds|kilograms|liters|gallons|pallets — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Total time in minutes required to switch from one product or SKU to another, including teardown, cleaning, setup, and validation. Key metric for production efficiency and scheduling.

Indicates whether this work center requires automated clean-in-place procedures between production runs. Critical for liquid processing, dairy, and beverage lines.
Indicates whether this work center is approved for co-packing or toll manufacturing operations for third-party brands.
Timestamp when this work center record was first created in the system.
GMP hygiene classification level of the work center determining the sanitation, personnel, and environmental control requirements.

description: Indicates whether this work center is certified for halal food production by a recognized halal certification body.
Indicates whether this work center is certified for kosher food production by a recognized kosher certification agency.
Date of the most recent preventive maintenance or major service performed on this work center.
Timestamp when this work center record was last updated in the system.

Classification of the work center by its primary production process type. Determines the type of operations and products that can be manufactured on this line. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mixing|filling|packaging|pasteurization|fermentation|extrusion|co-packing|blending|bottling|canning — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Maximum production quantity that can be manufactured on this work center in a single run due to equipment, storage, or process constraints.

Date of the next planned preventive maintenance shutdown for this work center. Used for production scheduling and capacity planning.
Target OEE percentage for this work center representing the product of availability, performance, and quality rates. Industry benchmark for world-class manufacturing is 85%.
Indicates whether this work center is certified for organic food production under USDA NOP or equivalent organic standards.
Physical production area or zone within the plant where the work center is located (e.g., Mixing Room, Filling Hall, Packaging Wing, Cold Storage).
Maximum theoretical production capacity of the work center expressed in units per hour under optimal operating conditions. Used for production planning and scheduling.
Unit of measure for minimum and maximum run sizes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cases|bottles|cans|pounds|kilograms|liters|gallons|batches — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Standard time in minutes required to prepare the work center for a production run, including equipment configuration, material staging, and pre-operational checks.

description: Standard operating shift pattern for this work center determining its available production hours per day.
Standard time in minutes required to produce one unit or batch on this work center under normal operating conditions. Used for production scheduling and costing.
reference: SAP PP Routing and Work Center

description: Standard time in minutes required to clean, sanitize, and restore the work center after a production run. Critical for GMP compliance and allergen control.
High-level categorization of the work center by its functional role in the manufacturing process.
Business identifier code for the work center used in production planning and scheduling systems. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by manufacturing operations.
Detailed description of the work center including its purpose, capabilities, and operational characteristics.
Descriptive name of the work center for human identification and reporting purposes.
Current operational status of the work center indicating its availability for production scheduling and execution.
Unique identifier for the co-packing or toll manufacturing order. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the specific BOM record used for this co-packing order.
Reference to the third-party co-manufacturer (co-packer) supplier who will execute the production. Links to procurement supplier master.
Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Co‑packing orders occur at a plant; linking to plant centralizes location data and removes redundant facility name/location fields.
Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Co‑packing orders are often executed to supply promotional SKUs; linking enables Promo fulfillment tracking and cost allocation.
Reference to the master co-packing or toll manufacturing contract governing this order.
Reference to the purchase order record in the procurement system for this co-packing service.
Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Co‑packing operations are triggered by specific sales orders; linking enables order‑to‑batch traceability and accurate delivery scheduling.
Reference to the product master record for the SKU being co-packed.
The actual date when production was completed by the co-packer.
The actual quantity of finished goods units produced by the co-packer upon order completion.
Indicates whether allergen control procedures at the co-packer facility have been verified for this order. True if verified, False if not verified.
Version identifier of the BOM (Bill of Materials) supplied to the co-packer for production execution.
The batch or lot number assigned by the co-packer to the production run.
Business-facing unique order number for the co-packing arrangement, used for external communication with co-packer and internal tracking.
User ID or name of the person who created this co-packing order record.
Timestamp when this co-packing order record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the landed cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The calculated expiration date for the co-packed product batch based on production date and shelf life.

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether the co-packer facility is compliant with FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) requirements. True if compliant, False if non-compliant.

description: The specific GFSI-recognized certification scheme held by the co-packer (e.g., SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000, IFS).
Date of the most recent GMP audit conducted at the co-packer facility.
Status of the co-packer facility's GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) audit: current (audit valid), expired (audit needs renewal), pending (audit scheduled), not-audited (no audit on file).
The date when the co-packed finished goods were received into our inventory.
The goods receipt document number generated when the co-packed finished goods are received into inventory.

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) CCP Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether the co-packer has documented compliance with HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) critical control points for this production run. True if compliant, False if non-compliant or not verified.
Our internal batch or lot number assigned to the co-packed production for traceability and inventory management.

The total landed cost per unit for the co-packed product, including manufacturing cost, raw materials (if supplied by us), logistics, and any other charges. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this co-packing order record.
Timestamp when this co-packing order record was last updated.

Current lifecycle status of the co-packing order: planned (order created), in-production (manufacturing underway), received (goods received), quality-hold (pending quality clearance), closed (order completed), cancelled (order terminated).
The scheduled date when production is planned to start or be completed at the co-packer facility.
The planned quantity of finished goods units to be produced by the co-packer as per the order.
Identifier of the specific production line at the co-packer facility used for this order.
Description of the reason for quality hold if applicable (e.g., failed inspection, documentation incomplete, sensory deviation).
Indicates whether the co-packed goods are currently on quality hold pending inspection and release. True if on hold, False if released or not applicable.
Indicates who supplies the raw materials for production: us (we supply), copacker (co-packer supplies), or mixed (both parties supply components).
Code of the warehouse or distribution center where the co-packed goods are received.
The shelf life in days assigned to the co-packed product batch from production date.
The level of SQF certification held by the co-packer (Level 1: Food Safety Fundamentals, Level 2: Food Safety Plans, Level 3: Comprehensive Food Safety and Quality).

Status of the co-packer's SQF (Safe Quality Food) or GFSI (Global Food Safety Initiative) certification: certified (valid certification), expired (needs renewal), pending (application in progress), not-certified (no certification).

Unit of measure for the planned and actual quantities (e.g., cases, units, pallets, kg). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cases|units|pallets|kg|lbs|liters|gallons — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Unique identifier for the production BOM record. Primary key for the production BOM entity.
Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Links BOM to the plant where it is valid, supporting plant‑specific formulation control and compliance with local regulations.
The user ID of the quality or production manager who approved this BOM for production use.
Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Ensures each production BOM version is tied to the SKU it produces, critical for traceability in batch release and regulatory reporting.
Indicates whether this BOM contains any of the major allergens requiring declaration per FDA and FSMA regulations.
