description: Groups alternative BOMs for the same SKU (e.g., seasonal formulations, regional variants, equipment-specific recipes). Null if no alternatives exist.
The date when this BOM received final approval for production execution.
Indicates whether this BOM has passed all quality, regulatory, and operational reviews and is authorized for commercial production.
The standard batch size for which component quantities are defined. Typically expressed in production units (e.g., 1000 kg, 500 cases).
The unit of measure for the base quantity. Must align with the SKU's primary production UOM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|EA|CS|CASE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Reference to the master batch record template used for GMP documentation when executing this BOM.
Current lifecycle status of the BOM. Only active BOMs are available for production order creation.
The primary purpose of this BOM. Production BOMs are executable; costing BOMs are used for standard cost calculation; engineering BOMs are R&D prototypes.
Version identifier for this BOM configuration. Increments with each formulation change or process improvement.
The expected time in minutes required to changeover equipment from the previous product to this BOM. Critical for production scheduling optimization.
Indicates whether this BOM meets clean label criteria (no artificial ingredients, preservatives, or additives) for marketing and consumer transparency.
Indicates whether this BOM is executed under a co-packing or toll manufacturing arrangement with a third-party manufacturer (3PL).
Links to the standard cost estimate version used for COGS calculation based on this BOM's component costs.
The timestamp when this BOM record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether this BOM has been validated for GMP compliance including proper sequencing, critical control points, and documentation requirements.
reference: Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP), FDA 21 CFR Part 110

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Flag
description: Indicates whether this BOM includes HACCP critical control points requiring enhanced monitoring and documentation during production.
reference: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP), FDA FSMA

description: The method used to consume component inventory during production. FIFO (First In First Out) or FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard for food and beverage to ensure freshness and minimize waste.
The timestamp when this BOM record was last modified. Tracks all changes for audit and compliance purposes.
The date when this BOM was last reviewed for accuracy, regulatory compliance, and cost optimization. BOMs should be reviewed annually or upon ingredient changes.
Indicates whether full lot-level traceability is required for all components per FSMA traceability rules or customer requirements.
Reference to the R&D formulation record in the PLM system from which this production BOM was derived. Maintains traceability from innovation to manufacturing.
The specific production line or work center where this BOM is typically executed. Supports line-specific BOMs for equipment constraints.

description: The guaranteed shelf life in days for the finished product produced from this BOM, from production date to expiration.

The unique identifier for this BOM in the source system. Enables traceability back to the operational system of record.
The expected production cycle time in minutes to produce one base quantity batch. Used for capacity planning and OEE calculation.
reference: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE), SAP PP Routing

description: The required storage conditions for the finished product (e.g., ambient, refrigerated 2-8°C, frozen below -18°C). Critical for warehouse management and distribution planning.
The date from which this BOM version becomes effective for production scheduling and execution.
The date after which this BOM version is no longer valid for new production orders. Null indicates open-ended validity.
Expected yield percentage accounting for normal production losses, evaporation, and waste. Used for accurate material requirements planning (MRP).
Unique identifier for the production order. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand performance report requires linking each production order to its brand for tracking volume and cost per brand.
Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_footprint. Business justification: Regulatory GHG reporting requires linking each production order to its calculated carbon footprint for traceability.
Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Co‑packer supplier must be recorded for FDA/HACCP traceability and allergen control.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Manufacturing Cost Allocation Report to allocate production costs to the correct cost center; cost_center string is denormalized and replaced.
Foreign key linking to supply.demand_plan. Business justification: Production orders are created from demand plans; linking enables S&OP reconciliation and demand‑supply variance analysis.
Employee ID or badge number of the production operator or supervisor who posted the confirmation transaction.

Foreign key linking to research.formulation_version. Business justification: Manufacturing must reference the exact R&D formulation version approved for the SKU to satisfy compliance and quality audits.
Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Linking production_order to plant centralizes plant metadata and removes the redundant plant_code string.
Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Production order release requires verified product registration to ensure compliance with FDA/USDA regulations.

Foreign key linking to workforce.production_shift. Business justification: Required for assigning each production order to a specific shift, enabling shift‑based labor planning and OTIF performance reporting.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports Profitability Analysis per production order, linking manufacturing output to finance profit_center for reporting.

Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Production Planning uses Promotion Events to drive order quantities; linking enables the Production Planning Report to allocate capacity to specific promotions.
Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Production runs are executed under specific supplier contracts; linking enables contract compliance reporting.
Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: OTIF and cost tracking require linking each production order to the originating purchase order.
Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: HACCP plan execution is tracked per production order for compliance reporting and audit traceability.

Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Regulatory & cost reporting requires each production order to be linked to its originating R&D project for traceability and budgeting.
Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Order fulfillment process requires linking each production order to the originating sales order for traceability and scheduling.
Reference to the finished goods SKU or semi-finished intermediate being produced in this order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: A production order is executed on a specific work center; linking to work_center provides hierarchy and eliminates the free‑text production_line column.
Actual date and time when production execution was completed and final confirmation posted. Captured from MES or CO11N transaction.
Actual date and time when production execution began on the shop floor. Captured from MES or manual confirmation.
Standard or planned batch size for this production run. Represents the nominal production lot size for the SKU on the assigned line.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Batch Management

description: Version identifier of the BOM used for this production order. Ensures traceability of ingredient and component specifications used in production.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - BOM Master

description: SAP PP confirmation document number generated by CO11N transaction. Links to detailed confirmation records including time, quantity, and goods movements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Order Confirmation (CO11N)

description: Date and time when the production confirmation was posted in SAP PP via CO11N transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Confirmation Posting

description: Type of production confirmation: partial (interim confirmation during production run) or final (completion confirmation closing the order).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Confirmation Processing

description: Actual quantity confirmed as produced via CO11N confirmation transaction. Represents good output quantity posted to inventory.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Order Confirmation (CO11N)

description: Date and time when the production order record was first created in SAP S/4HANA PP system. Represents the beginning of the order lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Order Creation

business_glossary_term: First Expired First Out (FEFO) Lot Consumption Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether raw material lot consumption for this order followed FEFO inventory management rules (consuming lots with nearest expiration first). True if FEFO was enforced, False otherwise. Critical for perishable food and beverage ingredients.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Shelf Life Management

business_glossary_term: First In First Out (FIFO) Lot Consumption Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether raw material lot consumption for this order followed FIFO inventory management rules. True if FIFO was enforced, False otherwise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Inventory Management Strategy

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this production order was executed in full compliance with GMP requirements. True if all GMP critical control points were met and documented, False if any deviations occurred.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 110 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice

description: SAP MM material document number for the goods receipt posting (MIGO 101 movement type) that moved confirmed quantity into finished goods inventory.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Goods Movement

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Log Reference
description: Reference identifier to the HACCP CCP monitoring log for this production order. Links to detailed records of critical control point measurements (temperature, pH, time, etc.) taken during production.
Date and time when the production order record was last updated in the source system. Tracks the most recent change to order header data, status, or confirmations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Change Management

business_glossary_term: Manufacturing Execution System (MES) Work Order Reference
description: Work order identifier from Aveva/Wonderware MES system. Provides linkage between SAP PP production order and detailed MES execution records including real-time process data, equipment performance, and operator actions.
SAP PP production order number - the externally-known business identifier for this manufacturing execution record. Typically 10-12 alphanumeric characters.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Production Order Header

description: Current lifecycle status of the production order: created (order created but not released to shop floor), released (released for execution), in_progress (active production), confirmed (production completed and confirmed via CO11N), technically_complete (all confirmations and goods movements posted), closed (order financially settled and closed), cancelled (order cancelled before completion). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|in_progress|confirmed|technically_complete|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Order Status Management

description: Classification of the production order: standard (regular production run), rework (reprocessing of non-conforming product), trial (R&D or NPD pilot batch), co-pack (third-party manufacturing arrangement), toll (toll manufacturing arrangement where customer provides materials).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Order Type Configuration

description: Target quantity to be produced as specified in the production order header. Measured in base unit of measure for the SKU.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Order Header Quantity

description: Final disposition of reworked material: reprocessed (successfully reworked to original spec), downgraded (reworked to lower-grade product), scrapped (could not be salvaged), returned_to_vendor (raw material defect). Applicable only when order_type is rework.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module - Disposition Decision

description: Quantity of product requiring rework or reprocessing. This quantity is typically moved to a rework order for corrective processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Rework Management

description: Code identifying the reason for rework when order_type is rework. Examples: quality defect, packaging error, labeling error, contamination, off-spec product. Null for non-rework orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module - Defect Catalog

description: Version identifier of the production routing or recipe used for this order. Defines the sequence of operations and process parameters.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Routing Master

description: Planned completion date for production order execution as determined by production scheduling or SAP IBP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Production Scheduling

description: Planned start date for production order execution as determined by production scheduling or SAP IBP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Production Scheduling

description: Quantity of product scrapped or discarded during production due to quality defects, process failures, or other non-conformances. Measured in base unit of measure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Scrap Confirmation

description: Shift during which the production order was executed: day, evening, night, or weekend shift.

Name of the toll manufacturing partner when order_type is toll. In toll arrangements, the customer provides raw materials and the toll manufacturer provides processing services. Null for non-toll orders.
Base unit of measure for all quantities on this order (e.g., KG for kilograms, EA for each, LB for pounds, CS for cases). ISO standard unit codes.
Actual yield percentage calculated as (confirmed_quantity / planned_quantity) * 100. Measures production efficiency and material utilization.
Variance between planned and actual yield, calculated as (actual_yield - planned_yield) / planned_yield * 100. Positive values indicate over-yield, negative values indicate under-yield.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Variance Analysis

description: Unique identifier for the production schedule record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Needed for Production Planning report; schedules are built around specific customer orders, linking to account provides accurate capacity planning per customer.
Identifier for a production campaign (a series of related production runs for similar products to minimize changeovers). Used in campaign-based manufacturing strategies.
Reference to the production planner or planning team responsible for creating or maintaining this schedule entry.
Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Scheduling must verify product registration status to avoid producing non‑compliant SKUs.
Reference to the production order that this schedule entry is planning for. Links to the production order master data.

Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Production schedules are aligned with Promotion Events to ensure capacity meets promotional demand; required for the Promotion Capacity Planning report.
Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Schedules reference the approved product specification to ensure correct parameters and compliance during execution.
Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that is scheduled to be produced in this schedule entry.
Reference to the co-packing or toll manufacturing partner if this production is outsourced. Null if production is internal.
Reference to the work center (production line, machine, or manufacturing cell) where this production is scheduled to occur.
Flag indicating whether allergen-related line clearance and deep cleaning is required before this production run due to allergen matrix considerations.
Target batch size for this production run, expressed in the same UOM as planned quantity. Used for batch manufacturing and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance.
Planned utilization percentage of the work center's available capacity for this schedule entry. Used for capacity planning and bottleneck analysis.
Allocated time in minutes for changeover activities (cleaning, setup, line clearance) before this production run. Critical for sequencing optimization and minimizing downtime.
The date and time when this production schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Number of operators or crew members required to execute this production schedule entry. Used for labor capacity planning.
The date and time when the actual production execution for this schedule entry was completed and confirmed by the MES. Used for schedule vs. actual performance analysis.
The forecasted demand quantity that this schedule entry is intended to fulfill. Used for demand-driven planning and forecast accuracy tracking.
Flag indicating whether this schedule entry falls within the frozen planning horizon where changes are restricted to maintain schedule stability.

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Applicable
description: Flag indicating whether this production schedule entry involves HACCP Critical Control Points that require monitoring and documentation per food safety regulations.
The date and time when this production schedule record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The date and time when this schedule entry was last revised or replanned. Used for tracking schedule stability and replanning frequency.
Pre-assigned lot number for traceability purposes. Enables First In First Out (FIFO) and First Expired First Out (FEFO) inventory consumption tracking and lot genealogy.

Status indicating whether all required raw materials and ingredients are available for this scheduled production. Confirmed indicates full availability; partial indicates some materials available; pending indicates materials not yet checked; shortage indicates insufficient inventory.

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Target Date
description: The target delivery date for On Time In Full (OTIF) performance measurement. Used to track schedule adherence and customer service level.
The quantity of the SKU planned to be produced in this schedule entry, expressed in the base unit of measure for the SKU.
Unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., cases, kilograms, liters, pallets). Aligns with SKU base UOM.
The planning time bucket or horizon this schedule belongs to (e.g., weekly, daily, shift-level). Indicates the granularity of the planning cycle.

Business priority level assigned to this schedule entry. Critical indicates customer-committed or regulatory deadline; high indicates tight inventory; normal indicates standard planning; low indicates buffer or make-to-stock.
Flag indicating whether this production run requires in-process or post-production quality inspection per Quality Management (QM) protocols.

business_glossary_term: Released to Manufacturing Execution System (MES) Timestamp
description: The date and time when this schedule entry was released to the Manufacturing Execution System (MES) for execution. Marks the transition from planning to execution.
Business reason for the most recent replanning event (e.g., demand change, material shortage, equipment breakdown, customer request). Used for root cause analysis of schedule instability.
Target safety stock level that this production schedule entry is intended to maintain or replenish. Used for inventory optimization.
Free-text notes or comments from the planner regarding special considerations, constraints, or instructions for this schedule entry.

Origin system or method that generated this schedule entry. MRP indicates Material Requirements Planning; manual indicates planner override; IBP indicates Integrated Business Planning system; demand_sensing indicates real-time demand signal; optimization indicates algorithmic sequencing.
reference: SAP IBP / JDA Blue Yonder Planning Source

description: Current lifecycle status of the production schedule entry. Draft indicates preliminary planning; firmed indicates committed plan; released indicates sent to MES for execution; executed indicates production completed; cancelled indicates schedule entry voided.
Version identifier for the production schedule. Allows tracking of schedule revisions and replanning iterations (e.g., V1, V2, V2.1).
Total planned production duration in minutes, including run time but excluding changeover time. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) baseline calculations.
The planned date and time when production for this schedule entry is expected to be completed. Used for capacity planning and On Time In Full (OTIF) tracking.
The planned date and time when production for this schedule entry is expected to begin. Used for capacity planning and sequencing.
The order position of this schedule entry within the work center's production sequence for the planning period. Used for sequencing optimization and changeover minimization.
Special setup or configuration instructions for the work center to execute this schedule entry. May reference standard operating procedures (SOPs) or work instructions.
Identifier for the production shift during which this schedule entry is planned (e.g., Day, Night, Swing). Used for labor planning and shift performance analysis.
Unique identifier for the electronic batch record. Primary key for the batch record entity. Serves as the system-generated surrogate key for tracking manufacturing batch execution history.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211.188 - Batch Production and Control Records

description: Reference to the co-packer supplier who manufactured this batch. Populated only when co_packer_flag is True. Links to supplier master data for contract terms and quality agreements.
reference: Supplier Master Data, TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Reference to the approved formulation (recipe) used for this batch. Links to the bill of materials and ingredient specifications.

Foreign key linking to research.formulation_version. Business justification: Batch traceability to the specific R&D formulation version used is required for product recall, shelf‑life, and regulatory documentation.
Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Batch traceability to the inbound shipment is required for recall, quality investigation, and regulatory reporting.
Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Inspection results are recorded per batch; linking enables release decisions and traceability of quality inspections.
Reference to the primary production operator responsible for executing this batch. Used for accountability, training verification, and performance tracking.
Reference to the production order that authorized this batch manufacturing run. Links the batch record to the planning and scheduling system.

Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Batch records must be tied to the Promotion Event they fulfill for traceability, ROI analysis, and regulatory reporting of promotional batches.
Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Batch traceability to the source PO is required for recalls and cost allocation.
Reference to the finished goods SKU being manufactured in this batch. Identifies the specific product variant produced.
Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this batch was produced. Critical for multi-site traceability and regulatory compliance.
Reference to the quality assurance professional who reviewed and approved this batch for release. Required for batch disposition decision and regulatory compliance.

Foreign key linking to sustainability.waste_generation. Business justification: Waste generation reports are produced per batch; linking batch_record to waste_record enables accurate waste accounting.
Reference to the production line or work center where this batch was manufactured. Identifies the physical manufacturing resource.
The actual quantity of finished goods produced and approved from this batch. May differ from batch size due to process losses, quality rejections, or overproduction.

The allergen declaration for this batch as it appears on the product label. Lists all major allergens present (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame) and may-contain statements for cross-contact allergens. Critical for consumer safety and regulatory compliance.
reference: FDA FALCPA (Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act), FDA 21 CFR Part 101.22

description: Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or special instructions related to this batch. May include process notes, quality observations, equipment issues, or other relevant information not captured in structured fields.

The unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to a specific production batch. Also known as lot number. This is the externally-known business identifier used for traceability, recall management, and regulatory compliance. Must be unique across all production facilities and time periods.
The planned or target quantity of finished goods to be produced in this batch. Represents the theoretical yield based on the formulation and production order.

Current lifecycle status of the batch record. Tracks the batch through manufacturing execution, quality approval, and release stages. In-process indicates active manufacturing; pending QA indicates awaiting quality approval; released indicates approved for distribution; rejected indicates failed quality checks; quarantine indicates isolated pending investigation; on hold indicates regulatory or quality hold.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211.192 - Production Record Review, GMP Guidelines

description: The date until which the product is expected to retain optimal quality characteristics. May differ from expiry date. Used for quality assurance and consumer guidance. Common in food and beverage products where safety extends beyond optimal quality period.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969, FDA Guidance on Food Labeling

description: Indicates whether this batch was manufactured by a third-party co-packer (contract manufacturer) rather than in-house. True indicates co-packer production; False indicates internal manufacturing. Critical for supply chain visibility and quality accountability.
The date and time when this batch record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance.
reference: Data Governance Policy, Audit Trail Requirements

description: The total number of manufacturing deviations, exceptions, or non-conformances recorded during batch execution. Includes process parameter excursions, equipment failures, and procedural deviations. Zero indicates perfect execution. Used for quality trending and CAPA analysis.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211.192, ISO 9001:2015 - Nonconformity and Corrective Action

description: The date after which the product should not be consumed or used. Calculated based on manufacturing date plus shelf life days. Critical for FEFO inventory management and regulatory compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211.137, Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969

description: Indicates whether this batch carries a gluten-free claim on the label. True indicates gluten-free claim present; False indicates no claim. Must meet FDA gluten-free standard of less than 20 ppm gluten. Requires testing and validation.

Indicates whether this batch was manufactured in full compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice requirements. True indicates all GMP controls were met; False indicates GMP deviations occurred. Critical for regulatory audit trail.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, WHO GMP Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Sign-Off Status
description: Status of HACCP critical control point verification and approval for this batch. Indicates whether all critical control points were monitored and within acceptable limits. Pending indicates awaiting review; approved indicates all CCPs passed; rejected indicates CCP failure; not applicable for products not requiring HACCP.
Indicates whether this batch is certified halal per Islamic dietary law. True indicates halal certified; False indicates not certified. Requires halal certificate and compliance documentation.
reference: Halal Certification Standards (IFANCA, HFA, JAKIM, etc.)

description: Indicates whether this batch is certified kosher by a recognized kosher certification agency. True indicates kosher certified; False indicates not certified. Requires kosher certificate and supervision records.
reference: Kosher Certification Standards (OU, OK, Star-K, etc.)

description: The date and time when this batch record was last updated. Audit trail field for tracking changes and data lineage.
reference: Data Governance Policy, Audit Trail Requirements

description: The calendar date of batch production. This is the date printed on product labels and used for regulatory compliance. Typically the date manufacturing started or the date the batch was completed, per company policy.
The date and time when batch manufacturing execution was completed. Marks the completion of all processing steps and final packaging. Used for cycle time analysis and OEE calculation.
The date and time when batch manufacturing execution began. Marks the start of raw material dispensing and processing. Critical for shelf life calculation and FIFO/FEFO inventory management.

Indicates whether this batch is verified as non-genetically modified organism (non-GMO) by a third-party certification program. True indicates non-GMO verified; False indicates not verified or contains GMO ingredients.
reference: Non-GMO Project Verification, EU Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003

description: Indicates whether this batch is certified organic per USDA NOP or equivalent organic standards. True indicates certified organic; False indicates conventional. Requires organic certificate and audit trail.
reference: USDA NOP (National Organic Program) 7 CFR Part 205

description: Description of the primary and secondary packaging used for this batch. Includes package type, size, and material. Examples: 12oz PET bottle 24-pack, 500g stand-up pouch, 1L Tetra Pak carton. Links to packaging specification.
The batch number of the original batch from which reworked material was sourced. Populated only when rework_flag is True. Maintains traceability chain for reworked batches.
The date and time when the quality assurance reviewer approved this batch for release to distribution. Marks the transition from pending QA to released status.

Indicates whether this batch is under regulatory hold pending investigation or approval from regulatory authorities (FDA, USDA, etc.). True indicates hold in place; False indicates no regulatory restriction. Prevents distribution until cleared.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211.204 - Returned Drug Products, FSMA Preventive Controls

description: Free-text description of the reason for regulatory hold. Populated only when regulatory_hold_flag is True. Documents the specific regulatory concern, investigation, or pending approval.
Indicates whether this batch includes reworked material from a previous batch. True indicates rework present; False indicates all new material. Rework must be documented per GMP requirements.

The number of days from manufacturing date to expiry date. Defines the product stability period. Used to calculate expiry and best before dates. Derived from product specification and stability studies.

The thermal or non-thermal sterilization process applied to this batch. Pasteurization indicates low-temperature short-time treatment; UHT indicates ultra-high temperature; retort indicates in-container sterilization; aseptic indicates sterile processing and packaging; none indicates no sterilization (e.g., dry goods); other for alternative methods. Critical for food safety and shelf life.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 113 - Thermally Processed Low-Acid Foods, Codex Alimentarius

description: Indicates whether this batch was produced under a toll manufacturing arrangement where the company provides raw materials and the toll manufacturer provides processing services. True indicates toll manufacturing; False indicates standard production. Distinct from co-packing where the manufacturer may source materials.
The unit of measure for batch size and yield quantities. Common values include KG (kilograms), LB (pounds), L (liters), GAL (gallons), EA (each), CS (cases). Must align with SKU base unit of measure.
Unique identifier for the GMP compliance event record. Primary key.
Reference to the manufacturing plant where the GMP event occurred. Supports multi-site compliance tracking and regulatory reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211, GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

description: Reference to the employee who reported or documented the GMP event. Supports accountability and recognition of quality culture.
Reference to the production order during which the GMP event occurred. Links the event to the specific manufacturing run.
Reference to the product being manufactured when the GMP event occurred. Enables product-specific compliance tracking and risk assessment.
Reference to the employee or quality manager who approved closure of the GMP event. Null if event is not yet closed.
Reference to the work center or production line where the GMP event was observed. Enables analysis of event patterns by production area.
The batch or lot number of the product being manufactured when the GMP event occurred. Critical for traceability and potential recall scenarios.

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Reference Number
description: Reference number linking this GMP event to a formal CAPA record in the quality management system. Null if no CAPA was initiated.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211, ISO 9001, GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

description: The specific HACCP Critical Control Point number if this event relates to a CCP. Null if the event does not involve a CCP.
The date and time when the GMP event was formally closed after all corrective actions were verified as effective. Null if event is not yet closed.
System timestamp when this GMP event record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

business_glossary_term: Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) Event Date and Time
description: The date and time when the GMP event was observed or occurred on the production floor. This is the actual event timestamp, distinct from when it was recorded in the system.
Business identifier for the GMP event, typically formatted as GMP-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trail.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

description: Current lifecycle status of the GMP event. Open indicates newly reported, under investigation indicates active root cause analysis, pending approval indicates awaiting management review, closed indicates investigation complete and actions implemented, cancelled indicates event was determined invalid or duplicate.

Classification of the GMP event indicating the nature of the compliance issue or action. Deviation indicates departure from approved procedures, observation is a potential issue noted, non-conformance is a failure to meet requirements, corrective action addresses existing problems, preventive action prevents potential problems, and near miss is an event that could have resulted in a deviation.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211, GFSI Benchmarking Requirements, ISO 22000

description: Reference number or identifier linking this GMP event to an external audit finding from FDA, USDA, GFSI auditor, or customer audit. Null if not related to an external audit.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211, GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

business_glossary_term: Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Notification Required Flag
description: Indicates whether this GMP event requires specific notification to the FDA under food safety regulations. True if FDA notification is required, false otherwise.
Estimated or actual financial impact of the GMP event, including costs of rework, scrap, investigation, and potential regulatory penalties. Measured in the company's reporting currency.

Detailed narrative description of the GMP event, including what was observed, the circumstances, and any immediate context. This field captures the full story of the event for investigation and audit purposes.

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Flag
description: Indicates whether this GMP event occurred at or relates to a HACCP Critical Control Point. True if the event involves a CCP, false otherwise. Critical for food safety risk assessment.
Description of the immediate containment or corrective actions taken at the time the GMP event was discovered. Documents the initial response to prevent further impact.
Detailed assessment of the potential or actual impact of the GMP event on product quality, food safety, regulatory compliance, and business operations. Documents the risk evaluation performed.
The date and time when the investigation of the GMP event was completed and root cause determined. Null if investigation is still in progress.
The date and time when formal investigation of the GMP event began. Used to track investigation cycle time and compliance with internal SLAs.
System timestamp when this GMP event record was last updated in the data platform. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Reference to the event number of a previous related GMP event if this is a recurrence. Null if this is a first occurrence or not linked to a prior event.

Final disposition decision for the product batch affected by the GMP event. Released indicates product approved for distribution, rework indicates product requires reprocessing, regrade indicates product downgraded to alternate use, quarantine indicates product held pending further evaluation, destroy indicates product must be disposed of, under evaluation indicates disposition not yet determined.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211, ISO 22000, GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

description: The quantity of product affected by the GMP event, measured in the product's base unit of measure. Used to assess the scale of impact and potential financial exposure.

The unit of measure for the quantity affected field. Common units include kg (kilograms), lb (pounds), l (liters), gal (gallons), ea (each), case, or pallet. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|lb|l|gal|ea|case|pallet — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether this GMP event is a recurrence of a previously documented event with the same or similar root cause. True if recurrence, false if first occurrence. Used to identify systemic issues.
Indicates whether this GMP event meets the criteria for mandatory reporting to regulatory authorities such as FDA or USDA. True if reportable, false otherwise.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211, FSMA, USDA FSIS Regulations

description: The date and time when the GMP event was formally reported and recorded in the quality management system. May differ from event_datetime if there was a delay in reporting.

High-level classification of the root cause of the GMP event. Used for trend analysis and identification of systemic issues requiring corrective action. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|human_error|material_defect|process_deviation|environmental_condition|documentation_error|training_gap|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

business_glossary_term: Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) Event Severity Level
description: Severity classification of the GMP event. Critical indicates potential for serious harm to consumers or significant regulatory impact, major indicates substantial deviation from GMP requirements, and minor indicates low-impact deviation with minimal risk.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211, GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

business_glossary_term: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Notification Required Flag
description: Indicates whether this GMP event requires specific notification to the USDA, typically for meat, poultry, or egg product manufacturing. True if USDA notification is required, false otherwise.
Reference to the master document record in Veeva Vault QMS where the complete GMP event investigation file, attachments, and audit trail are stored.

business_glossary_term: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) CCP (Critical Control Point) Log ID
description: Unique identifier for the HACCP critical control point monitoring log entry. Primary key for the log record.
Identifier of the specific equipment or instrument used to perform the CCP monitoring (e.g., thermometer serial number, metal detector unit ID). Critical for calibration traceability.
Reference to the operator or quality technician who performed and verified the CCP monitoring. Establishes accountability and traceability for compliance audits.
Reference to the production order under which this CCP monitoring occurred. Links the CCP log to the manufacturing execution context.
Reference to the work center or production line where the CCP monitoring took place. Enables location-specific analysis of control point performance.
The production batch identifier for which this CCP monitoring was performed. Critical for lot traceability and recall management.
The unique code or identifier for the critical control point as defined in the HACCP plan (e.g., CCP-1, CCP-2-PASTEURIZE).
The descriptive name of the critical control point being monitored (e.g., Pasteurization Temperature, Metal Detection, pH Check, Brix Measurement, Allergen Verification).
Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context related to the CCP monitoring event. May include operator observations, environmental conditions, or other relevant details.
Boolean indicator of whether a corrective action is required based on the CCP monitoring result (True = corrective action needed, False = no action needed).
Detailed description of the corrective action(s) taken in response to a CCP deviation. Documents the specific steps taken to bring the process back into control and disposition of affected product.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117.150, GFSI Corrective Action Requirements

description: The minimum acceptable value for this CCP as defined in the HACCP plan. Values below this threshold indicate a deviation requiring corrective action.
reference: HACCP Plan Critical Limits, FDA FSMA

description: The maximum acceptable value for this CCP as defined in the HACCP plan. Values above this threshold indicate a deviation requiring corrective action.
reference: HACCP Plan Critical Limits, FDA FSMA

description: The origin of the CCP monitoring data (mes_automated = automatically captured from Aveva/Wonderware MES, manual_entry = operator-entered, laboratory_system = from LIMS, sensor_network = from IoT sensors).
reference: MES Integration Standards, GMP Data Integrity

description: Classification of the severity of the CCP deviation if one occurred (minor = slight deviation with low risk, major = significant deviation requiring investigation, critical = severe deviation with immediate food safety risk).
reference: GFSI Deviation Management, FDA Risk-Based Preventive Controls

description: The category of food safety hazard that this CCP is designed to control (biological, chemical, physical, or allergen).
reference: HACCP Principle 1 - Hazard Analysis, Codex Alimentarius

description: Boolean indicator of whether the measured value falls within the defined critical limits (True = within limits, False = out of specification).
The actual measured or observed value at the critical control point (e.g., temperature reading, pH level, Brix degree, metal detector sensitivity test result).
The required frequency of monitoring for this CCP as defined in the HACCP plan (e.g., continuous, every 15 minutes, every batch, hourly).
The method or procedure used to monitor this CCP (e.g., calibrated thermometer, pH meter, metal detector test, visual inspection, laboratory analysis).
The exact date and time when the CCP measurement or observation was recorded. Critical for establishing real-time compliance and traceability.

The final disposition decision for product affected by this CCP monitoring event (released = approved for distribution, rework = reprocessed to meet specifications, reprocess = returned to earlier production step, destroyed = disposed of, quarantine = held pending investigation).
The date and time when this CCP log record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data integrity and compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GMP Data Integrity

description: The date and time when this CCP log record was last modified. Critical for audit trail and data integrity compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GMP Data Integrity

description: Boolean indicator of whether this CCP deviation triggered a regulatory hold on the batch, preventing release until investigation and disposition are complete (True = hold triggered, False = no hold).
The date and time when the supervisor or quality manager completed their review of this CCP log entry.
Boolean indicator of whether this CCP log entry requires supervisor or quality manager review due to deviation or other exceptional circumstances (True = review required, False = no review needed).
The unit in which the measured value is expressed (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit, pH units, Brix degrees, ppm, CFU/g).
The date and time when the CCP monitoring record was verified and signed off by the responsible operator or quality personnel.
Primary key for goods_receipt
Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: Upon receipt of capital equipment, the goods receipt must reference the corresponding asset for warranty and compliance tracking.
Reference to the material master record for the received item. Identifies the raw material, ingredient, packaging component, or indirect material received.
Reference to the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the goods were physically received. Critical for inventory positioning and FEFO/FIFO assignment.
Reference to the specific line item on the purchase order for which goods are being received. Enables line-level receipt tracking.
Reference to the purchase order against which this goods receipt is recorded. Links to the purchase order that authorized the procurement.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Receiving clerk identity is required for traceability, quality checks, and labor cost allocation.
Reference to the specific storage location within the plant where the received goods are placed. Enables warehouse bin-level traceability.
Reference to the supplier who delivered the goods. Links to the supplier master data for vendor performance tracking and compliance.
Indicates whether the supplier-provided allergen declaration was received with the goods. Critical for allergen matrix management and food safety compliance.
The supplier-provided batch or lot number for the received material. Critical for lot traceability, FSMA compliance, recall management, and FEFO inventory rotation.
The date until which the received material maintains optimal quality characteristics. Used for inventory prioritization and customer service level management.
The carrier-issued bill of lading number for the shipment. Links goods receipt to transportation and logistics documentation for freight audit and claims.
The name of the transportation carrier who delivered the goods. Used for carrier performance tracking and freight cost allocation.
Indicates whether the supplier-provided certificate of analysis was received with the goods. Required for raw material release and TraceGains compliance documentation.
Indicates whether the received material met cold chain temperature requirements during transportation and receipt. Non-compliance triggers quality hold and supplier corrective action.
The date and time when this goods receipt record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data lineage and system reconciliation.

The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the goods receipt value is denominated. Required for multi-currency procurement and financial consolidation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|CAD|MXN|BRL|CNY|JPY|AUD — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The supplier-provided delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the shipment. Used for cross-reference with supplier documentation and ASN reconciliation.
The date after which the received material should not be used or sold. Critical for FEFO inventory rotation, quality hold decisions, and food safety compliance.

The date on which the goods were physically received at the plant or distribution center. This is the principal business event timestamp for the receipt transaction and triggers inventory valuation and FEFO/FIFO aging calculations.
The externally-known business identifier for this goods receipt transaction. Used for cross-system reconciliation, three-way match with invoice, and audit trails.
The current lifecycle status of the goods receipt transaction. Tracks the receipt through posting, quality inspection, approval, and final disposition workflows.
The precise date and time when the goods receipt transaction was posted in the system. Provides granular audit trail for receipt processing and system reconciliation.
The date on which the received material was manufactured or produced by the supplier. Used for shelf life calculation and FEFO inventory management.
The date and time when this goods receipt record was last modified in the system. Tracks changes for audit compliance and data quality monitoring.
The SAP movement type code that classifies the type of goods receipt transaction. Standard codes include 101 (GR for PO), 122 (return delivery), 161 (return from customer).
The quantity originally ordered on the purchase order line. Used to calculate receipt variance and trigger exception workflows for over-receipts or under-receipts.
The quality management inspection lot number assigned to this goods receipt for quality testing and approval. Links to quality inspection results and certificate of analysis.
Indicates whether the received material requires quality inspection before being released to unrestricted inventory. Triggers quality management workflows per GMP and HACCP requirements.

The difference between received quantity and ordered quantity. Positive values indicate over-receipt; negative values indicate under-receipt. Triggers procurement exception handling and supplier performance scoring.

The actual quantity of material received and accepted at the plant or distribution center. This is the confirmed receipt quantity used for inventory updates and three-way match with purchase order and invoice.
Free-text notes entered by the receiving clerk documenting any observations, damage, discrepancies, or special handling instructions for the received goods.
The remaining shelf life of the received material in days, calculated from goods receipt date to expiration date. Used for FEFO assignment and inventory aging analytics.
The total tax amount associated with this goods receipt. Used for three-way match with invoice and tax compliance reporting.
The tax jurisdiction code applied to this goods receipt for sales and use tax calculation. Used for tax compliance and invoice verification.
The temperature of the received material at the time of receipt, measured in degrees Celsius. Critical for cold chain compliance and temperature-sensitive ingredient quality verification.
The total monetary value of the goods receipt, calculated as received quantity multiplied by unit price. Used for inventory accounting and financial reconciliation.

The unit of measure in which the received quantity is expressed. Must align with the purchase order UOM and material master base UOM for accurate inventory accounting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|EA|CS|PAL|MT|OZ|ML — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The per-unit purchase price of the received material as recorded on the purchase order. Used for inventory valuation and three-way match with invoice.
The specific dock or unloading bay at the plant or distribution center where the goods were received. Supports warehouse operations and receiving efficiency analytics.
The inventory valuation method applied to this goods receipt for financial accounting. Determines how the received material cost is recorded in inventory and COGS.
Unique surrogate identifier for each purchase order line item in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the po_line data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module – Purchase Order Item (EKPO)

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each PO line may be charged to a specific cost center, supporting detailed cost allocation and variance analysis per line item in F&B cost management.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Links PO line items to GL accounts for precise posting of material costs, essential for financial reporting and audit trails.
Reference to the raw material pricing contract or framework agreement governing the terms of this purchase order line. Supports contract compliance and price variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Outline Agreement (EKKO/EKPO); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Purchasing Contracts

description: Foreign key reference to the parent purchase order header. Establishes the header/detail relationship for BOM-driven procurement and spend analytics. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: HEADER_REFERENCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module – Purchase Order Header (EKKO)

description: Reference to the material or ingredient master record being procured on this line. Links to the material master for BOM-driven procurement, allergen matrix, and raw material cost tracking (COGS). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: RESOURCE_REFERENCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Master (MARA/MARM); TraceGains Specification Management

description: Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Detailed material consumption analysis for R&D needs line‑level PO linkage to the rd_project to reconcile actual usage vs. plan.

Identifier linking this purchase order line to the approved raw material or ingredient specification document in TraceGains. Ensures that the procured material conforms to the approved specification for food safety and regulatory compliance.
reference: TraceGains Specification Management; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 – Supplier Verification; GFSI – Supplier Approval

description: Reference to the supplier (vendor) fulfilling this purchase order line. Used for supplier performance scorecards, approved vendor list validation, and spend analytics by supplier.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Vendor Master (LFA1); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Supplier Master

description: Determines how the procurement cost is assigned in financial accounting (e.g., to a cost center for indirect materials, to a production order for direct ingredients, to a project for capital expenditure). Critical for COGS and EBITDA reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-KNTTP (Account Assignment Category); SAP FI/CO Integration

description: Indicates whether the material being procured on this line contains or may contain one or more regulated allergens (e.g., peanuts, tree nuts, milk, eggs, wheat, soy, fish, shellfish). Triggers allergen matrix validation and segregated storage requirements.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); EU Regulation 1169/2011 – Food Information to Consumers; TraceGains Allergen Matrix

description: Indicates whether batch management is required for this material upon goods receipt, enabling lot traceability for food safety compliance (HACCP, FSMA). When true, a batch number must be assigned at goods receipt.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Master (MARA-XCHPF Batch Management); FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S – Traceability

description: The delivery date confirmed by the supplier via order acknowledgment or Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN). Used to calculate delivery variance against the requested date for OTIF performance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKET-EINDT (Confirmed Schedule Line); EDI 855 Purchase Order Acknowledgment; ASN Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the raw material or ingredient was produced or sourced. Required for country-of-origin labeling (COOL) compliance, import duty calculation, and supply chain risk assessment.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; USDA Agricultural Marketing Act – Country of Origin Labeling (COOL); FDA 21 CFR Part 101 – Food Labeling

description: Timestamp when this purchase order line record was first created in the source ERP system. Used for audit trail, procurement cycle time analysis, and data lineage tracking in the Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-AEDAT (Document Date); ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the monetary values on this purchase order line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and financial consolidation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM – EKKO-WAERS (Currency Key)

description: Indicates whether this purchase order line has been marked for deletion in the source ERP system. Soft-delete flag used to exclude cancelled or obsolete lines from active procurement reporting while retaining audit history.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-LOEKZ (Deletion Indicator); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Line Cancel Status

description: The date on which delivery of the material or service is requested at the receiving plant or distribution center. Drives production scheduling, inventory planning, and OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKET-EINDT (Delivery Date); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether a goods receipt is required for this purchase order line before invoice payment can be processed. Mandatory for direct materials (ingredients, packaging) to enforce three-way match; may be waived for certain service lines.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-WEPOS (Goods Receipt Indicator); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Receipt Required Flag

description: Cumulative quantity of the material actually received against this purchase order line, expressed in the order unit of measure. Used to calculate open quantity, delivery completeness, and three-way match for invoice verification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-WEMNG (Quantity of Goods Received); FDA FSMA – Receiving Records

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and supplier for this purchase order line (e.g., FOB, DDP, CIF). Impacts landed cost calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM – EKKO-INCO1 (Incoterms Part 1); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Freight Terms

description: Indicates whether an invoice is expected for this purchase order line. When false, the system may use evaluated receipt settlement (ERS/self-billing) for high-volume ingredient suppliers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-REPOS (Invoice Receipt Indicator); Oracle Cloud Financials – ERS (Evaluated Receipt Settlement)

description: Cumulative quantity invoiced by the supplier against this purchase order line. Used in three-way match (PO quantity vs. GR quantity vs. invoice quantity) for accounts payable processing and accrual management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-REMNG (Quantity Still to Be Invoiced); Oracle Cloud Financials – Invoice Matching

description: SAP item category code defining the procurement type for this line (e.g., standard stock material, subcontracting for toll manufacturing/co-packing, consignment, service). Determines goods receipt and invoice verification behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-PSTYP (Item Category); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Line Type

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this purchase order line in the source ERP system. Supports change detection for incremental data loads and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO Change Documents; ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: Sequential line item number within the parent purchase order, as assigned by the ERP system (e.g., 10, 20, 30 in SAP). Used for ordering and referencing individual line items within a PO. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_SEQUENCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-EBELP (Item Number of Purchasing Document)

description: Current processing status of the purchase order line item reflecting its fulfillment lifecycle. Drives procurement workflow, goods receipt matching, and accounts payable processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|partially_delivered|fully_delivered|invoiced|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO Delivery Completion Indicator; Oracle Cloud Procurement – PO Line Status

description: ERP-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the raw material, ingredient, or packaging material being procured. Aligns with the material master and Bill of Materials (BOM) for ingredient traceability and COGS tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-MATNR (Material Number); FDA 21 CFR Part 117 – Ingredient Traceability

description: Short descriptive name of the raw material, ingredient, or packaging item as defined in the material master. Supports procurement reporting, invoice matching, and supplier communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-TXZ01 (Short Text); TraceGains Specification Management

description: Classification code grouping materials by commodity or category (e.g., sweeteners, dairy ingredients, flexible packaging, corrugated). Used for spend analytics, category management, and sourcing strategy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-MATKL (Material Group); GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC)

description: Minimum Order Quantity as agreed with the supplier for this material. Used to validate that ordered_quantity meets supplier requirements and to optimize order quantities in demand planning and procurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Master MRP2 View (MARC-MINBE); TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Total net monetary value of this purchase order line, calculated as (ordered_quantity / price_unit) × unit_price. Represents the committed spend for this line item and feeds into COGS and procurement spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-NETWR (Net Order Value); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Line Amount

description: Unit of measure in which the ordered quantity is expressed (e.g., KG, LB, L, EA, CS, MT). Must align with the material master order unit for accurate goods receipt and invoice verification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-MEINS (Purchase Order Unit of Measure); ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Quantity of the material or service ordered on this purchase order line, expressed in the order unit of measure. Compared against Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) thresholds and used for demand planning reconciliation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_QUANTITY.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-MENGE (PO Quantity); FDA FSMA – Traceability Lot Quantity

description: Indicates whether the material procured on this line is certified organic, requiring supplier certification documentation and chain-of-custody compliance. Supports clean label and organic product formulation requirements.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; EU Regulation 2018/848 – Organic Production; TraceGains Document Management

description: Maximum percentage by which the supplier may over-deliver against the ordered quantity without triggering a purchase order amendment. Common in bulk ingredient procurement where exact quantities are difficult to control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-UEBTO (Over-Delivery Tolerance); Codex Alimentarius – Commodity Trade Tolerances

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility, distribution center, or co-packing location where the material is to be delivered and consumed. Links procurement to production and inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-WERKS (Plant); GMP 21 CFR Part 110 – Manufacturing Facility

description: The quantity base for which the unit price is quoted (e.g., price per 100 KG, price per 1000 units). Required for accurate net value calculation when pricing is expressed per multiple units, as is common in bulk ingredient procurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-PEINH (Price Unit); Codex Alimentarius – Commodity Pricing Standards

description: Code identifying the buyer or purchasing group responsible for managing this purchase order line. Used for workload distribution, buyer performance reporting, and escalation routing.
Code identifying the purchasing organization responsible for this purchase order line. Determines the negotiating entity, contract scope, and spend reporting hierarchy in the ERP system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKKO-EKORG (Purchasing Organization); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Procurement BU

description: Indicates whether incoming quality inspection is required for this material upon goods receipt, per HACCP, GMP, or SQF requirements. When true, the material is placed in quality inspection stock until released by the QM team.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM – Inspection Lot (QMEL); FDA 21 CFR Part 117 – Preventive Controls; GFSI – SQF Code; ISO 22000

description: Minimum remaining shelf life in days required at the time of goods receipt for this raw material or ingredient. Enforces FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management and prevents use of near-expiry ingredients in production.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Master MRP5 View (MARA-MHDRZ Minimum Remaining Shelf Life); FDA 21 CFR Part 117 – Shelf Life

description: Code identifying the specific storage location within the plant where the received material will be placed (e.g., raw material warehouse, cold storage, dry goods). Supports FIFO/FEFO inventory management and WMS integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-LGORT (Storage Location); WMS Integration Standards

description: Maximum percentage by which the supplier may under-deliver against the ordered quantity and still have the line considered fully delivered. Prevents unnecessary open PO lines for minor quantity shortfalls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-UNTTO (Under-Delivery Tolerance); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Receiving Tolerance

description: Agreed net price per unit of measure for this purchase order line as negotiated with the supplier or derived from a pricing contract. Core input for COGS calculation and raw material cost tracking. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – EKPO-NETPR (Net Price); Oracle Cloud Procurement – Unit Price

description: Unique identifier for the purchase contract. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Contract ownership is assigned to a specific employee for responsibility tracking and reporting.

Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_initiative. Business justification: Procurement contracts are linked to internal sustainability initiatives they fund (e.g., carbon‑reduction projects).

Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Contract management tracks which product registrations are covered by each purchase contract to ensure legal market eligibility.
Reference to the supplier party with whom this contract is established.
Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Contracts embed ESG performance targets that suppliers must meet, tracked per contract.

Specific allergen control and handling requirements stipulated in the contract to ensure compliance with allergen labeling and cross-contamination prevention (e.g., dedicated lines, allergen-free facility).
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA)

description: Indicates whether the contract automatically renews for an additional term unless either party provides termination notice.
URL or file path to the signed contract document stored in the document management system.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the purchase contract, used in procurement documents and supplier communications.

Current lifecycle state of the purchase contract. Active contracts are in force; suspended contracts are temporarily paused; expired contracts have passed their end date; terminated contracts were ended early.

Classification of the procurement contract structure. Value contracts specify total value with flexible quantities; quantity contracts commit to specific volumes; scheduling agreements include delivery schedules; framework agreements establish terms for multiple purchase orders.
Timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract.

Type of delivery arrangement under this contract. Scheduled deliveries follow a fixed calendar; just-in-time aligns with production schedules; vendor-managed inventory allows supplier to manage stock levels.
Date when the contract expires or terminates. Nullable for open-ended agreements.
Date when the contract terms become binding and procurement can begin under this agreement.
Indicates whether this contract includes exclusivity terms requiring the buyer to source specified materials exclusively from this supplier, or vice versa.
Food safety certifications that the supplier must maintain to fulfill this contract (e.g., SQF Level 2, FSSC 22000, BRC, GFSI-recognized standard).
Text describing the force majeure provisions that excuse performance due to unforeseeable circumstances (natural disasters, pandemics, war, government actions).

International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller for delivery. Examples: FOB (Free On Board), CIF (Cost Insurance Freight), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Named place or port specified in the Incoterms clause where risk and cost transfer occurs (e.g., Port of Los Angeles, Chicago DC).
Timestamp when this contract record was last updated.
Standard number of days from purchase order placement to delivery under this contract. Critical for production planning and inventory management.

High-level classification of the materials or services covered by this contract. Raw materials and ingredients are used in production; packaging materials are for finished goods; indirect materials and MRO (Maintenance, Repair, Operations) support operations.
Minimum quantity that must be ordered per purchase order or delivery under this contract. Critical for production planning and supplier efficiency.
Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity (e.g., KG, LB, EA, CS, MT, GAL).
Standard payment terms code defining the payment schedule and conditions (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30 for early payment discounts).

Method by which contract prices may be adjusted over time. Index-linked ties to commodity indices; cost-plus adjusts based on supplier cost changes; fixed prices remain constant; periodic reviews allow renegotiation.
Name of the commodity or inflation index used for price escalation (e.g., USDA Dairy Index, CPI, PPI, CME Corn Futures). Applicable when price_adjustment_mechanism is index_linked.
Date until which the pricing conditions remain valid. After this date, prices may be renegotiated or adjusted per escalation clauses.
Date from which the pricing conditions in this contract become effective.
Code identifying the buyer or procurement team responsible for managing this contract and related purchase orders.
Code or name of the purchasing organization or business unit responsible for this contract within the enterprise.
Reference to the quality specification document or standard that materials supplied under this contract must meet (e.g., TraceGains spec ID, internal QA standard).
Indicates whether this contract includes volume-based rebate or discount tiers that provide retroactive price reductions when volume thresholds are met.
Percentage rebate applied when volume commitments or spend thresholds are achieved. May be tiered based on volume levels.
Number of days prior to contract expiration that termination notice must be provided to prevent automatic renewal.
Detailed spend classification for analytics and category management (e.g., Dairy Ingredients, Flexible Packaging, Transportation Services).
Number of days advance notice required for early termination of the contract by either party.

Maximum monetary value committed under this contract across all line items and purchase orders. For value contracts, this is the spending limit; for quantity contracts, this is the estimated total value.
Total quantity committed to be purchased over the contract period. Used for volume-based pricing tiers and rebate calculations.
Unit of measure for the volume commitment quantity.
Unique surrogate identifier for the purchase order record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — canonical minimum categories enforced.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Module; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Direct customer‑specific purchase orders tie spend to the originating account for order‑to‑cash reconciliation and account‑level reporting.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: CAPITAL EQUIPMENT PROCUREMENT process requires linking each purchase order to the asset created, enabling asset registration and cost tracking.

Foreign key linking to customer.bill_to_location. Business justification: Invoice generation requires the billing address; linking PO to the customer's bill‑to location ensures accurate invoicing and tax handling.

Foreign key linking to procurement.category_strategy. Business justification: Each purchase order is governed by a category strategy; linking provides strategic alignment and removes the redundant free‑text procurement_category column.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating PO expenses to the appropriate cost center in financial reporting and budgeting, as the PO expense is posted to a cost center per standard F&B accounting practice.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PO creation audit trail records which employee entered the order for compliance and cost control.
Foreign key linking to regulatory.fsma_record. Business justification: Order processing records the associated FSMA compliance record for the supplier’s facility to support food safety audits.

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Needed to map each purchase order to the GL account for expense posting, enabling accurate GL trial balance and compliance with financial statements.
Reference to the receiving plant or distribution center (DC) where the ordered goods or services are to be delivered. Maps to SAP EKPO-WERKS plant code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Master; Oracle Cloud Inventory Organization

description: Reference to the underlying procurement contract or blanket purchase agreement (outline agreement) against which this PO is released. Nullable for spot/one-time purchases. Maps to SAP EKKO-KONNR contract number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement (EKKO); Oracle Cloud Procurement Contracts

description: Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: R&D project spend tracking report requires each PO to be linked to the funding rd_project for budgeting and cost allocation.
Reference to the supplier (vendor) master record from whom goods or services are being procured. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction header. Maps to SAP EKKO-LIFNR vendor number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master (LFA1); Oracle Cloud Supplier Master

description: The date on which goods were physically received at the destination plant or DC, as recorded in the goods receipt (GR) posting. Used for OTIF calculation and supplier performance scorecarding.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt (MIGO); Oracle Cloud Receiving

description: The date on which the purchase order received final approval and was authorized for transmission to the supplier. Used for cycle time analysis and procurement governance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Release Strategy; SOX Procurement Controls

description: Current status of the purchase order within the approval workflow. 'pending' = awaiting approver action; 'approved' = all required approvals obtained; 'rejected' = denied by an approver; 'escalated' = routed to higher authority due to value threshold or timeout. Supports SOX compliance and procurement governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Release Strategy; Oracle Cloud Approval Workflow; SOX Internal Controls

description: User ID or name of the final approver who authorized the purchase order. Used for audit trail, SOX compliance, and procurement governance. Maps to SAP workflow approval history.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Workflow; SOX Section 302/404; Oracle Cloud Approval History

description: Maximum cumulative value authorized for release against a blanket purchase order or framework agreement. Applicable only when po_type = 'blanket_release'. Used for spend control and contract compliance monitoring. Maps to SAP EKKO-KTWRT.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement EKKO-KTWRT; Oracle Cloud Blanket PO

description: SAP or Oracle company code representing the legal entity issuing the purchase order. Determines the accounting ledger, currency, and fiscal year variant for financial postings. Maps to SAP EKKO-BUKRS.
