reference: SAP S/4HANA FI EKKO-BUKRS; Oracle Cloud Financials Legal Entity; GAAP/IFRS

description: The delivery date confirmed by the supplier in their order acknowledgment. May differ from the requested delivery date. Used to calculate delivery variance and OTIF performance. Maps to SAP EKPO-EINDT after supplier confirmation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Order Acknowledgment; EDI X12 855

description: Timestamp when the purchase order record was first created in the source ERP system. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category. Maps to SAP EKKO-AEDAT / creation time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO; Oracle Cloud Procurement; ISO 8601

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the purchase order is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD). Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category. Maps to SAP EKKO-WAERS.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-WAERS

description: Full delivery address of the destination plant or DC where goods are to be received. Includes street, city, state/province, postal code, and country. Used for logistics coordination and ASN matching.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Delivery Address; Oracle Cloud Receiving Location

description: Foreign currency exchange rate applied at the time of PO creation to convert the PO currency to the company code local currency. Used for financial reporting and spend analytics in local currency. Maps to SAP EKKO-WKURS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Valuation; IFRS IAS 21

description: Flag indicating whether this purchase order or its associated materials are currently under a food safety hold pending quality inspection, allergen verification, or HACCP review. When True, goods receipt and production use are blocked until cleared by QA. Supports FSMA and HACCP compliance.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; HACCP; GFSI; SAP S/4HANA QM Quality Hold

description: Flag indicating whether a goods receipt (GR) is required before invoice verification for this purchase order. When True, the three-way match (PO-GR-Invoice) is enforced. When False, two-way match (PO-Invoice) applies. Maps to SAP EKPO-WEPOS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKPO-WEPOS; Three-Way Match Control; SOX Procurement Controls

description: Flag indicating whether any item on this purchase order is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, labeling, or transport documentation under DOT, IATA, or IMDG regulations. Relevant for chemical ingredients, cleaning agents, and certain food additives.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Hazardous Materials; IATA DGR; FDA 21 CFR; EPA

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the transfer of risk, cost responsibility, and delivery obligations between buyer and supplier. Critical for import/export compliance, freight cost allocation, and insurance. Maps to SAP EKKO-INCO1. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-INCO1; Codex Alimentarius Commission

description: The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., 'Port of Los Angeles' for FOB, 'Buyer Warehouse Chicago' for DDP). Required to fully define the delivery obligation under Incoterms 2020. Maps to SAP EKKO-INCO2.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-INCO2

description: Flag indicating whether invoice-based verification (LIV - Logistics Invoice Verification) is required for this PO. When False, evaluated receipt settlement (ERS) may apply. Maps to SAP EKPO-REPOS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKPO-REPOS; Oracle Cloud Payables

description: Timestamp when the purchase order record was last modified in the source ERP system. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-AEDAT; Oracle Cloud Procurement; ISO 8601

description: Commodity or material group classification code for the primary category of goods or services on this PO (e.g., raw ingredients, packaging materials, indirect MRO, co-packing services). Used for spend analytics by category and supplier segmentation. Maps to SAP EKPO-MATKL.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKPO-MATKL; UNSPSC Commodity Classification

description: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) stipulated by the supplier for this purchase order, expressed in the base unit of measure. Used in procurement planning, order optimization, and supplier negotiation. Maps to SAP EKPO-MINBM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKPO; Food and Beverage Procurement MOQ Standard

description: Total net value of the purchase order after discounts but before tax, in the PO currency. This is the principal financial commitment amount used in spend analytics, budget tracking, and COGS reporting. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET net total component. Maps to SAP EKKO-NETWR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-NETWR; Oracle Cloud Procurement; GAAP/IFRS

description: The calendar date on which the purchase order was formally issued and transmitted to the supplier. This is the principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for the procurement transaction. Maps to SAP EKKO-BEDAT.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-BEDAT; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Code identifying the agreed payment terms between the buying organization and the supplier (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Drives accounts payable scheduling and cash flow planning. Maps to SAP EKKO-ZTERM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/MM EKKO-ZTERM; Oracle Cloud Payables; GAAP

description: Total gross value of the purchase order before discounts, taxes, and freight charges, expressed in the PO currency. Represents the sum of all line-level extended prices. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-NETWR; Oracle Cloud Procurement; GAAP/IFRS Procurement Accounting

description: Externally-known alphanumeric purchase order number assigned by the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA or Oracle Cloud Procurement) and communicated to the supplier on the PO document. This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER used in supplier correspondence, EDI transactions, and ASN matching. Maps to SAP EKKO-EBELN.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO; Oracle Cloud Procurement; EDI X12 850

description: Current lifecycle status of the purchase order. 'draft' = created but not submitted; 'pending_approval' = in approval workflow; 'approved' = approved and transmitted to supplier; 'open' = active with outstanding delivery; 'partially_received' = goods receipt posted for some lines; 'closed' = fully received and invoiced; 'cancelled' = voided before fulfillment. LIFECYCLE_STATUS canonical category. Maps to SAP EKKO processing status. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|open|partially_received|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing; Oracle Cloud Procurement PO Lifecycle

description: Classification of the purchase order by procurement strategy. 'standard' = one-time discrete PO; 'blanket_release' = release against a blanket/framework agreement; 'subcontracting' = toll manufacturing or co-packing arrangement where components are supplied to the vendor; 'service' = service entry sheet-based PO for indirect services; 'consignment' = vendor-managed inventory replenishment. Maps to SAP EKKO-BSART document type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-BSART; Oracle Cloud Procurement PO Type

description: Code identifying the buyer or purchasing group (commodity team) within the purchasing organization responsible for this PO. Used for spend analytics by buyer, workload management, and supplier relationship ownership. Maps to SAP EKKO-EKGRP.

Code identifying the SAP purchasing organization responsible for negotiating and issuing this purchase order. Determines the procurement authority, pricing conditions, and vendor master assignments. Maps to SAP EKKO-EKORG.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-EKORG; Oracle Cloud Procurement Business Unit

description: The date by which the buying organization requests delivery of the ordered goods or services at the destination plant or DC. Used for supply planning, OTIF measurement, and SLA tracking. Maps to SAP EKPO-EINDT.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKPO-EINDT; OTIF Performance Standard

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this purchase order was sourced. 'SAP_S4HANA' = SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing module; 'ORACLE_CLOUD' = Oracle Cloud Procurement; 'MANUAL' = manually entered in the lakehouse. Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle Cloud Procurement; Data Governance Best Practices

description: The native primary key or document number of the purchase order in the originating source system (e.g., SAP EKKO-EBELN or Oracle PO header ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracing, and support ticket resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKKO-EBELN; Oracle Cloud Procurement PO Header ID

description: Total tax amount applicable to the purchase order (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax) in the PO currency. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component. Maps to SAP EKKO tax conditions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Tax Conditions; IFRS; FDA/USDA Procurement Compliance

description: Total quantity of goods or services ordered across all lines of this purchase order, expressed in the base unit of measure (uom_code). Used for inventory planning, receiving, and OTIF measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKPO-MENGE; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Cumulative quantity of goods received against this purchase order as of the latest goods receipt posting, expressed in the base unit of measure. Used to calculate open PO quantity, OTIF fill rate, and goods receipt variance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt (MIGO); Oracle Cloud Receiving

description: Compliance status of the supplier for this purchase order as tracked in TraceGains, the supplier compliance and document management platform. 'compliant' = all documents valid; 'non_compliant' = missing or expired documents; 'pending_review' = documents submitted but under review; 'not_required' = compliance check not applicable.
reference: TraceGains Supplier Compliance; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; GFSI; SQF; HACCP

description: Base unit of measure code for the purchase order quantity (e.g., KG, LB, L, EA, CS, PAL). Aligns with SAP base UOM and is used for inventory valuation, recipe/BOM costing, and nutritional compliance calculations. Maps to SAP EKPO-MEINS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EKPO-MEINS; GS1 Unit of Measure Standards

Date when the purchase requisition was converted to a purchase order.

Full delivery address where the materials or services should be delivered, if different from the plant default address.

General ledger account number to which the procurement expense will be posted in the financial system.

Date when the purchase requisition was created or submitted by the requestor.
Business identifier for the purchase requisition, externally visible and used in procurement workflows. Typically 10-character alphanumeric code from SAP MM.

Current lifecycle status of the purchase requisition in the approval and conversion workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|rejected|partially_converted|fully_converted|cancelled|on_hold — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the purchase requisition based on procurement scenario: standard purchase, subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer between plants, service procurement, or third-party direct delivery.
System of record from which the purchase requisition originated (SAP S/4HANA MM, Oracle Cloud Procurement, or manual entry).
Additional instructions or notes provided by the requestor regarding handling, delivery, quality requirements, or other special considerations.
Code identifying the storage location or warehouse within the plant where the material will be received and stored.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Location Master

description: Unit of measure for the requested quantity (e.g., KG for kilograms, LB for pounds, EA for each, L for liters).

Unique surrogate identifier for each procurement spend transaction record at the invoice/purchase order (PO) line level in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the spend_transaction data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; Oracle Cloud Financials

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Financial spend reports allocate procurement costs to the customer account that generated the demand, supporting profitability analysis.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Spend transactions must reference the cost center to which the spend is charged, enabling cost center budgeting and performance reporting.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Associates each spend transaction with the GL account for proper ledger posting and regulatory financial reporting.

Reference to the material or item master record for the raw material, ingredient, packaging, indirect material, or service procured in this transaction. Supports material-level spend analytics and Bill of Materials (BOM) cost analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Oracle Cloud Item Master

description: Reference to the procurement contract or scheduling agreement under which this spend transaction was executed. Enables contract compliance tracking, contracted vs. off-contract spend analysis, and savings realization measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Contract (ME31K); Oracle Cloud Procurement Contracts

description: Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Financial spend analysis by rd_project needs each transaction linked to the project to calculate true R&D cost of goods.

Reference to the supplier (vendor) master record who fulfilled this procurement transaction. Links to the approved vendor list and supplier performance scorecard for spend consolidation and supplier analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master; Oracle Cloud Supplier Master; TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Negotiated contract price per unit of measure as specified in the procurement contract or scheduling agreement. Used to measure contract compliance and calculate savings vs. contract price.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement; Oracle Cloud Procurement Contracts

description: Timestamp when this spend transaction record was first created or loaded into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and incremental load tracking.

Fiscal accounting period (1–16 in SAP, typically 1–12 for regular periods plus special periods) within the fiscal year to which this spend transaction is attributed. Used for period-over-period spend analysis and monthly close reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Period; Oracle Cloud Financials Accounting Period

description: Four-digit fiscal year to which this spend transaction is attributed based on the posting date and the company's fiscal calendar. Used for year-over-year spend trending, budget vs. actual analysis, and annual savings tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year; Oracle Cloud Financials; GAAP/IFRS

description: Date on which the goods or services were physically received and confirmed in the ERP system (SAP MIGO goods receipt or Oracle receiving transaction). Used for three-way match validation, FIFO/FEFO inventory management, and supplier on-time delivery performance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt; Oracle Cloud Receiving; FDA FSMA

description: Food Beverage internal commodity category code from the company's proprietary procurement taxonomy (e.g., RAW-ING-DAIRY, PKG-FLEX-FILM, IND-MRO). Used alongside UNSPSC for category management, sourcing strategy, and spend cube construction.
reference: Internal Procurement Taxonomy; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group

description: Date on the supplier invoice document as issued by the vendor. Used for payment terms calculation, aging analysis, and fiscal period assignment. Represents the principal business event date for this spend transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; Oracle Cloud Payables; GAAP/IFRS

description: Line item number within the supplier invoice document corresponding to this spend transaction. Supports granular invoice-level spend reconciliation and dispute resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; Oracle Cloud Financials

description: Supplier-issued invoice document number associated with this spend transaction as recorded in SAP FI or Oracle Cloud Financials. Used for three-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice) and accounts payable reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; Oracle Cloud Financials; FDA FSMA traceability

description: Quantity of goods or services invoiced by the supplier on this transaction line. Used for unit price validation, purchase price variance (PPV) calculation, and volume-based spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this spend transaction was executed under an approved procurement contract (True) or represents off-contract/maverick spend (False). Key metric for contract compliance rate and spend under management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle Cloud Procurement; Internal Procurement Policy

description: Short descriptive name of the procured material, ingredient, packaging component, or service as defined in the ERP material master. Supports spend reporting without requiring a join to the material master.

SAP material group or Oracle category code grouping materials by procurement category (e.g., raw ingredients, packaging, MRO, services). Used for spend aggregation, sourcing strategy, and supplier consolidation analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group; Oracle Cloud Procurement Categories

description: ERP-assigned alphanumeric material or item number (SAP material number or Oracle item number) identifying the procured good or service. Used for cross-system reconciliation and spend cube construction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Date by which payment must be made to the supplier to comply with agreed payment terms and avoid late payment penalties. Derived from invoice date and payment terms; stored for accounts payable aging and cash flow management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; Oracle Cloud Payables; EU Late Payment Directive

description: ERP payment terms code (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30) defining the due date and any early payment discount conditions for this invoice. Used for cash flow forecasting, dynamic discounting, and working capital optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms; Oracle Cloud Payables; GAAP/IFRS

description: SAP plant or Oracle organization code identifying the manufacturing facility, distribution center (DC), or business unit that received the goods or services and is accountable for the spend. Key dimension for plant-level spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Plant Master; Oracle Cloud Organization

description: Sequential line item number within the purchase order document identifying the specific goods or services line that generated this spend transaction. Enables granular PO line-level spend analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Externally-known purchase order document number from the source ERP system (SAP S/4HANA MM or Oracle Cloud Procurement) that authorizes the procurement spend. Used to trace spend back to the originating PO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle Cloud Procurement; ISO 20022

description: Date on which the spend transaction was posted to the general ledger in the ERP system. Determines the fiscal period and accounting period to which the spend is attributed. May differ from invoice date due to accruals or late processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Date; Oracle Cloud Financials; GAAP/IFRS

description: Purchase Price Variance (PPV) amount calculated as the difference between the actual invoice price and the standard cost, multiplied by the invoiced quantity. Positive PPV indicates overspend vs. standard; negative indicates savings. Key metric for procurement performance and COGS management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO PPV; Oracle Cloud Cost Variance; GAAP/IFRS; CIMA

description: SAP purchasing group code identifying the buyer or procurement team responsible for this transaction. Used for buyer-level spend analytics, workload management, and procurement performance reporting.

SAP purchasing organization or Oracle procurement business unit code responsible for negotiating and managing the procurement of this transaction. Used for spend analytics by procurement organization and category management accountability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Organization; Oracle Cloud Procurement Business Unit

description: Unit of measure for the invoiced quantity (e.g., KG, LB, L, EA, CS, MT). Required for accurate spend-per-unit analysis, minimum order quantity (MOQ) compliance, and cross-supplier price benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM UOM; UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20; GS1

description: ERP document type code identifying the accounting document category for this spend transaction (e.g., SAP document types: RE=Invoice Receipt, KR=Vendor Invoice, KG=Vendor Credit Memo, KA=Vendor Document). Used for spend classification and financial reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Types; Oracle Cloud Payables Transaction Types

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this spend transaction was sourced (SAP S/4HANA FI/CO, Oracle Cloud Financials, or manual entry). Required for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; Oracle Cloud Financials; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Gross spend amount for this procurement transaction line in the transaction currency as invoiced by the supplier. Represents the actual purchase price multiplied by the invoiced quantity before any discounts or adjustments. Core metric for spend analytics and category management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; Oracle Cloud Financials; GAAP/IFRS; ISO 4217

description: Spend amount converted to the company code local currency (e.g., USD for US entities) using the exchange rate at the time of posting. Enables consistent spend aggregation and reporting across multi-currency procurement transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Translation; Oracle Cloud Financials; ISO 4217; GAAP/IFRS

description: High-level classification of the procurement spend indicating whether it is direct (raw materials, ingredients, packaging used in finished goods), indirect (MRO, services, overhead), capital expenditure (capex), or operating expenditure (opex). Critical dimension for spend analytics and financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; Oracle Cloud Financials; GAAP/IFRS

description: Standard cost per unit of measure for the material as defined in the ERP cost master (SAP CK11N standard cost estimate or Oracle standard cost). Used as the baseline for purchase price variance (PPV) calculation and COGS analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Product Costing; Oracle Cloud Cost Management; GAAP/IFRS

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the currency in which the supplier invoice was issued (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency spend analytics and foreign exchange exposure reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI; Oracle Cloud Financials

description: Current workflow status of the spend transaction in the source ERP system. 'Posted' indicates the invoice is fully approved and posted to the general ledger; 'reversed' indicates a reversal entry; 'blocked' indicates a payment block; 'parked' indicates a pending document; 'cleared' indicates the payment has been made.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Status; Oracle Cloud Payables Status

description: Actual price per unit of measure paid to the supplier on this transaction as invoiced, in the transaction currency. Used for purchase price variance (PPV) calculation against standard cost and contract price benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI Invoice Verification; Oracle Cloud Procurement; GAAP/IFRS

business_glossary_term: United Nations Standard Products and Services Code (UNSPSC)
description: Eight-digit United Nations Standard Products and Services Code (UNSPSC) classifying the procured commodity or service into a standardized global taxonomy. Enables cross-supplier, cross-category spend benchmarking and category management.
reference: UNSPSC v23 (UN/CEFACT); GS1; Codex Alimentarius Commission

description: Timestamp when this spend transaction record was last modified or refreshed in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental processing, and audit trail.
Unique surrogate identifier for each supplier-material combination record in the Approved Vendor List. Primary key for the AVL entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master / Info Record; TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Reference to the supplier/vendor master record. Identifies the approved supplier entity in this AVL entry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master (LFA1); TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Reference to the raw material, ingredient, or packaging material record that this supplier is approved to supply.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master (MARA); TraceGains Specification Management

description: Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Ingredient qualification process ties each AVL record to the rd_project that requested the material, enabling traceability in the qualification report.

Indicates whether the supplier has been specifically assessed and approved for allergen management controls relevant to this material. Supports allergen matrix compliance and FSMA preventive controls.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 — Preventive Controls; FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); GFSI

description: Date on which the supplier-material combination was formally approved and added to the AVL. Represents the effective start of the approval period.
reference: TraceGains Supplier Compliance; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; SAP S/4HANA MM — Info Record (EINE-EKDAT)

description: Free-text field capturing conditions, restrictions, or rationale associated with the approval decision. For example, conditional approvals may specify required corrective actions or usage limitations.
reference: TraceGains Supplier Compliance; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117

description: Current lifecycle status of the supplier-material approval. 'approved' = fully qualified; 'conditionally_approved' = approved with restrictions; 'suspended' = temporarily halted pending investigation; 'revoked' = permanently removed; 'pending_review' = under qualification assessment; 'expired' = approval period lapsed without renewal.
reference: TraceGains Supplier Compliance; SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Evaluation; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; GFSI

description: Sourcing tier designation for this supplier-material combination. 'primary' = preferred first-source supplier; 'secondary' = qualified backup supplier; 'contingency' = emergency-use only supplier activated during supply disruptions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Source List (EORD); Supply Chain Risk Management best practices

description: Name or employee ID of the quality or procurement professional who formally approved this supplier-material combination for the AVL. Supports audit trail and regulatory accountability.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; ISO 22000:2018; GMP 21 CFR Part 110

description: Classification of the most recent audit conducted for this supplier-material combination. 'first_party' = self-assessment; 'second_party' = customer-conducted; 'third_party' = independent certification body; 'remote' = virtual audit; 'desk_audit' = document review only.
reference: GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; ISO 22000:2018; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117

description: Business-facing unique identifier for the AVL record, formatted as AVL-XXXXXXXX. Used for cross-system referencing between SAP MM and TraceGains.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record; TraceGains Document Management

description: Expiry date of the supplier's primary food safety certification (e.g., SQF, FSSC 22000, BRC). Triggers re-certification alerts and may affect approval status if lapsed.
reference: GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; FSSC 22000; SQF Code; BRC Global Standard

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the material is sourced or manufactured by this supplier. Required for FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP) compliance.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart L — Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP); ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL)

description: Timestamp when this AVL record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: ISO 22000:2018 — Records and Documentation; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 — Records

description: Date on which the current approval expires and must be renewed through re-qualification. Null indicates an open-ended approval with no scheduled expiry. Drives automated renewal workflow triggers.
reference: TraceGains Supplier Compliance; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; ISO 22000:2018

description: Primary food safety or quality certification held by the supplier for this material. SQF (Safe Quality Food), BRC (British Retail Consortium), FSSC 22000 (Food Safety System Certification), IFS (International Featured Standards). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|BRC|FSSC_22000|IFS|GlobalGAP|Halal|Kosher|Organic|none — promote to reference product]
reference: GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; SQF Code; FSSC 22000; BRC Global Standard; ISO 22000:2018

business_glossary_term: Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether this supplier-material combination is subject to FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program requirements due to the material being imported from outside the United States.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart L — Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP)

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Verified Flag
description: Indicates whether the supplier has been verified as having an implemented and validated HACCP plan for this material, as required under FSMA preventive controls and GFSI standards.
reference: HACCP Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

description: Date of the most recent quality or food safety audit conducted at this supplier's facility for this material. Supports HACCP supplier verification and GFSI compliance tracking.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 — Supplier Verification; HACCP Codex Alimentarius; GFSI Benchmarking; ISO 22000:2018

description: Numeric score (0.00–100.00) from the most recent supplier quality/food safety audit. Used in supplier performance scorecards and AVL renewal decisions.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 — Supplier Verification; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Date of the most recent supplier requalification assessment for this material. Used to track compliance with periodic requalification requirements under FSMA and GFSI.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this AVL record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between TraceGains and SAP MM, and compliance audit trails.
reference: ISO 22000:2018 — Records and Documentation; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 — Records

description: Standard procurement lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to material receipt for this supplier-material combination. Used in demand planning and inventory optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record (EINE-WEBAZ); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: High-level classification of the material type the supplier is approved to provide. Drives procurement routing and compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ingredient|packaging|indirect|co-manufacturing|toll_manufacturing — promote to reference product if additional categories are added]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Type (MARA-MTART); FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

description: Short description of the raw material, ingredient, or packaging item as defined in the SAP Material Master. Supports readability in AVL reports.
SAP material number (MATNR) for the raw material, ingredient, or packaging item that the supplier is approved to provide. Denormalized for operational reporting.
Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required by this supplier for this material. Sourced from SAP MM Purchasing Info Record. Used in purchase order creation and demand planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record (EINE-MINBM); Procurement Policy

business_glossary_term: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) Unit of Measure
description: Unit of measure for the Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ), aligned with SAP MM base unit of measure. Examples: KG (kilogram), LB (pound), L (liter), EA (each), CS (case), PAL (pallet), MT (metric ton). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|EA|CS|PAL|MT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Unit of Measure (T006-MSEHI); ISO 80000-1

description: Scheduled date for the next supplier requalification review. Calculated based on requalification frequency policy and last requalification date. Drives procurement compliance calendar.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Indicates whether the supplier's material has been verified as non-GMO (Genetically Modified Organism), supporting clean label and non-GMO product claims.
reference: USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (NBFDS); Non-GMO Project Verified Standard

description: Indicates whether the supplier holds USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification for this material. Required for organic product formulations and clean label claims.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; FDA Organic Labeling

description: SAP plant code (WERKS) indicating the manufacturing or distribution facility for which this supplier-material approval is valid. Null indicates approval is valid across all plants in the purchasing organization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Plant (T001W-WERKS); Manufacturing and Production domain

description: Indicates whether this supplier is designated as the preferred (first-choice) source for this material within its approval tier. Used in automated purchase order source determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Source List (EORD-VORZUG); Procurement Policy

description: SAP MM Purchasing Organization code (EKORG) responsible for managing this supplier-material approval. Determines the organizational scope of the AVL entry.

The primary quality and regulatory basis on which the supplier approval was granted. Indicates the type of evidence used to qualify the supplier for this material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: audit|certificate|questionnaire|third_party_cert|historical_performance|regulatory_approval — promote to reference product]
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 — Supplier Verification; HACCP Codex Alimentarius; GFSI Benchmarking; ISO 22000:2018

description: SAP MM Purchasing Info Record number (EINE-INFNR) linking this AVL entry to the corresponding purchasing conditions and pricing data in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record (EINE/EINA)

description: Indicates whether this supplier is the only approved source for this material, representing a single-source supply risk. Triggers supply risk alerts and dual-sourcing initiatives.
reference: Supply Chain Risk Management; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 — Supply Chain Program

description: Indicates whether this supplier-material combination is included in the SAP MM Source List (EORD), making it eligible for automatic source determination in purchase requisitions and orders.

Risk classification assigned to this supplier-material combination based on food safety hazard severity, supply criticality, and supplier performance history. Drives frequency of verification activities under FSMA.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 — Hazard Analysis; HACCP Codex Alimentarius; GFSI; ISO 22000:2018

description: Reason code or description explaining why the supplier-material approval was suspended or revoked. Populated when approval_status is 'suspended' or 'revoked'. Supports CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) tracking.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Reference to the primary compliance document (e.g., supplier specification, audit report, certificate of analysis) stored in TraceGains Document Management that supports this AVL approval.
reference: TraceGains Document Management; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the procurement category strategy record. Primary key for the category_strategy data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module – Category Management

description: Indicates whether this procurement category includes materials that are or may contain regulated allergens (e.g., dairy, tree nuts, wheat, soy, peanuts). When true, suppliers must provide allergen declarations and the category is subject to enhanced TraceGains specification management and FDA allergen labeling compliance.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; Codex Alimentarius Allergen Management; TraceGains Allergen Matrix

description: Targeted procurement savings in US dollars for this category in the current strategy period. Represents the expected reduction in total cost of ownership (TCO) through sourcing initiatives, negotiations, and demand management. Feeds into annual procurement planning and EBITDA improvement targets.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO – Cost Savings Tracking; Oracle Cloud Procurement – Savings Management; CIPS Procurement Value Measurement

description: Total annual procurement spend for this category in US dollars, based on the most recently completed fiscal year. Used to size the category, prioritize sourcing investment, and calculate savings targets. Classified confidential as it reveals commercial spend positioning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO – Spend Analytics; Oracle Cloud Financials – Procurement Spend; ISO 20400 Sustainable Procurement

business_glossary_term: Approved Vendor List (AVL) Target Supplier Count
description: Target number of approved suppliers to maintain on the Approved Vendor List (AVL) for this category. Balances supply security with supplier relationship management investment. Reviewed annually during strategic sourcing planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Approved Vendor List; TraceGains – Supplier Compliance; Oracle Cloud Procurement – Supplier Qualification

description: Name or code of the primary business unit or division that owns and sponsors this category strategy (e.g., Snacks BU, Beverages BU, Dairy BU, Supply Chain). Supports category strategy alignment with business unit P&L and procurement planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO – Cost Center / Business Unit; Oracle Cloud Financials – Business Unit

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the procurement category (e.g., DAIRY-ING, GRAIN-OIL, FLEX-PKG, CO-MFG, MRO). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing category hierarchy and used as the business key across ERP and sourcing systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Purchasing Category Hierarchy; Oracle Cloud Procurement – Category Management

description: Numeric level of this category within the procurement category hierarchy (e.g., 1 = top-level segment, 2 = category, 3 = sub-category). Supports hierarchical drill-down in spend analytics and category management dashboards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Purchasing Category Hierarchy; CIPS Category Management Framework

description: Corporate email address of the procurement category manager. Used for workflow notifications, strategy review communications, and stakeholder escalation. Classified confidential as it is an employee contact identifier.
reference: Workday HCM – Core HR; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Full name of the procurement category manager responsible for owning and executing this category strategy. Used for accountability, escalation routing, and category management reporting. Sourced from Workday HCM employee master.
reference: Workday HCM – Core HR; CIPS Category Management Framework

description: Full descriptive name of the procurement category (e.g., Dairy Ingredients, Grain and Oilseed Commodities, Flexible Packaging, Co-Manufacturing Services, Maintenance Repair and Operations). Used in category management reporting and strategic sourcing planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Purchasing Category Hierarchy; CIPS Category Management Framework

description: Classification of the category by commodity type. 'agricultural_commodity' = exchange-traded raw materials (grains, dairy, oils); 'industrial_commodity' = packaging materials, chemicals; 'manufactured_input' = processed ingredients, co-pack services; 'service' = logistics, co-manufacturing; 'indirect' = MRO, facilities, IT. Drives hedging eligibility and pricing contract structure.
reference: CBOT/CME Commodity Classification; SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Group; FDA FSMA Supply Chain Program

description: Target contract duration in months for supplier agreements in this category. Informs negotiation strategy and contract renewal planning. Typical ranges: 12 months for commodity categories, 24–36 months for strategic categories with capital investment requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Outline Agreements; Oracle Cloud Procurement – Contract Management

description: Preferred commercial contract structure for this category. 'spot' = transactional market pricing; 'annual_fixed' = fixed price for 12-month period; 'multi_year' = fixed or structured pricing over 2+ years; 'index_linked' = price tied to commodity index (e.g., USDA, CBOT); 'cost_plus' = supplier cost transparency with agreed margin. Drives contract strategy in SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing contracts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Outline Agreements; Oracle Cloud Procurement – Contract Management; CIPS Contract Management

business_glossary_term: Dual Source Primary Supplier Split Percentage
description: Target volume allocation percentage to the primary supplier when a dual-source policy is in effect (0.00–100.00%). For example, 70.00 means 70% to primary supplier and 30% to secondary. Null when supply_base_policy is 'single_source' or 'multi_source'. Supports supply continuity risk management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Source List Quota Arrangement; Oracle Cloud Procurement – Supplier Allocation

description: Date from which this category strategy version becomes binding and operational. Aligns with the annual procurement planning cycle start date (typically fiscal year start).
reference: Oracle Cloud Procurement – Strategic Sourcing; SAP S/4HANA MM – Purchasing Contracts

description: Date on which this category strategy version expires or is scheduled for renewal. Nullable for open-ended strategies. Triggers review workflow when approaching expiry.
reference: Oracle Cloud Procurement – Strategic Sourcing; SAP S/4HANA MM – Purchasing Contracts

description: Ethical sourcing and labor standards audit framework required for suppliers in this category. 'sedex' = Supplier Ethical Data Exchange; 'smeta' = Sedex Members Ethical Trade Audit; 'bsci' = Business Social Compliance Initiative; 'sa8000' = Social Accountability International; 'custom' = company-specific standard; 'none' = not applicable. Supports responsible sourcing programs.
reference: Sedex SMETA; SA8000 Social Accountability International; BSCI; UN Global Compact; FTC Advertising and Marketing Claims

description: Minimum food safety certification or standard required for suppliers in this category. 'gmp' = Good Manufacturing Practice; 'haccp' = Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; 'gfsi' = Global Food Safety Initiative benchmarked scheme; 'sqf' = Safe Quality Food; 'fssc22000' = Food Safety System Certification 22000. Mandatory for direct food ingredient and packaging categories. Managed via TraceGains supplier compliance.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; SQF Code; FSSC 22000; HACCP Codex Alimentarius; ISO 22000

description: Geographic scope of the approved supply base for this category. 'domestic' = suppliers within the home country only; 'regional' = suppliers within a defined trade region; 'global' = worldwide supplier base; 'preferred_domestic' = global scope with preference for domestic suppliers. Impacts trade compliance, tariff exposure, and supply chain resilience.
reference: FDA FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP); USDA Country of Origin Labeling; Codex Alimentarius; EPA Environmental Compliance

description: Target percentage of forecasted commodity volume to be covered by hedging instruments (0.00–100.00%). For example, 75.00 means 75% of projected annual volume should be hedged. Supports COGS risk management and financial planning. Classified confidential as it reveals pricing risk posture.
reference: CME Group Risk Management; SAP S/4HANA CO – Commodity Risk Management; FASB ASC 815 Derivatives and Hedging

description: Commodity price risk management strategy applied to this category. 'no_hedge' = market price accepted; 'forward_contract' = fixed-price forward purchase agreements; 'futures' = exchange-traded futures contracts (e.g., CBOT corn, CME dairy); 'options' = price floor/ceiling options; 'index_pricing' = contract pricing linked to published commodity index (e.g., USDA dairy price). Planned date for the next scheduled audit of this supplier facility, based on audit frequency requirements and previous audit outcomes.
Total number of observations or opportunities for improvement noted during the audit that do not constitute non-conformances but represent best practice recommendations.

description: Percentage of inbound shipments meeting the minimum remaining shelf life requirement at time of receipt, as defined in the supplier specification and procurement contract. Supports FEFO inventory management.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the total_spend_amount field (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency spend analytics and FX exposure reporting.

description: Indicates whether the supplier has formally acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the scorecard results. Required for closed-loop supplier performance management and dispute resolution.

description: Total number of purchase orders (POs) issued to this supplier during the evaluation period. Provides volume context for interpreting rate-based KPIs such as OTIF rate and invoice accuracy rate.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchase Order (ME21N/ME2M); Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Total procurement spend with this supplier during the evaluation period in the reporting currency. Used for spend analytics, strategic sourcing prioritization, and COGS impact assessment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Spend Analytics; Oracle Cloud Financials; COGS Management

description: Timestamp when this scorecard record was most recently modified. Tracks revisions during the review and approval workflow.
reference: ISO 8601; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Audit Standards

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each supplier site record. Primary key for the supplier_site data product in the procurement domain.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Site‑level compliance requires associating each supplier site with its official establishment registration record for regulatory inspections.
Reference to the parent supplier (vendor) record to which this site belongs. A single supplier may operate multiple sites with distinct compliance profiles and capabilities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master (LFA1); Oracle Cloud Procurement — Supplier Header

description: Primary street address line of the supplier site. Used for logistics routing, ASN validation, and regulatory country-of-origin documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Address Management (ADRC-STREET); FSMA 204 — Traceability Rule

description: Secondary address line for the supplier site (suite, building, floor, or unit number). Supplements address_line1 for precise logistics and compliance documentation.

Declared annual production or throughput capacity of the supplier site in the site's primary unit of measure. Used for supply assurance assessments, dual-sourcing decisions, and demand planning stress tests.
reference: SAP IBP — Supply Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder — Supply Planning; TraceGains — Supplier Capability

description: Date on which this supplier site was formally approved and added to the Approved Vendor List (AVL). Marks the start of the site's eligibility for purchase order creation.
reference: TraceGains — Approved Supplier List; SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master; ISO 22000:2018 §8.5.1

description: Indicates the site's standing on the Approved Vendor List (AVL). Only sites with 'approved' status are eligible for standard purchase order creation. 'conditional' or 'probationary' may require additional quality oversight.
reference: TraceGains — Approved Supplier List; GFSI — Supplier Approval Requirements; ISO 22000:2018 §8.5.1

description: Categorical outcome of the most recent supplier site audit. Drives AVL status updates and corrective action plan (CAP) initiation. 'conditional_pass' requires documented corrective actions within a defined timeframe.
reference: TraceGains — Audit Management; GFSI — Audit Outcome Classification; SQF Institute

description: Unit of measure for the annual_production_capacity field (e.g., MT for metric tonnes, KG, L, CS). Ensures correct interpretation of capacity data across procurement and supply planning systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Unit of Measure (T006); ISO 80000-1

description: City in which the supplier site is physically located. Used for geographic sourcing analysis, logistics zone assignment, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the supplier site location (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Drives country-of-origin labeling, import/export compliance, and tariff classification.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; FDA FSMA — Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP); Codex Alimentarius

description: Timestamp when the supplier site record was first created in the source system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle tracking per enterprise data governance standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Change Documents; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the default transaction currency used with this supplier site (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used in purchase order creation and spend analytics.
reference: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master (LFM1-WAERS)

description: FDA-assigned facility registration number for the supplier site, required under FSMA for domestic and foreign food facilities. Used to verify regulatory standing and support FSVP compliance.
reference: FDA — FSMA Food Facility Registration (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart H); FDA Bioterrorism Act of 2002

description: Expiration date of the site's primary food safety certification (SQF, FSSC 22000, BRC, etc.). Triggers automated renewal alerts in TraceGains and may suspend sourcing eligibility if expired.
reference: GFSI — Certification Validity Requirements; TraceGains — Document Expiry Management; FSSC 22000

description: Primary food safety certification held by this supplier site (e.g., SQF, FSSC 22000, BRC, IFS). Determines eligibility for sourcing food-contact materials and ingredients. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|FSSC_22000|BRC|IFS|SQF_2000|HACCP|GMP|none — promote to reference product]
reference: GFSI — Global Food Safety Initiative Benchmarking; FSSC 22000; SQF Institute; BRC Global Standards; ISO 22000:2018

description: Specific GFSI-benchmarked certification scheme held by this site. Distinct from the broader food_safety_certification field — captures the exact scheme name for regulatory and customer audit reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|FSSC_22000|BRC_GSFS|IFS_Food|GLOBALG.A.P.|PRIMUS_GFS|none — promote to reference product]
reference: GFSI — Benchmarked Schemes List; Codex Alimentarius; ISO 22000:2018

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Certified
description: Indicates whether the supplier site holds a current Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) certification. Required for sites supplying direct food ingredients or food-contact packaging.
reference: FDA — FSMA Preventive Controls Rule (21 CFR Part 117); Codex Alimentarius — HACCP Guidelines; USDA FSIS

description: Indicates whether the supplier site holds a current Halal certification from a recognized certifying body. Required for ingredient sourcing for Halal-labeled product lines in applicable markets.
reference: ISWA — Islamic Society of the Washington Area; IFANCA; Codex Alimentarius — General Guidelines for Use of the Term 'Halal'

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code governing the transfer of risk and freight responsibility for shipments from this supplier site (e.g., FOB, DDP, CIF). reference: ICC — Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record (EKPO-INCO1); Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Indicates whether the supplier site holds a current Kosher certification from a recognized certifying agency. Relevant for ingredient sourcing for Kosher-labeled product lines.
reference: Orthodox Union (OU); Star-K; FDA — Labeling Requirements (21 CFR Part 101)

description: Date of the most recent quality or food safety audit conducted at this supplier site. Used to determine audit frequency compliance and trigger re-audit scheduling per GFSI and internal procurement policy.
reference: GFSI — Supplier Audit Requirements; TraceGains — Audit Management; ISO 22000:2018 §9.2

description: Numeric score achieved by the supplier site in the most recent quality or food safety audit, expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used in supplier performance scorecards and AVL status decisions.
reference: TraceGains — Audit Scoring; SQF — Audit Scoring Methodology; GFSI

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the supplier site record in the source system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change detection in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Change Documents; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5

description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the supplier site in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Enables geospatial supply chain risk analysis, logistics optimization, and distance-to-DC calculations.

Standard procurement lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt at the destination DC for this specific supplier site. Site-level lead times override supplier-level defaults in MRP/IBP planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record (EINE-WEBAZ); SAP IBP — Supply Planning Parameters; JDA/Blue Yonder — Supply Planning

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the supplier site in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used alongside latitude for geospatial supply chain risk analysis and logistics routing.

Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required by this supplier site per purchase order line. Used in procurement planning, MRP order proposals, and trade negotiation. Expressed in the site's standard ordering unit of measure.

business_glossary_term: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) Unit of Measure
description: Unit of measure applicable to the MOQ field (e.g., KG, LB, CS, PAL, EA). Ensures correct interpretation of the MOQ value in procurement planning and ERP order creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Unit of Measure (T006); ISO 80000-1 — Quantities and Units

description: Scheduled due date for the next quality or food safety audit at this supplier site. Calculated based on audit frequency policy, risk tier, and last audit date. Managed in TraceGains.
reference: TraceGains — Audit Scheduling; GFSI — Audit Frequency Requirements; ISO 22000:2018

description: Indicates whether the supplier site holds a current organic certification (e.g., USDA NOP, EU Organic). Required for sites supplying organic-labeled ingredients or finished goods.

description: Code identifying the negotiated payment terms for this supplier site (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30). Drives accounts payable scheduling and cash flow planning in SAP FI.

description: Postal or ZIP code of the supplier site. Used for logistics carrier zone rating, geographic clustering, and regulatory reporting.

description: Full name of the quality or food safety contact at the supplier site. Used for HACCP documentation requests, corrective action plan (CAP) communications, and audit coordination.

description: Primary email address for the supplier site, used for purchase order transmission, ASN notifications, and compliance document requests via TraceGains.

description: Human-readable name of the supplier site (e.g., 'Acme Ingredients — Chicago Plant', 'Global Packaging — Rotterdam DC'). Used in procurement documents, scorecards, and audit reports.

description: Primary telephone number for the supplier site, used for procurement communications, quality escalations, and logistics coordination.

Current lifecycle status of the supplier site record. Controls whether the site is eligible for purchase order creation and sourcing. 'suspended' indicates a temporary hold pending audit or corrective action.

description: Classification of the supplier site by its primary operational function. Drives sourcing decisions, compliance requirements, and audit frequency. Primary key for inbound_receipt
Reference to the inbound logistics shipment or Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) associated with this goods receipt. Enables end-to-end supply chain traceability from supplier dispatch to DC receipt.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; GS1 ASN (DESADV) Standard

description: SAP plant code (WERKS) identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the goods were physically received. Drives inventory posting and valuation area.
Internal identifier for the material or finished good received (MATNR). Links to the material master for product specifications, allergen data, shelf life parameters, and storage requirements.
SAP storage location code (LGORT) within the plant where received goods are physically placed. Determines bin assignment in WMS and supports FIFO/FEFO rotation policy enforcement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.LGORT; SAP WM

description: Internal identifier for the supplier/vendor (LIFNR) from whom the materials were received. Enables supplier performance tracking, OTIF measurement, and accounts payable matching. PARTY_REFERENCE category.

Supplier-transmitted Advanced Shipping Notice number received via EDI (856 transaction set) prior to physical delivery. Used to pre-receive inventory, schedule dock appointments, and validate shipment contents against PO.
reference: GS1 DESADV; ANSI X12 856 EDI; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Supplier or internally assigned batch/lot number (CHARG) for the received material. Critical for food safety traceability, FEFO rotation, allergen management, and recall execution per FSMA 204 requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.CHARG; FSMA 204; FDA 21 CFR Part 1

description: Carrier-issued bill of lading number for the inbound shipment. Serves as the legal contract of carriage and is required for freight claims, customs clearance, and 3PL reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; US UCC Article 7; INCOTERMS 2020

description: Reference number of the supplier-provided Certificate of Analysis accompanying the shipment. Documents quality test results (microbiological, chemical, physical) for the received batch. Required for GFSI, SQF, and HACCP compliance.
reference: TraceGains Supplier Compliance; GFSI; SQF; HACCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 117

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the received material was manufactured or grown. Required for customs declarations, USDA country-of-origin labeling (COOL), and import compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.HERBL; USDA COOL; FDA Import Regulations; Codex Alimentarius

description: Timestamp when the goods receipt record was first created in the source system (SAP CPUDT + CPUTM). Used for audit trail and data lineage. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the valuation and receipt value amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency operations across global DC network.
Supplier-provided delivery note or packing slip number (LIEFN) accompanying the inbound shipment. Used for three-way match (PO, GR, invoice) and ASN reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MKPF.XBLNR; GS1 ASN Standard

description: The date on the physical delivery document or packing slip (BLDAT) as provided by the supplier. May differ from posting_date when receipts are backdated or processed with delay.

Expiration or best-before date of the received batch. Governs FEFO inventory rotation, shelf life compliance, and eligibility for distribution. Mandatory for perishable food and beverage products per FDA and EFSA regulations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master — MHDRZ; FDA 21 CFR Part 101; EU Reg 1169/2011; EFSA

description: Free-text notes entered by the receiving clerk at time of goods receipt. Captures exceptions, damage observations, temperature deviations, or other relevant receiving conditions not captured in structured fields.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MKPF.BKTXT; GMP Documentation

description: INCOTERMS code from the purchase order defining the point of title transfer and risk for the inbound shipment (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP, EXW). Determines when inventory ownership transfers to Food Beverage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKKO.INCO1; ICC INCOTERMS 2020

description: Indicates whether the received goods are consignment stock (supplier-owned inventory held at Food Beverage's facility). True = consignment; False = standard purchased stock. Affects accounts payable timing and inventory ownership.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Consignment Processing; IAS 2

description: Indicates whether this goods receipt represents a return delivery from a customer or a reversal of a prior outbound delivery being returned to stock. True = return; False = standard inbound receipt.
Date on which the received batch was manufactured by the supplier or co-packer. Used for shelf life calculation, FEFO rotation policy, and food safety compliance documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master; FDA 21 CFR Part 117; FSMA

description: SAP S/4HANA MM material document number (MBLNR) generated upon goods receipt posting. Serves as the externally-known business identifier for this receipt transaction, referenced on supplier correspondence and internal audit trails.
Fiscal year (MJAHR) associated with the SAP material document number. Required in combination with material_document_number to uniquely identify the document in SAP S/4HANA.

SAP MM movement type code (BWART) classifying the nature of the goods movement (e.g., 101 = GR for PO, 103 = GR into blocked stock, 105 = GR from blocked stock, 501 = receipt without PO). Drives inventory account determination and stock type posting.

Original quantity ordered on the purchase order line item. Compared against received_qty to calculate receipt variance and identify short-shipments or over-deliveries for supplier performance management.

The accounting posting date (BUDAT) on which the goods receipt is recorded in SAP, triggering inventory update and accounts payable accrual. Determines the fiscal period for financial reporting. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MKPF.BUDAT; IAS 2 Inventories

description: Line item number (EBELP) within the purchase order that this goods receipt references. Identifies the specific material, quantity, and price agreed upon in the PO.
SAP purchase order number (EBELN) against which this goods receipt is posted. Links the physical receipt to the procurement commitment and triggers accounts payable processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.EBELN; ISO 15000 EDI

description: SAP QM inspection lot number (PRUEFLOS) created automatically upon goods receipt for materials subject to quality inspection. Links the receipt to the quality inspection results and usage decision in Veeva Vault or SAP QM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — QMEL; Veeva Vault QMS; ISO 22000; FSSC 22000

description: Outcome of the quality inspection usage decision for the received batch. 'accepted' = released to unrestricted stock; 'rejected' = returned to vendor or destroyed; 'conditional_release' = released with deviations noted; 'pending' = inspection in progress; 'waived' = skip-lot or pre-approved supplier.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; Veeva Vault QMS; ISO 22000; GFSI; SQF

description: Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt transaction. 'posted' = inventory updated; 'reversed' = full reversal posted; 'blocked' = held pending quality or compliance review; 'in_quality_inspection' = under QM usage decision; 'partially_reversed' = partial quantity reversed; 'cancelled' = voided before posting. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: posted|reversed|blocked|in_quality_inspection|partially_reversed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Movement Type (BWART); FSMA 204

description: Precise date and time when the goods were physically received and scanned at the dock door or receiving bay. Used for OTIF calculation, lead time analysis, and dock scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; GS1 EPCIS Standard

description: Actual quantity of material physically received and posted to inventory (MENGE). Expressed in the base unit of measure. Used for inventory reconciliation, fill rate calculation, and OTIF measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.MENGE; GS1 GPC

description: Classification of the receiving location type. Distinguishes between manufacturing plants, distribution centers (DC), co-packer facilities, 3PL warehouses, and cross-dock points. Drives putaway rules and inventory ownership.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Configuration; Supply Chain Network Design

description: Number of days remaining between the receipt posting date and the expiration date at time of receipt. Used to enforce minimum remaining shelf life (MRSL) thresholds for customer acceptance and distribution eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management; GS1 Shelf Life Standards; GFSI

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this goods receipt record was ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: SAP stock type (INSMK) into which the received quantity is posted. 'unrestricted' = available for use/sale; 'quality_inspection' = under QM hold; 'blocked' = not available; 'restricted' = batch-restricted; 'returns' = returned goods stock. Governs FIFO/FEFO rotation eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.INSMK; HACCP; GMP

description: Supplier's own lot or batch identification number as printed on the shipping label or certificate of analysis. Distinct from the internal batch_number assigned in SAP. Required for supplier traceability and recall coordination.
reference: FSMA 204; FDA 21 CFR Part 1; GS1 EPCIS

description: Total inventory value posted for this receipt (valuation_price × received_qty) in local currency. Drives the accounts payable accrual entry and inventory balance update in SAP FI/CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.DMBTR; IAS 2; IFRS 15

description: Base unit of measure (MEINS) in which the received quantity is expressed (e.g., KG, L, EA, CS, PAL). Aligns with the material master base UOM for inventory valuation and planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.MEINS; UN/CEFACT Rec 20

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this goods receipt record in the source system. Supports change detection in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline and audit compliance. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.

Unit price at which the received material is valuated in inventory (DMBTR/MENGE). Used for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and accounts payable accrual. Reflects moving average price or standard price per material master. MONETARY_TRIPLET category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.DMBTR; IAS 2 Inventories

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the inbound shipment record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete inbound logistics event with a full lifecycle from ASN through goods receipt and putaway.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module — Goods Receipt (MIGO/MIGO_GR)

description: Reference to the transportation carrier or 3PL partner responsible for the inbound shipment leg. Used for carrier performance tracking, OTIF (On Time In Full) measurement, and freight cost allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Transportation (LT01); JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Each inbound shipment requires a linked COA to satisfy FDA/GFSI audit and product release decisions.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocate inbound logistics expenses to the responsible cost center for expense tracking.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Inbound shipments must be tied to the registered facility of the supplier for FDA/USDA import compliance tracking.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Post freight and handling charges of inbound shipments to the designated GL account.
Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: INBOUND_SHIPMENT quality inspection results must be linked to INSPECTION_LOT for compliance release and traceability reports.
Reference to the receiving plant or Distribution Center (DC) in the supply network where the inbound shipment is destined. Used for network design analytics and inventory update triggering.

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) must be tied to the originating purchase order for traceability and compliance reporting.

Reference to the material or Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) being received. Links to the product/material master for ingredient, packaging, or finished goods identification. Supports RESOURCE_REFERENCE for downstream line-level analytics.

description: Reference to the supplier, co-packer, or inter-company transfer source party in the master data. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for carrier performance tracking and supplier compliance reporting via TraceGains.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master (XK01); TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Precise timestamp when the carrier physically arrived at the receiving dock or gate. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category. Used for OTIF (On Time In Full) calculation, carrier performance KPIs, and dock turnaround time analysis.

description: Date the Advance Ship Notice (ASN) was transmitted by the supplier. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (day-level precision for planning). Used for lead time analysis and supplier compliance measurement against EDI 856 transmission SLAs.

description: Externally-known business identifier for the Advance Ship Notice (ASN) transmitted by the supplier or co-packer prior to physical shipment. description: Supplier-assigned or internally-generated batch or lot number assigned to the received material at goods receipt. Critical for FIFO/FEFO rotation policy enforcement, allergen matrix traceability, HACCP compliance, and FSMA lot-level traceability requirements.

description: ISO 6346 shipping container identification number for ocean or intermodal freight. Used for port customs clearance, container tracking, and import compliance documentation.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the material was manufactured or processed. Required for customs declarations, USDA country-of-origin labeling (COOL) compliance, and import duty calculation.

description: Timestamp when the inbound shipment record was first created in the source system (SAP MM or EDI inbound processing). RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Document Creation; ISO 22000 Traceability

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the invoice_value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP processing and financial reporting. MONETARY_TRIPLET component.

description: Supplier-issued delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the physical shipment. Used for goods receipt posting in SAP MM (MIGO) and for dispute resolution with suppliers.

description: Expiration or best-before date of the received batch/lot. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation, shelf life compliance, and quality hold decisions. Mandatory for food safety regulatory compliance under FDA and FSMA.

description: Freight payment terms indicating who bears the transportation cost for the inbound shipment. Prepaid means supplier pays; collect means Food Beverage pays at destination. Used for freight cost allocation and AP processing.

description: Timestamp when the goods receipt was formally posted in SAP MM (MIGO transaction), triggering inventory update and accounts payable (AP) posting. Critical for FIFO/FEFO lot assignment and financial period close.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Receipt Posting (MIGO); FSMA Traceability Rule

description: Total gross weight of the inbound shipment in kilograms, including packaging and pallets. Used for freight cost calculation, dock capacity planning, and customs documentation. QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between supplier and Food Beverage for the shipment. reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order Incoterms; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Supplier-issued commercial invoice number associated with the inbound shipment. Used for three-way match (PO, GR, Invoice) in accounts payable (AP) processing and financial period close.

description: Commercial invoice value of the inbound shipment in the transaction currency. Used for AP three-way match, landed cost calculation, and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) valuation. MONETARY_TRIPLET component (gross amount).

description: Indicates whether the inbound shipment contains hazardous materials (e.g., cleaning chemicals, food-grade solvents) requiring special handling, labeling, and regulatory compliance under DOT/IATA regulations.

description: Indicates whether the inbound shipment requires temperature-controlled (cold chain) transportation and storage conditions (e.g., refrigerated or frozen). Triggers cold chain compliance checks and specialized putaway routing in WMS.

description: Date the material was manufactured by the supplier or co-packer. Used in conjunction with expiry_date for FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation policy enforcement and shelf life management.

description: Boolean indicator of whether the inbound shipment was delivered On Time and In Full (OTIF) against the scheduled_arrival_date and quantity_shipped. True = OTIF achieved; False = OTIF missed. Used for supplier and carrier performance scorecards.

description: Number of pallets included in the inbound shipment. Used for dock scheduling, labor resource planning, and warehouse space allocation at the receiving DC or plant.

description: Carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number or Bill of Lading (BOL) number used to track the shipment in transit. Enables real-time inbound visibility and carrier performance tracking. Used for freight claims and dispute resolution.

description: Timestamp when the received goods were physically placed into the designated storage location within the plant or DC. Marks completion of the inbound logistics lifecycle. Used for dock-to-stock cycle time measurement.

description: Status of the quality inspection lot associated with the goods receipt in SAP QM. Indicates whether the received batch has passed, failed, or is pending quality inspection before release to unrestricted stock. Supports HACCP, SQF, and GFSI compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Inspection Lot (QA01); HACCP; SQF; GFSI; ISO 22000; Veeva Vault QMS

description: Actual quantity of the material physically received and posted in SAP MM goods receipt (MIGO). May differ from quantity_shipped due to transit damage, short shipment, or over-delivery. Triggers inventory update upon posting.

description: Total quantity of the material dispatched by the supplier as declared in the ASN. Expressed in the base unit of measure (UoM) of the material master. Used as the baseline for receipt variance calculation and supplier compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Inbound Delivery Quantity; GS1 DESADV

description: Unit of measure (UoM) for quantity_shipped and quantity_received fields (e.g., KG, LB, CS, EA, L, GAL). Aligns with SAP MM material master base unit of measure. Required for accurate inventory valuation and supply planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Unit of Measure (T006); ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Difference between quantity_shipped and quantity_received (shipped minus received). Positive value indicates short delivery; negative value indicates over-delivery. Used for receipt-vs-order reconciliation, supplier dispute management, and AP invoice matching.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Receipt Variance; Oracle Cloud Procurement — Three-Way Match

description: Reason code explaining the cause of any quantity variance between quantity_shipped and quantity_received. Used for supplier dispute management, root cause analysis, and AP invoice reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: short_shipment|over_delivery|transit_damage|quality_rejection|counting_error|no_variance — promote to reference product if additional codes required]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Receipt Variance; Oracle Cloud Procurement — Three-Way Match

description: Expected date of physical arrival at the receiving plant or DC as communicated in the ASN or purchase order. Used for inbound dock scheduling, labor planning, and OTIF (On Time In Full) baseline measurement.

description: Current lifecycle status of the inbound shipment. Tracks progression from in-transit through physical receipt and putaway. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Inbound Delivery Status; GS1 Supply Chain Visibility

description: Classification of the inbound shipment origin type. Distinguishes between direct supplier deliveries, co-packing receipts, inter-company stock transfers, 3PL-managed transfers, and toll manufacturing returns. Supports supply chain segmentation and cost allocation.
