description: SAP storage location code within the receiving plant or DC where the goods are physically putaway after receipt. Supports FIFO/FEFO rotation policy enforcement and WMS integration.

description: Required temperature range classification for the inbound shipment during transport and storage. Ambient (15-25°C), Chilled (0-8°C), Frozen (-18°C or below), Ultra-Frozen (-40°C or below). Used for cold chain compliance and carrier selection.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 110; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule; Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969

description: Mode of transportation used for the inbound shipment leg. Used for freight cost benchmarking, carbon footprint/sustainability reporting, and carrier performance segmentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Transportation Mode; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; EPA Scope 3 Emissions

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the inbound shipment record in the source system. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the lakehouse Silver layer and audit trail requirements.

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the transportation lane record. Primary key for the lane entity.

Reference to the destination network node (e.g., Distribution Center (DC), customer distribution center, retail store, or 3PL facility) to which shipments are delivered on this lane.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Reference to the secondary or backup carrier assigned to this lane, activated when the primary carrier is unavailable or capacity-constrained. Supports supply chain resilience and continuity planning.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Reference to the primary carrier assigned to this transportation lane for standard replenishment and distribution movements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Reference to the origin network node (e.g., manufacturing plant, Distribution Center (DC), supplier facility, or 3PL warehouse) from which shipments depart on this lane.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Estimated CO2 equivalent emissions per unit distance (kg CO2e per mile) for this lane based on freight mode and carrier fleet profile. Supports sustainability reporting and EPA environmental compliance.
reference: EPA Environmental Compliance; ISO 14064; GHG Protocol

description: Date from which the carrier contract and associated freight rates for this lane become effective. Used for rate validity checks during shipment planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Date on which the carrier contract and associated freight rates for this lane expire. Triggers carrier bid management and contract renewal workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Reference number or identifier of the freight contract or carrier rate agreement governing this lane. Links to the carrier contract management system for rate validation and audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Indicates whether this lane crosses an international border, triggering customs documentation, import/export compliance, and regulatory reporting requirements (e.g., FDA Prior Notice for food imports).
reference: FDA FSMA; Codex Alimentarius Commission; EFSA

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all freight cost fields on this lane (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency network operations.
Comma-separated list of days of the week on which deliveries are accepted at the destination node for this lane (e.g., 'Tue,Thu,Sat'). Used for appointment scheduling and receiving window management.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination node's country (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Required for cross-border compliance, import duties, and regulatory reporting.

reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Validity End (EKKO KDATV); Oracle Cloud Procurement — Agreement End Date

description: Food safety certification standard that the 3PL partner is contractually required to maintain at the DC node. SQF = Safe Quality Food; BRC = British Retail Consortium; FSSC 22000 = Food Safety System Certification; ISO 22000 = Food Safety Management System. Supports GFSI compliance and supplier qualification.
reference: GFSI — Benchmarking Requirements; SQF Code; FSSC 22000; ISO 22000; FDA FSMA — Supplier Verification

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern this SLA contract (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Required for multi-national 3PL contracts to determine applicable regulatory and legal frameworks.
reference: ISO 3166-1 — Country Codes; Internal Legal and Compliance Policy

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Compliance Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the 3PL partner is contractually required to maintain a HACCP plan at the DC node. Applicable for food-grade warehousing and cold chain operations. Supports FDA and USDA regulatory compliance.
reference: FDA FSMA — Preventive Controls for Human Food (21 CFR Part 117); USDA FSIS — HACCP Regulations (9 CFR Part 417); Codex Alimentarius — HACCP System

description: Contractually committed inventory record accuracy target expressed as a percentage, measured via cycle counts or physical inventory audits at the DC node. Supports WMS reconciliation and shrinkage management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM — Inventory Management; JDA/Blue Yonder — Inventory Accuracy KPI; Internal 3PL Governance

description: Indicates whether this SLA governs temperature-controlled (cold chain) logistics operations at the DC node. When True, temperature compliance monitoring and FSMA cold chain traceability requirements apply.
reference: FDA FSMA — Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food Rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O); GFSI — Cold Chain Management

description: Numeric target threshold that the 3PL partner must achieve for the specified SLA type. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.5 for 98.5% OTIF) or absolute rate depending on sla_type. This is the contractually committed performance level.
reference: GS1 — OTIF Measurement Standard; Internal 3PL Performance Management Policy

business_glossary_term: Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Unit of Measure
description: Unit of measure for the kpi_target_value and any associated threshold values. Ensures unambiguous interpretation of numeric SLA targets across different SLA types (e.g., percent for OTIF, degrees_celsius for temperature compliance).
reference: ISO 80000-1 — Quantities and Units; GS1 — Unit of Measure Codes

description: Date of the most recent formal compliance or performance audit conducted at the 3PL partner's DC node under this SLA. Used to track audit cadence and identify overdue assessments.
reference: Veeva Vault QM — Audit Management; GFSI — Supplier Audit Requirements; FDA FSMA — Supplier Verification

description: Cadence at which the 3PL partner's performance against this SLA is formally measured and reported. Drives the scheduling of performance scorecards and billing reconciliation cycles.
reference: Internal 3PL Performance Management Policy; JDA/Blue Yonder — Supply Chain Performance Reporting

description: Floor threshold below which the 3PL partner's performance triggers a penalty clause or escalation. Distinct from the target value — represents the contractual breach boundary rather than the aspirational goal.
reference: Internal 3PL Contract Governance Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM — Condition Threshold

description: Scheduled date for the next formal SLA performance review between Food Beverage and the 3PL partner. Derived from review_cadence but stored explicitly to support calendar-driven workflow triggers and procurement planning.
reference: Oracle Cloud Procurement — Supplier Review Schedule; Internal 3PL Governance Framework

description: Contractually committed order accuracy target expressed as a percentage of orders fulfilled without errors (wrong item, wrong quantity, wrong destination). Specific to order_accuracy SLA type. Supports fulfillment quality management.
reference: GS1 — Order Accuracy Metric; Internal 3PL Performance Scorecard

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Target Percentage
description: Contractually committed OTIF delivery performance target expressed as a percentage. Specific to OTIF-type SLAs. Captures the percentage of orders that must be delivered on time and in full per measurement period. Distinct from the generic kpi_target_value to enable direct OTIF reporting.
reference: GS1 — OTIF Measurement Standard; Walmart OTIF Policy; Internal 3PL Performance Scorecard

description: Classification of the financial penalty mechanism applied when the 3PL partner fails to meet the minimum acceptable KPI value. fixed_fee = flat dollar amount; percentage_of_invoice = deduction from billing; credit_note = issued credit; service_credit = applied to future invoices; none = no financial penalty.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI — Vendor Credit Memo; Oracle Cloud Financials — Supplier Deduction; Internal Contract Terms

description: Numeric value of the penalty applied per measurement period when the 3PL partner breaches the minimum acceptable KPI threshold. Interpreted in context of penalty_structure_type (e.g., dollar amount for fixed_fee, percentage for percentage_of_invoice).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI — Condition Value; Oracle Cloud Financials — Deduction Amount; Internal Contract Terms

description: Number of calendar days prior to expiry_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew. Applicable when auto_renewal_flag is True. Drives contract management alerts and procurement workflows.
reference: Oracle Cloud Procurement — Contract Notification; Internal Contract Management Policy

description: Frequency at which formal business reviews of this SLA are conducted between Food Beverage and the 3PL partner. Governs the scheduling of QBRs (Quarterly Business Reviews) and contract renewal discussions.
reference: Internal 3PL Governance Framework; Oracle Cloud Procurement — Supplier Performance Review

description: Contractually mandated inventory rotation method the 3PL partner must apply at the DC node. FIFO = First In First Out; FEFO = First Expired First Out (preferred for perishable food and beverage products); LIFO = Last In First Out; none = no rotation requirement.
reference: FDA FSMA — Preventive Controls for Human Food; GFSI — Inventory Management; Internal Supply Chain Policy

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this SLA record, used in contract documents, performance reviews, and EDI communications with the 3PL partner. Follows the format SLA-[alphanumeric code].
reference: EDI X12 / ANSI ASC X12 — Trading Partner Agreement Identification; Internal Contract Numbering Standard

description: Current lifecycle state of the SLA record. Active = currently in force; suspended = temporarily paused (e.g., force majeure); expired = past contract end date; pending_renewal = under renegotiation; terminated = ended before natural expiry; draft = not yet executed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Status; Oracle Cloud Procurement — Agreement Status

description: Classification of the SLA by the performance dimension it governs. OTIF = On Time In Full delivery; order_accuracy = correctness of order fulfillment; inventory_accuracy = cycle count accuracy; throughput = units processed per period; temperature_compliance = cold chain adherence; damage_rate = product damage incidence.
reference: GFSI — Cold Chain Management Requirements; GS1 — Supply Chain Performance Metrics; Internal 3PL Governance Framework

description: Operational system of record from which this SLA record was ingested into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability across the enterprise integration landscape.
reference: Internal Data Governance — Data Lineage Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle Cloud Procurement; TraceGains

description: Maximum allowable storage or transport temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain SLA compliance. Applicable when is_cold_chain is True. Exceedances trigger penalty clauses and food safety escalation protocols.
reference: FDA FSMA — Sanitary Transportation Rule; EFSA — Cold Chain Requirements; ISO 22000 — Food Safety Management

description: Minimum allowable storage or transport temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain SLA compliance. Applicable when is_cold_chain is True. Used to define the contractual temperature band for chilled or frozen product handling.
reference: FDA FSMA — Sanitary Transportation Rule; EFSA — Cold Chain Requirements; ISO 22000 — Food Safety Management

description: Contractually committed daily throughput capacity target in units (cases or eaches) that the 3PL partner must sustain at the DC node. Applicable to throughput-type SLAs. Supports capacity planning and peak season readiness assessments.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder — Distribution Center Throughput Planning; Internal DC Capacity Model

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this SLA record. Used to detect changes, support incremental data loads, and maintain audit trail integrity in the Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 8601 — Date and Time Format; Internal Data Governance — Audit Trail Standard

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the replenishment order record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete supply chain planning event with a full lifecycle (planned → firmed → released → received).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module — Stock Transport Order / Planned Order

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Replenishment Order Execution Report ties each order to the retailer account that generated demand, enabling OTIF performance tracking and cost allocation.
Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Promotional replenishment orders are created from active campaigns to ensure inventory availability for campaign‑driven sales.
Reference to the logistics carrier or 3PL partner assigned to transport this replenishment order. Used for carrier performance tracking, freight cost allocation, and SLA compliance monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Freight Vendor; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Replenishment Order Execution process requires identifying the target DC to restock; linking ensures accurate inventory allocation and DC‑level reporting.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assign order processing and procurement costs to a cost center for internal budgeting.

Reference to the demand plan or forecast that drove the generation of this replenishment order. Links supply execution back to the demand sensing or S&OP cycle, enabling supply-demand balancing analysis and forecast accuracy measurement.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Reference to the receiving plant, Distribution Center (DC), or 3PL facility where inventory is to be delivered. Drives inbound shipment creation, receiving dock scheduling, and inventory position updates at the destination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Receiving Plant; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Record the financial impact of replenishment orders in the appropriate GL account.

Reference to the inbound shipment or Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) created from this replenishment order upon release. Links the supply order to physical logistics execution for tracking, dock scheduling, and OTIF measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Inbound Delivery (VL31N); EDI 856 ASN

description: Reference to the originating plant, Distribution Center (DC), or 3PL facility from which inventory will be shipped or transferred. Used for network design analysis, inbound logistics planning, and supply-demand balancing across the DC network.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Plant/Storage Location Master; JDA/Blue Yonder Network Design

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Replenishment orders are converted to external purchase orders; linking tracks the conversion and order lifecycle.

Reference to the finished good, raw material, or packaging material being replenished. Links to the material master for product specifications, shelf life parameters, and FIFO/FEFO rotation rules applicable to this order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master (MARA/MARC); FDA 21 CFR Part 117

description: Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Order‑driven replenishment: each replenishment order is generated to satisfy a specific sales order, required for the Replenishment Execution Report.

Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: REQUIRED: Replenishment order processing and regulatory traceability need a direct FK to the SKU; experts expect sku_id on order for cost, shelf‑life, and compliance reporting.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Order fulfillment process tracks which inventory stock position is being replenished, enabling OTIF and inventory aging analysis.

Reference to the supplier or vendor party fulfilling this replenishment order for external procurement types (purchase requisition, purchase order). For internal stock transfers, this references the supplying plant or DC as a party. Maps to PARTY_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master (LFA1); TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: The date on which goods were physically received at the destination location (goods receipt posting date). Used to calculate On Time In Full (OTIF) performance, lead time actuals, and delivery variance against requested and confirmed dates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Receipt Date (MIGO); OTIF KPI

description: The Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) number transmitted by the supplier or source location confirming shipment of this replenishment order. Enables proactive receiving dock scheduling, WMS pre-receipt processing, and shipment tracking. Transmitted via EDI 856.
reference: EDI 856 ASN Standard; GS1 Supply Chain Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM — Inbound Delivery

description: The production batch or lot number associated with the material being replenished. Enables batch-level traceability for food safety recall management, FEFO rotation enforcement, and quality hold management. Populated when the replenishment order is linked to a specific production batch.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117 — FSMA Traceability; ISO 22000; HACCP

description: The delivery date confirmed by the supplier or supplying location. May differ from requested_delivery_date. Used to calculate delivery date variance, assess OTIF risk, and trigger escalation workflows when confirmation is later than requested.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Confirmed Delivery Date; EDI 855

description: The quantity confirmed by the supplier or supplying location as available and committed for fulfillment. May differ from ordered_quantity due to partial availability, capacity constraints, or MOQ adjustments. Used for supply-demand gap analysis and OTIF measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Confirmed Quantity; EDI 855 Purchase Order Acknowledgment

description: Timestamp when this replenishment order record was first captured in the data platform. Distinct from order_date (business event time). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of data freshness. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA MM — Change Document

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit_cost and total_order_value are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and financial consolidation across global operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI — Currency Configuration

description: The expiration or best-before date of the batch being replenished. Used to enforce FEFO rotation policy, calculate shelf_life_days_remaining, and ensure compliance with customer minimum remaining shelf life requirements. Critical for food safety and waste reduction.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117; Codex Alimentarius — Food Labeling; ISO 22000

description: The SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) plan version or S&OP cycle version under which this replenishment order was generated (e.g., 'BASELINE_2024Q3', 'CONSENSUS_V2'). Enables comparison of supply proposals across planning scenarios and IBP consensus cycles.
reference: SAP IBP — Integrated Business Planning; S&OP Process

description: The planned lead time in calendar days from order release to expected goods receipt at the destination location. Derived from the purchasing info record or supply planning parameters. Used for replenishment timing calculations, safety stock parameter setting, and supply planning optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record Lead Time; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization

description: Indicates whether the ordered_quantity meets or exceeds the Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) requirement for this material-supplier combination. True = compliant; False = below MOQ threshold, requiring buyer review or rounding up before release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MOQ Check; SAP IBP Supply Planning

description: The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) applicable to this replenishment order as defined in the purchasing info record or supply planning parameters. Ensures ordered_quantity meets supplier or logistics minimum thresholds. Critical for procurement compliance and cost optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record (EINA/EINE); MOQ Policy

description: The MRP controller code or planner identifier responsible for managing the replenishment of this material at the source plant. Used for workload assignment, exception management, and accountability tracking in supply planning operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MRP Controller (DISPO); MD04 MRP List

description: Free-text field for operational notes, buyer comments, exception explanations, or special handling instructions associated with this replenishment order. Used by supply planners and procurement teams to document non-standard situations, escalations, or supplier communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order Text

description: The business date on which the replenishment order was created or generated by the MRP/supply planning system. Represents the principal real-world event time for this transaction. Used for aging analysis, lead time calculation, and OTIF measurement baseline. Maps to BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — ME21N Order Date; SAP IBP Demand Sensing

description: Externally-known business identifier for the replenishment order as assigned by the ERP or supply planning system (e.g., SAP purchase order number, stock transport order number, or MRP planned order number). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with suppliers and logistics partners. Maps to BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — ME21N / MD04; SAP IBP Supply Planning

description: Current lifecycle state of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from MRP-generated planned order through firming, release to warehouse/supplier, in-transit movement, and final goods receipt. Cancelled indicates the order was voided before fulfillment. Maps to LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Stock Transport Order Status; SAP IBP Supply Planning

description: Classification of the replenishment order by its procurement mechanism. Stock Transfer Order (STO) moves inventory between internal plants or DCs. Purchase Requisition is a request to procure from an external supplier. Planned Order is an MRP-generated supply proposal. Purchase Order is a confirmed external procurement. Intercompany Transfer moves goods between legal entities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stock_transfer_order|purchase_requisition|planned_order|purchase_order|intercompany_transfer — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Order Type Configuration; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: The quantity of material requested in the replenishment order, expressed in the base unit of measure (e.g., cases, pallets, kg). This is the gross supply proposal quantity generated by MRP or supply planning before any MOQ rounding or adjustment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Order Quantity (MENGE); SAP IBP Inventory Optimization

description: The planned date on which goods are to be issued from the source location (outbound shipment departure). Used for outbound logistics scheduling, warehouse pick/pack planning, and lead time calculation between goods issue and goods receipt.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Planned Goods Issue Date (STO); JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Reference to the MRP or supply planning run that generated this replenishment order. Enables traceability of supply proposals back to the specific planning cycle, demand signal, and parameter set used. Supports audit of planning decisions and scenario comparison.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MRP Run (MD01/MD02); SAP IBP Planning Run

description: Business priority assigned to the replenishment order to govern fulfillment sequencing when supply is constrained. Critical = immediate action required (e.g., stockout risk); High = elevated urgency; Medium = standard planning cycle; Low = opportunistic or forward-buy.
reference: SAP IBP Supply Planning — Priority; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether this replenishment order or the associated batch is subject to a quality hold that prevents goods receipt or putaway at the destination. True = quality hold active; False = no hold. Integrates with QM module and HACCP/GMP compliance processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Quality Hold; HACCP; GMP; ISO 22000; GFSI

description: The actual quantity of material received at the destination location upon goods receipt. Used to calculate fill rate, identify short shipments, and trigger OTIF performance measurement against the confirmed quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Receipt (MIGO); OTIF Measurement

description: The date by which the replenishment order is required to arrive at the destination location to meet demand or safety stock targets. Drives supplier scheduling, transportation booking, and OTIF measurement. Set by supply planning based on demand signals and inventory parameters.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Delivery Date; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: The inventory rotation policy applicable to this replenishment order. FIFO (First In First Out) = oldest stock consumed first; FEFO (First Expired First Out) = stock with earliest expiry date consumed first (critical for perishable food and beverage products); LIFO (Last In First Out) = rarely used in food industry.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117 — FSMA; GFSI; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Indicates whether this replenishment order was triggered by a safety stock breach (projected inventory falling below safety stock level) rather than a standard demand-driven replenishment signal. True = safety stock replenishment; False = demand-driven or scheduled replenishment.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization — Safety Stock; SAP S/4HANA MM — MRP Safety Stock

description: The number of days of remaining shelf life on the material at the time of order creation, based on the production date and expiry date of the batch being replenished. Critical for FEFO compliance, quality assurance, and ensuring product arrives at destination with sufficient remaining shelf life to meet customer requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117; ISO 22000; GFSI — Shelf Life Management

description: The total monetary value of the replenishment order calculated as ordered_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Used for procurement spend analysis, inventory investment tracking, and financial planning. Expressed in the order currency. Classified as confidential.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order Value; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The mode of transportation used to move the replenishment order from source to destination location. Drives freight cost estimation, lead time calculation, and carbon footprint reporting for sustainability compliance.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; EPA Environmental Compliance

description: The standard or purchase cost per unit of measure for the material in this replenishment order. Used to calculate total order value, Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) impact, and inventory valuation. Classified as confidential as it reflects procurement pricing strategy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Standard Cost; SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record

description: The base unit of measure in which ordered, confirmed, and received quantities are expressed (e.g., CS=Cases, EA=Each, KG=Kilograms, LB=Pounds, PAL=Pallets, L=Liters, GAL=Gallons). Aligns with the material master UOM configuration in SAP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|PAL|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: GS1 Unit of Measure Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM — Base Unit of Measure (MEINS)

description: Timestamp when this replenishment order record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks changes to order quantity, status, dates, or routing. Used for incremental data processing and audit compliance. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA MM — Change Document

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the safety stock policy record. Primary key for the safety_stock_policy data product in the supply domain.
Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Safety‑stock policies are often set per brand to mitigate demand spikes generated by brand‑specific marketing promotions.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Track safety‑stock holding costs against the cost center responsible for inventory.
Reference to the DC (Distribution Center) or warehouse location where this safety stock policy applies. Supports multi-node DC network and 3PL partner locations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Location; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning Network

description: Reference to the supply planner or demand planner responsible for owning and maintaining this safety stock policy. Supports accountability in the IBP/S&OP cycle and manual override workflows.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; Workday HCM

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Safety‑stock calculations must respect product‑specific regulatory limits (e.g., maximum allowable stock for allergen‑controlled items).
Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) governed by this safety stock policy. Links to the product master to identify the specific finished good or semi-finished item.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 GTIN Standard

description: Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Safety‑stock calculations depend on product specs (shelf‑life, storage) defined in SPECIFICATION; required for inventory planning reports.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Safety‑stock policy execution needs the specific stock position to apply reorder points and safety‑stock quantities per location.

ABC classification of the SKU based on demand volume or revenue contribution. 'A' items are high-value/high-velocity requiring tighter safety stock management; 'C' items are low-velocity with broader buffers. Used to prioritize inventory management effort.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM

description: Date on which this safety stock policy was formally approved by the designated supply chain authority. Part of the S&OP governance and change management audit trail.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 9001

description: Method used to derive the safety stock quantity. 'statistical' uses demand variability and service level targets; 'days_of_supply' uses a fixed days-of-supply multiplier; 'manual' is planner-set; 'min_max' uses min/max replenishment bands; 'demand_driven' uses demand sensing signals from SAP IBP.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; APICS CPIM

description: Free-text description of the business reason for the most recent change to this safety stock policy (e.g., 'Seasonal uplift for Q4 holiday demand', 'Supplier lead time increase', 'Post-launch NPD recalibration'). Required for audit and S&OP governance.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 9001

description: Timestamp when this safety stock policy record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning

description: Number of forward-looking days of demand used in the safety stock calculation. Defines the planning horizon over which demand variability is measured for statistical safety stock methods.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Standard deviation or coefficient of variation of demand used in statistical safety stock calculations. Derived from historical demand sensing data in SAP IBP. Higher values result in larger safety stock buffers.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning; APICS CPIM

description: Date from which this safety stock policy becomes binding and governs replenishment decisions for the SKU-location combination. Aligns with IBP/S&OP planning cycle activation.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 22000 Supply Chain Management

description: Date on which this safety stock policy ceases to be effective. Null indicates an open-ended policy with no planned expiration. Used to manage seasonal or promotional policy windows.

business_glossary_term: SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) Policy ID
description: Source system identifier for this safety stock policy record in SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning). Used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the SAP IBP operational system.

Indicates whether this safety stock policy applies to a new product introduction (NPD/NPI). New products typically use manual or days-of-supply methods due to absence of historical demand data. Triggers enhanced monitoring during launch phase.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; NPD; PLM

description: Indicates whether this policy includes a promotional uplift buffer to support planned trade promotions (TPM/TPO events). When True, additional safety stock is pre-positioned at the DC ahead of promotional periods.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning; TPM

description: Indicates whether this safety stock policy incorporates seasonal demand adjustments. When True, the safety stock quantity and days of supply parameters are adjusted based on seasonal demand patterns from the demand planning system.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

business_glossary_term: JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning Policy ID
description: Source system identifier for this safety stock policy record in JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning. Populated when JDA is the system of record for replenishment planning at the DC network level.

Date on which this safety stock policy was last reviewed by the supply planning team. Supports periodic review cadence governance within the IBP/S&OP cycle. Policies not reviewed within the review cycle may be flagged for recalibration.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Average replenishment lead time in days from order placement to receipt at the DC location. Used in reorder point and statistical safety stock calculations. Sourced from supplier SLA agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SLA

description: Standard deviation of replenishment lead time in days. Used in statistical safety stock calculations to account for supply uncertainty. Higher variability increases the required safety stock buffer.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM

description: Maximum allowable on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location. Defines the upper bound of the min/max replenishment band. Replenishment orders are sized to bring inventory up to this level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Minimum allowable on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location. Defines the lower bound of the min/max replenishment band. Replenishment is triggered when inventory falls to or below this level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Minimum Order Quantity constraint applied to replenishment orders for this SKU-location policy. Sourced from supplier agreements or manufacturing run minimums. Replenishment orders cannot be placed below this quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this safety stock policy. Drives the supply planning review calendar and ensures policies remain calibrated to current demand and supply conditions.

Supply chain planning segment classification for the SKU-location combination. Determines the applicable replenishment and safety stock strategy. 'make_to_stock' is most common for CPG/food and beverage finished goods.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; APICS CPIM

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this safety stock policy record across systems (e.g., SAP IBP, JDA/Blue Yonder). Used for cross-system reconciliation and EDI references.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Human-readable name describing the safety stock policy, typically referencing the SKU, location, or planning segment it governs (e.g., 'RTD Beverage - DC East - Statistical SS').

Current lifecycle status of the safety stock policy. 'active' means the policy is live and governing replenishment; 'draft' means under configuration; 'pending_review' means awaiting S&OP approval; 'suspended' means temporarily disabled.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Version number of the safety stock policy record, incremented each time the policy parameters are updated. Supports policy change history tracking and audit trail requirements within the supply planning governance framework.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Minimum acceptable remaining shelf life percentage at time of receipt or shipment from the DC. Ensures safety stock inventory meets customer and retailer freshness requirements. Expressed as a percentage of total shelf life (e.g., 66.67 means at least 2/3 of shelf life must remain).
reference: FDA FSMA; GFSI; ISO 22000; Codex Alimentarius; GMP

description: Inventory level at which a replenishment order is triggered for the SKU at the specified location. Calculated as safety stock plus expected demand during replenishment lead time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM

description: Standard replenishment order quantity triggered when the reorder point is reached. May be constrained by MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) or pallet/layer rounding rules from the supplier or DC network.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization

description: Frequency in days at which inventory position is reviewed and replenishment decisions are made. Relevant for periodic review systems. Impacts the safety stock calculation for periodic review models.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Inventory rotation rule applied at the DC location for this SKU. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for ambient products; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is mandatory for perishable, chilled, and frozen food and beverage products to minimize waste and ensure food safety compliance.
reference: FDA FSMA; GFSI; ISO 22000; HACCP; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Safety stock expressed as number of days of forward demand coverage. Used when calculation_method is 'days_of_supply'. Provides a demand-relative buffer measure aligned with FIFO/FEFO rotation policies.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Statistical safety factor (z-score) corresponding to the target service level. Applied to demand and lead time variability to compute the safety stock quantity in statistical calculation methods. Derived from the normal distribution at the specified service level target.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM

description: The calculated or manually set safety stock quantity (in base unit of measure) that must be maintained as a buffer to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty. Principal quantitative fact of this policy record.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM

description: Target service level (cycle service level or fill rate) expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.5) used in statistical safety stock calculations. Drives the safety factor (z-score) applied to demand variability. Directly impacts OTIF performance.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM; ISO 22000

description: Total shelf life of the SKU in days from manufacture date. Used in FEFO rotation policy enforcement and safety stock sizing to ensure buffer inventory does not expire before consumption. Critical for perishable food and beverage products.
reference: FDA FSMA; USDA; ISO 22000; GFSI; Codex Alimentarius

description: Base unit of measure in which safety stock quantities, reorder points, and replenishment quantities are expressed (e.g., CS for case, EA for each, KG for kilogram). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA material master UOM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 Unit of Measure Standard; ISO 80000

description: Timestamp when this safety stock policy record was most recently modified. Used to detect changes during incremental Silver layer processing and to support S&OP change management audit trails.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning

description: XYZ classification of the SKU based on demand variability. 'X' items have stable, predictable demand; 'Y' items have moderate variability; 'Z' items have highly erratic demand. Combined with ABC classification to set differentiated safety stock policies.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning; APICS CPIM

description: System-generated unique identifier for the allocation plan record.
Identifier of the customer receiving the allocated quantity.
User identifier of the person who approved the allocation plan.
Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Allocation plans may reserve inventory for upcoming promotions; linking supports the Promotion‑Driven Allocation report.
Identifier of the SKU to which the allocation applies.
Self-referencing FK on allocation_plan (superseded_allocation_plan_id)

business_glossary_term: Allocation Plan Supply Plan Version Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to supply.plan_version. Business justification: Allocation plan is versioned; linking to the supply plan version provides hierarchy and removes redundant version fields.
Quantity of the SKU that is allocated to the customer for the plan horizon.
Number of days the allocation plan covers from its effective start date.
Specific algorithm or rule used to compute the allocation (e.g., fair share, priority).
Current lifecycle status of the allocation plan.
Unit of measure for allocated and demanded quantities.
Business driver for the allocation (e.g., shortage, new product launch).
Timestamp when the allocation plan was approved.
Sales or distribution channel for which the allocation is defined.
Indicates whether the allocation is driven by a contractual commitment.
Timestamp when the allocation plan record was created in the system.
Classification of demand intensity for the SKU‑customer pair.
Customer‑projected demand quantity for the SKU during the allocation horizon.
Date on which the allocation plan becomes effective for execution.
Date on which the allocation plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended plans.
Origin of the demand forecast used for the allocation (e.g., statistical, consensus).
Indicates whether the plan is locked from further edits.
Free‑form comments or rationale for the allocation decisions.
Classification of the plan based on the allocation strategy applied.
Numeric priority used when the method is priority‑based; lower numbers indicate higher priority.
Three‑letter code representing the geographic region or market.
Signal indicating if replenishment is triggered by this allocation.
Weight factor based on the customer's revenue contribution, used in revenue‑weighted allocations.
Indicates whether safety stock considerations are applied in the allocation.
Name of the source system that originated the allocation plan record.
Business tier classification of the customer for allocation weighting.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation plan record.

Unique surrogate identifier for each capacity constraint record in the supply planning system. Primary key for the capacity_constraint data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a managed planning resource (capacity bucket) with its own identity and lifecycle.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or warehouse node to which this capacity constraint applies. Null when the constraint is associated with a manufacturing plant rather than a DC. Links to the DC master in the supply network.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocate capacity constraint impacts (e.g., overtime) to the relevant cost center for cost control.

Name or user identifier of the supply planner, capacity manager, or S&OP analyst who last reviewed and validated this capacity constraint record. Provides accountability and audit trail for planning decisions.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder; Internal Governance

description: Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility to which this capacity constraint applies. Null when the constraint is associated with a DC or logistics node rather than a production plant. Links to the plant master in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; SAP IBP Supply Planning

description: Reference to the specific production line or work center within a plant to which this capacity constraint applies. Populated only when resource_type is 'production_line'. Links to the work center or production line master in SAP S/4HANA PP or Aveva MES.

business_glossary_term: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle ID
description: Reference to the S&OP/IBP planning cycle during which this capacity constraint was defined or last reviewed. Links to the S&OP cycle master to enable traceability of capacity decisions across monthly planning cycles.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; S&OP Best Practices

description: Reference to the transportation lane (origin-destination pair) to which this capacity constraint applies. Populated only when resource_type is 'transportation_lane'. Links to the lane master in JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Total available capacity for the resource within the defined capacity bucket period, expressed in the unit of measure specified by capacity_uom (e.g., cases per day, pallet positions, labor hours, truck loads). Represents the theoretical maximum throughput or storage before any utilization. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; SAP S/4HANA PP-CRP

description: Indicates whether this resource is identified as a bottleneck in the current planning cycle (True = bottleneck, False = not a bottleneck). Set by the planning engine (SAP IBP or JDA) when utilization_pct exceeds utilization_ceiling_pct. Triggers network rebalancing, co-packing evaluation, or 3PL overflow decisions.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: The last calendar date of the capacity bucket period to which this constraint record applies. Combined with bucket_start_date, defines the exact planning window for available and utilized capacity values.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 8601

description: The first calendar date of the capacity bucket period to which this constraint record applies. Combined with bucket_end_date, defines the exact planning window for available and utilized capacity values.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 8601

description: Time granularity of the capacity constraint record. Defines the planning horizon bucket over which available and utilized capacity are measured. 'daily' is used for short-term finite scheduling; 'weekly' for S&OP/IBP mid-term planning; 'monthly' and 'quarterly' for strategic network design.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Unit of measure in which available_capacity and utilized_capacity are expressed. Varies by resource_type: production lines typically use cases or kg per day; DC storage uses pallet positions; labor uses hours; transportation uses truck loads or pallets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cases|pallets|labor_hours|truck_loads|kg|liters|units — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM UOM; ISO 80000; GS1 Standards

description: Hours allocated within the capacity bucket for product changeovers (SKU switches) on the production line, including allergen cleaning, flavor changeovers, and packaging format changes. Reduces effective throughput capacity. Tracked separately from planned_downtime_hours for root cause analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Aveva/Wonderware MES; HACCP Allergen Management

description: Business category classifying the root cause or nature of the capacity constraint. 'throughput' relates to production or logistics flow rate; 'storage' to physical space; 'labor' to workforce availability; 'equipment' to machinery or vehicle availability; 'regulatory' to compliance-driven limits (e.g., FDA GMP, FSMA); 'seasonal' to demand-driven temporary constraints.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder; FDA FSMA; GMP

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the capacity constraint record, used in SAP IBP and JDA/Blue Yonder planning runs to reference this constraint. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the capacity constraint (e.g., 'Line 3 Daily Throughput Cap', 'DC Chicago Pallet Positions'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this capacity constraint record originated. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation across planning systems. 'sap_ibp' = SAP Integrated Business Planning; 'jda_blue_yonder' = JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; 'aveva_mes' = Aveva/Wonderware MES; 'manual' = planner-entered; 'erp' = SAP S/4HANA or Oracle Cloud ERP.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder; Aveva/Wonderware MES; SAP S/4HANA

description: Current lifecycle status of the capacity constraint record. 'active' means the constraint is live and enforced in planning runs; 'inactive' means it has been retired or superseded; 'pending' means it is defined but not yet effective; 'suspended' means temporarily bypassed. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether the capacity constraint is a hard or soft limit in supply planning. 'hard' constraints cannot be violated by the planning engine (e.g., physical line speed, dock door count); 'soft' constraints can be overridden with a penalty cost or planner approval (e.g., preferred utilization ceiling, labor overtime threshold).
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Timestamp when this capacity constraint record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 8601; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Governance

description: Number of dock doors available at the DC for inbound and/or outbound operations within the capacity bucket. DC-specific metric used to plan ASN (Advanced Shipping Notice) scheduling, carrier appointments, and DSD (Direct Store Delivery) operations. Null when resource_type is not 'dock_door' or 'dc_throughput'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; ASN Standards

description: Number of dock doors actually scheduled or utilized within the capacity bucket period. Used alongside dock_doors_available to compute dock door utilization rate and identify scheduling bottlenecks at the DC.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Net usable capacity after applying OEE, planned downtime, changeover time, and other efficiency factors to the theoretical available_capacity. Represents the realistic throughput ceiling used in constrained supply planning runs. Expressed in capacity_uom.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; SAP S/4HANA PP-CRP; Aveva/Wonderware MES

description: Date after which this capacity constraint record is no longer active and is excluded from planning runs. Null for open-ended constraints. Used to manage time-bound constraints such as seasonal capacity limits or temporary equipment outages.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; ISO 8601

description: Date from which this capacity constraint record becomes effective and is enforced in planning runs. Enables forward-looking constraint management and scenario planning for known future capacity changes (e.g., planned line shutdowns, DC expansions).
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; ISO 8601

description: Total labor hours available for production or DC operations within the capacity bucket, accounting for scheduled shifts, headcount, and planned absences. Used in labor capacity planning and S&OP cycles. Null when resource_type is not 'labor'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Workday HCM; SAP IBP

description: Actual or planned labor hours consumed within the capacity bucket period. Used to assess workforce loading against available labor hours and identify overtime or underutilization scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Workday HCM; SAP IBP

description: Date on which this capacity constraint record was last reviewed and validated by a supply planner or capacity manager. Used to ensure constraint parameters remain current and aligned with operational reality across S&OP cycles.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; S&OP Best Practices

description: Target OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) percentage for the production line or equipment resource within this capacity bucket. OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality. Used to set realistic effective capacity expectations in finite scheduling and SAP IBP planning runs. Applicable only when resource_type is 'production_line'.
reference: Aveva/Wonderware MES Performance Analytics; OEE Industry Standard; SAP IBP

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Risk Flag
description: Indicates whether this capacity constraint poses a risk to OTIF (On Time In Full) delivery performance (True = OTIF risk present). Set when the constraint is projected to cause order fulfillment shortfalls within the bucket period. Used to prioritize constraint resolution and escalation.
reference: SAP IBP Supply Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; OTIF Industry Standard

description: Recommended or approved action to resolve a capacity overload when bottleneck_flag is True. 'co_pack' triggers co-packing or toll manufacturing evaluation; '3pl_overflow' routes excess volume to a 3PL partner; 'demand_defer' defers lower-priority demand; 'interplant_transfer' reallocates production to another plant; 'none' means no action required.
reference: SAP IBP Supply Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Total number of pallet storage positions available at the DC or warehouse for this capacity bucket. DC-specific metric used in warehouse capacity management and network design. Null when resource_type is not 'storage' or 'dc_throughput'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Warehouse Management; WMS Standards

description: Hours of scheduled downtime within the capacity bucket for maintenance, sanitation (GMP/HACCP compliance), changeovers, or regulatory inspections. Reduces effective_capacity from available_capacity. Critical for food and beverage manufacturing where sanitation windows are mandated by GMP and FSMA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Aveva/Wonderware MES; FDA FSMA; GMP; HACCP

description: Free-text field for supply planners to document context, assumptions, or exceptions related to this capacity constraint (e.g., 'Line 3 down for allergen deep clean week of 2024-03-11', 'DC Chicago dock expansion in progress'). Supports S&OP cycle documentation and audit trail.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder; S&OP Best Practices

description: Identifier for the SAP IBP or JDA planning version or scenario to which this capacity constraint record belongs (e.g., 'BASELINE', 'SCENARIO_A', 'CONSENSUS'). Enables multi-scenario capacity analysis and S&OP cycle management.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether this capacity constraint is driven by a regulatory hold or compliance requirement (True = regulatory-driven constraint). Examples include FDA FSMA inspection holds, USDA audit shutdowns, or GMP sanitation mandates that reduce available production capacity.
reference: FDA FSMA; USDA; GMP; HACCP; GFSI

description: Categorizes the type of supply chain resource to which this capacity constraint applies. Drives how the constraint is interpreted in finite capacity planning: production_line (manufacturing throughput), dc_throughput (Distribution Center inbound/outbound volume), transportation_lane (carrier/lane capacity), dock_door (DC dock door availability), labor (workforce hours), storage (pallet positions or cubic volume). Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Quantity of safety stock that cannot be replenished due to this capacity constraint within the bucket period, expressed in capacity_uom. Used in inventory optimization to quantify the risk exposure created by constrained capacity and inform safety stock parameter adjustments in SAP IBP.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Timestamp when this capacity constraint record was last modified. Used to detect changes between planning cycles and support incremental Silver layer processing and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 8601; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Governance

description: Maximum acceptable utilization percentage for this resource before the constraint is flagged as a bottleneck or overload. Typically set below 100% to preserve buffer capacity (e.g., 85% for production lines to allow for changeovers and maintenance, 90% for DC throughput). Used in SAP IBP and JDA planning to trigger alerts and rebalancing.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; OEE Standards

description: Ratio of utilized_capacity to available_capacity expressed as a percentage (0–100). Calculated field stored for planning visibility: utilization_pct = (utilized_capacity / available_capacity) * 100. Used to identify bottleneck resources and trigger network rebalancing decisions. Values above the utilization_ceiling_pct indicate an overload condition.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) Standards

description: Actual or planned capacity consumed within the bucket period, expressed in the same unit of measure as available_capacity (capacity_uom). Used to compute utilization rate and identify bottlenecks. In constrained planning runs, this reflects the sum of all planned orders or shipments assigned to this resource.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; SAP S/4HANA PP-CRP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each point-in-time supply-demand balance record produced during an integrated business planning (IBP) balancing run. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete planning event with a clear lifecycle at SKU/location/week granularity.
Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or planning location node at which the supply-demand balance is evaluated. Supports multi-node network design and 3PL partner visibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Report supply‑demand balance metrics per cost center for internal performance dashboards.
Reference to the specific planning version (e.g., baseline, consensus, simulation) under which this balance record was generated. Enables version comparison and what-if scenario analysis.
Reference to the specific Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which the supply-demand balance is calculated. The SKU is the primary product planning unit in the food and beverage supply chain.

business_glossary_term: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle ID
description: Reference to the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) / Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle during which this balance record was produced. Links the balance snapshot to the formal monthly S&OP governance process.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Supply‑Demand Balance report requires linking each balance record to the actual stock position to compute days‑of‑supply and safety‑stock calculations.

Externally-known business identifier for this supply-demand balance record, formatted as SDB-YYYY-WW-NNNNNN. Used by supply planners to reference specific balance snapshots in workbench communications and exception resolution workflows.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Reference to the IBP/S&OP planning run that generated this supply-demand balance record. Links the balance snapshot to its originating planning execution cycle.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the IBP/S&OP balancing run that produced this record was executed. Used to sequence snapshots and identify the most current balance position.

Current lifecycle state of the supply-demand balance record. 'open' indicates an active unresolved balance; 'shortage' and 'excess' indicate exception states requiring planner action; 'balanced' indicates supply meets demand within tolerance; 'exception' indicates a system-flagged anomaly; 'closed' indicates the planning period has passed.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Sales channel for which the demand component of this balance record applies. Enables channel-specific supply-demand balancing, particularly for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) versus warehouse-distributed channels in food and beverage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Total projected supply quantity available for the SKU at the planning location for the planning bucket after applying capacity, material, and logistics constraints. Represents the supply plan output from SAP IBP or JDA supply planning.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Audit timestamp recording when this supply-demand balance record was first persisted in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, SLA tracking, and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 22000 Food Safety Management Systems; FSMA Food Safety Modernization Act

description: Number of days the projected available balance quantity can cover forward demand at the current demand rate. A key supply health metric used to identify shortage risk and excess inventory positions. Calculated as PAB divided by average daily demand.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Planner-assigned or system-calculated priority level for resolving the supply-demand imbalance. Used to sequence workbench exception resolution activities. 'critical' indicates immediate action required; 'none' indicates no exception.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Current resolution status of any supply-demand exception associated with this balance record. Tracks the planner workflow from initial exception identification through resolution or escalation.

Projected fill rate percentage for the planning bucket, calculated as constrained supply divided by unconstrained demand. A key On Time In Full (OTIF) performance indicator used to assess service level risk and customer order fulfillment capability.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Quantity of confirmed, firm customer orders or distribution orders already committed for the SKU at the planning location within the planning bucket. Represents the hard demand component within the planning time fence.

Indicates whether this supply-demand balance record has been flagged as an excess inventory exception, meaning projected available balance exceeds the maximum inventory target. Drives disposition and markdown decisions.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether the planning bucket falls within the frozen planning horizon, during which supply plan changes are restricted. Frozen periods reflect firm manufacturing schedules and committed supplier orders that cannot be altered without significant cost.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Indicates whether this supply-demand balance record has been flagged as a shortage exception, meaning projected available balance falls below the safety stock threshold. Drives exception-based workbench prioritization for supply planners.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) constraint applicable to the SKU at the planning location. Replenishment signals must meet or exceed this threshold. Sourced from supplier agreements or manufacturing run parameters.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Projected on-hand inventory quantity at the start of the planning bucket for the SKU at the planning location. Serves as the beginning balance for the supply-demand calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization

description: Total quantity of planned inbound supply receipts (purchase orders, production orders, transfer orders) scheduled to arrive at the planning location during the planning bucket. Feeds into the constrained supply calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM PP; SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Free-text notes entered by the supply planner documenting exception resolution actions, assumptions, escalation rationale, or override justifications for this supply-demand balance record. Supports audit trail and knowledge transfer.
The last calendar date of the weekly planning bucket for which this supply-demand balance record applies. Together with planning_bucket_start_date defines the exact planning week.
The first calendar date of the weekly planning bucket (time fence) for which this supply-demand balance record applies. Defines the start of the SKU/location/week granularity period.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; Codex Alimentarius Commission

description: Number of weeks forward from the balance run date that this supply-demand balance record covers within the planning horizon. Contextualizes the balance record within the short-term (frozen), mid-term, or long-term planning horizon.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Projected Available Balance (PAB) quantity at the end of the planning bucket, calculated as opening inventory plus constrained supply minus unconstrained demand. The primary output metric used by supply planners to assess inventory health and identify imbalances.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Unit of measure applied to all quantity fields in this supply-demand balance record (e.g., CS=Cases, EA=Each, LB=Pounds, KG=Kilograms). Ensures consistent interpretation of supply and demand quantities across SKUs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|LB|KG|OZ|GAL|L|MT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Codex Alimentarius Commission; ISO 22000

description: Estimated remaining shelf life in days for the projected on-hand inventory at the planning location as of the planning bucket start date. Used to identify at-risk inventory that may expire before it can be consumed, supporting FEFO-based exception management.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); GFSI; Codex Alimentarius Commission

description: Inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered for the SKU at the planning location. Used in conjunction with safety stock and lead time parameters to drive replenishment signals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization

description: Quantity recommended by the planning system to be replenished (via production, procurement, or transfer) to resolve the supply-demand gap. Subject to Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) and lot-size constraints.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Type of replenishment signal generated by the planning system to address a supply shortfall for this SKU/location/week. Indicates whether the system recommends a planned production order, purchase requisition, stock transfer order, or manual planner intervention.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM PP; SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Inventory rotation policy applied to this SKU at the planning location. FIFO (First In First Out) and FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard food and beverage policies to minimize waste and ensure product freshness compliance. Drives warehouse management and replenishment sequencing.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); GFSI; ISO 22000; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Target safety stock quantity for the SKU at the planning location for the planning bucket, as defined in the inventory optimization parameters. Used to evaluate whether the projected available balance meets the minimum buffer requirement.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Total shelf life in days for the SKU as defined in the product specification. Used in FEFO rotation policy calculations and to assess whether projected available balance will expire before consumption, triggering waste risk flags.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); Codex Alimentarius Commission; ISO 22000

description: Operational system of record from which this supply-demand balance record originated. Enables data lineage tracing and supports multi-system integration scenarios where both SAP IBP and JDA/Blue Yonder may contribute planning data.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Net quantity difference between constrained supply and unconstrained demand for the planning bucket. A positive value indicates excess supply; a negative value indicates a shortage. The primary exception-driving metric for supply planner workbench prioritization.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Classification of the supply strategy for this SKU at the planning location. Determines how supply is sourced: make-to-stock (MTS) from own manufacturing, make-to-order (MTO), co-packing (Co-Pack), toll manufacturing, or buy-resell. Influences replenishment lead times and MOQ parameters.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; SAP IBP; Aveva/Wonderware MES

description: Target customer service level percentage (Service Level Agreement) for the SKU at the planning location, used as the benchmark against which fill_rate_pct is evaluated. Drives safety stock parameter calculations in SAP IBP inventory optimization.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Total projected demand quantity for the SKU at the planning location for the planning bucket, before applying any supply constraints. Represents the market-facing demand signal from the consensus forecast or demand sensing output in SAP IBP.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Audit timestamp recording when this supply-demand balance record was last modified, such as after a planner override, exception resolution, or re-balancing run. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 22000 Food Safety Management Systems; FSMA Food Safety Modernization Act

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each demand plan record at the SKU/DC/time-period granularity. Primary key for the demand_plan data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning Object Model

description: Reference to the customer or channel account for which the demand plan is generated, when the plan is customer-specific (e.g., key account demand plan). Nullable for aggregate/market-level plans.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

description: Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or warehouse location for which the demand plan is generated. Supports network-level demand disaggregation across the DC network and 3PL partners.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Location; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning Network

description: Name or user identifier of the business stakeholder (e.g., Demand Planning Manager, VP Supply Chain) who approved the consensus forecast during the S&OP cycle. Supports governance and audit trail for plan approval workflows.
reference: SAP IBP S&OP Workflow; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning Approval

description: Reference to the specific planning version or scenario under which this demand plan record was generated (e.g., baseline, consensus, adjusted). Supports multi-version S&OP cycle management.
Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Demand planning incorporates product registration status to avoid forecasting for items pending regulatory approval.
Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Demand Planning incorporates promotion plans to adjust forecasted demand; linking enables the Demand Impact Forecast report.
Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Demand forecasting uses qualified sales opportunities as drivers; the Opportunity‑Based Forecasting report requires this link.
Reference to the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which the demand plan is generated. The SKU is the lowest-level product identifier used in demand planning and inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 GTIN Standard

business_glossary_term: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle ID
description: Reference to the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) / Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle in which this demand plan record was generated or last updated. Enables traceability of demand plan versions to specific planning cycles.

Demand forecast quantity after planner-applied manual adjustments, overrides, or market intelligence inputs on top of the statistical baseline. Reflects demand planner judgment before consensus alignment.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Date and time when the demand plan record was formally approved during the S&OP consensus cycle. Supports audit trail, SLA compliance tracking, and planning cycle performance measurement.
reference: SAP IBP S&OP Cycle; ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: Underlying demand forecast quantity representing the expected sell-through volume excluding any promotional uplift, new product launch effects, or extraordinary events. Used as the reference point for uplift/downlift factor calculation.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Baseline Demand

description: Trade channel for which this demand plan is generated (e.g., retail, foodservice, direct-to-consumer, e-commerce). Supports channel-level demand disaggregation and channel-specific supply planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|foodservice|direct_to_consumer|ecommerce|club|convenience|drug|mass — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Distribution Channel; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Final agreed demand volume (in selling units) resulting from the cross-functional S&OP consensus process, incorporating inputs from Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Supply Chain. This is the primary input to supply planning and inventory optimization.
reference: SAP IBP S&OP Consensus; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Date and time when this demand plan record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard; Databricks Delta Lake Audit

description: Confirmed customer purchase order quantity for the SKU/DC/period, sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD or EDI order feeds. Represents committed demand and is used to override or supplement statistical forecasts in the near-term horizon.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Order; EDI 850 Purchase Order Standard
