description: Classification of the demand type for supply planning purposes. 'regular' = baseline replenishment demand; 'promotional' = trade promotion-driven demand; 'new_product' = NPD launch demand; 'phase_out' = end-of-life demand; 'safety_stock' = buffer stock demand.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Classification

description: Near-term demand signal quantity derived from high-frequency data inputs including Point of Sale (POS) data, customer orders, and syndicated data (Nielsen/IRI). Used for daily/weekly supply execution and short-horizon replenishment decisions.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; Nielsen/IRI Syndicated Data

description: The fiscal period (month or quarter number) within the fiscal year to which this demand plan record belongs. Supports period-over-period demand trend analysis and S&OP cycle alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Period; SAP IBP Planning Calendar

description: The fiscal year to which this demand plan record belongs, as defined by the company's financial calendar. Used for annual budgeting, S&OP alignment, and year-over-year demand comparison.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant; SAP IBP Planning Calendar

description: Statistical or machine learning algorithm used to generate the baseline statistical forecast for this SKU/period. Used for model governance, accuracy benchmarking, and algorithm selection optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exponential_smoothing|arima|moving_average|machine_learning|croston|holt_winters|causal — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP Statistical Forecasting Engine; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Statistical confidence level (expressed as a percentage, 0–100) associated with the demand forecast, indicating the reliability of the forecast model output. Used to flag low-confidence forecasts for planner review and safety stock buffer sizing.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Forecast Accuracy

business_glossary_term: Forecast Error Percentage (Mean Absolute Percentage Error)
description: Historical Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the forecasting model for this SKU/DC combination, expressed as a percentage. Used to assess forecast model accuracy, trigger model recalibration, and size safety stock buffers.
reference: SAP IBP Forecast Accuracy KPI; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning Analytics

description: Lower confidence interval bound for the demand forecast quantity, representing the low-end demand scenario. Used for inventory optimization, downside risk assessment, and supply-demand balancing.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Probabilistic Forecasting

description: Unit of measure in which all forecast quantity fields are expressed (e.g., CS = cases, EA = each, LB = pounds, KG = kilograms). Aligns with the selling unit of measure defined in the SAP S/4HANA material master. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|LB|KG|OZ|GAL|L|MT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; GS1 Unit of Measure Standard

description: Upper confidence interval bound for the demand forecast quantity, representing the high-end demand scenario. Used for safety stock calculation, capacity planning, and supply risk management.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Probabilistic Forecasting

description: Indicates whether the demand plan record is frozen (locked) for supply execution and cannot be modified by planners. Frozen plans are used as firm inputs to production scheduling and procurement within the planning fence horizon.
reference: SAP IBP Planning Fence; JDA/Blue Yonder Frozen Horizon

description: Indicates whether this demand plan record is for a New Product Development (NPD) / new SKU launch, where no historical sales data exists for statistical forecasting. Triggers use of analogous product forecasting or market-based estimation methods.
reference: SAP IBP New Product Introduction; JDA/Blue Yonder NPD Forecasting

description: Externally-known business identifier for the demand plan record, formatted as DP-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN. Used for cross-system traceability between SAP IBP, JDA/Blue Yonder, and ERP systems.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: The last calendar date of the planning time bucket to which this demand plan record applies. Together with plan_period_start_date, defines the demand planning horizon window.
reference: SAP IBP Time Profile; ISO 8601 Date Standard

description: The first calendar date of the planning time bucket (day, week, or month) to which this demand plan record applies. Used to align demand signals with supply planning and inventory replenishment cycles.
reference: SAP IBP Time Profile; ISO 8601 Date Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the demand plan record within the S&OP consensus cycle. 'statistical' = system-generated baseline; 'adjusted' = planner-modified; 'consensus' = cross-functional agreed; 'approved' = executive-signed-off; 'frozen' = locked for supply execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|statistical|adjusted|consensus|approved|frozen|superseded — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP S&OP Cycle; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning Workflow

description: Classification of the demand plan by its generation method and purpose. 'statistical' = algorithm-generated baseline; 'consensus' = S&OP agreed volume; 'customer_order' = customer-committed orders; 'demand_sensing' = near-term POS/syndicated signal; 'promotional' = uplift-adjusted plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: statistical|consensus|customer_order|demand_sensing|promotional — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Free-text commentary entered by the demand planner to document the rationale for manual forecast adjustments, market intelligence inputs, or exceptions. Supports audit trail and knowledge transfer within the S&OP process.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Time granularity of the demand plan bucket. 'daily' and 'weekly' are used for near-term demand sensing and supply execution; 'monthly' is used for S&OP and IBP cycles; 'quarterly' for long-range planning.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Actual Point of Sale (POS) sell-through quantity for the SKU during the plan period, sourced from retailer POS data feeds. Used as a demand sensing input signal to update near-term forecasts and detect demand shifts.
reference: Nielsen/IRI Syndicated Data; SAP IBP Demand Sensing POS Integration

description: Incremental demand volume (in selling units) attributed to planned trade promotions, marketing campaigns, or temporary price reductions above the baseline forecast. Sourced from Trade Promotion Management (TPM) systems and applied as an uplift factor.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder TPO Integration

description: Seasonal index factor for the SKU/period combination, representing the ratio of period demand to average annual demand. Values >1.0 indicate above-average seasonal demand; <1.0 indicate below-average. Used in statistical forecasting model decomposition.
reference: SAP IBP Statistical Forecasting; JDA/Blue Yonder Seasonal Profile

description: Number of days into the future covered by the demand sensing signal for this plan record. Typically 1–14 days for near-term POS-driven demand sensing. Distinguishes short-horizon sensing from longer-horizon statistical forecasts.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Sensing

description: Originating system from which this demand plan record was generated or ingested. Used for data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder; Nielsen/IRI Syndicated Data

description: System-generated baseline demand forecast quantity (in selling units) produced by the statistical forecasting algorithm (e.g., exponential smoothing, ARIMA, machine learning) in SAP IBP or JDA/Blue Yonder, prior to any manual adjustment or consensus override.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Statistical Forecasting

description: Market-level demand quantity derived from syndicated data sources (Nielsen/IRI market tracking or SPINS natural channel data) for the SKU/period. Used to calibrate statistical forecasts against total market consumption trends.
reference: Nielsen/IRI Syndicated Data; SPINS Specialty Product Intelligence

description: Date and time when this demand plan record was last modified, including planner adjustments, consensus overrides, or system recalculations. Used for change tracking and incremental Silver Layer processing.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard; Databricks Delta Lake Audit

description: Multiplicative factor applied to the baseline forecast to account for demand uplift (>1.0) or downlift (<1.0) from events such as trade promotions, new product launches, distribution gains/losses, or market disruptions. A value of 1.0 indicates no adjustment.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder TPO Integration

description: Business reason code explaining the cause of the uplift or downlift factor applied to the baseline forecast. Used for demand decomposition analysis and post-event forecast accuracy review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promotion|new_product_launch|distribution_gain|distribution_loss|seasonality|event|competitor_action|price_change|none — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the IBP planning scenario or simulation version. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a planning scenario artifact with its own identity and lifecycle within the SAP IBP S&OP process.
Reference to the supply chain planner or analyst who created or owns this scenario within the IBP planning process.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); Workday HCM

business_glossary_term: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle ID
description: Reference to the S&OP/IBP planning cycle this scenario belongs to, linking the scenario to its parent consensus planning cycle.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: Native scenario identifier from the originating system of record (e.g., SAP IBP scenario key or JDA planning version ID). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the upstream planning system.
Date on which this scenario was formally approved within the S&OP/IBP governance process. Enables tracking of approval cycle times and scenario readiness for consensus planning meetings.
Name or user ID of the supply chain leader or S&OP process owner who approved this scenario for use in consensus planning. Provides audit trail for scenario governance.

Indicates whether co-packing (contract packaging) capacity is included in the supply assumptions for this scenario. True = co-packing capacity is modeled as an available supply source, relevant for NPD launches and capacity overflow scenarios.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (PP)

description: Timestamp when this scenario record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for scenario lifecycle management and S&OP cycle governance.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 (Quality Management Systems)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for all monetary values in this scenario (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial comparisons across multi-currency global F&B operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; Oracle Cloud ERP (Financials)

description: Number of Distribution Centers (DCs) included in this scenario's network design. Applicable primarily to network_design scenario type. Enables comparison of network configurations with different DC footprints.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder (Transportation Management); SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning)

description: Total assumed throughput capacity of the DC network in this scenario, expressed in dc_network_throughput_uom. Used in network design scenarios to evaluate whether the proposed DC configuration can handle projected demand volumes.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder (Transportation Management); SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning)

business_glossary_term: Distribution Center (DC) Network Throughput Unit of Measure
description: Unit of measure used to express DC network throughput assumptions in this scenario (e.g., cases, pallets, lbs). Standardizes capacity comparisons across network design scenario variants.

Source or method used to derive the demand input for this scenario. Determines whether the scenario uses the statistical baseline, consensus-adjusted forecast, customer committed orders, external market intelligence, or a custom demand override.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Demand Planning)

description: Percentage adjustment applied to the base demand forecast for this scenario. Positive values represent upside demand assumptions (e.g., +15% for promotional event); negative values represent downside risk (e.g., -10% for demand softening). Used in upside/downside scenario modeling.

Freight cost modeling approach applied in this scenario for inbound and outbound logistics cost estimation. Actual_rates uses current carrier contracts; spot_market uses prevailing spot rates; blended uses a weighted mix; custom uses scenario-specific rate assumptions.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder (Transportation Management); Oracle Cloud ERP (Supply Chain)

description: Inventory rotation policy applied within this scenario for perishable and shelf-life-sensitive F&B products. FIFO (First In First Out) and FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard in food manufacturing to minimize waste and ensure food safety compliance.
reference: FDA (Food and Drug Administration) - FSMA; ISO 22000; SAP S/4HANA (WM)

description: Indicates whether this scenario has been designated as the official consensus plan for the S&OP cycle. Only one scenario per S&OP cycle should be flagged True. Drives downstream supply execution and financial commitment.

Indicates whether this scenario is locked from further modification. True = scenario is frozen for audit and comparison purposes; no further edits are permitted. Supports version-controlled planning governance.
Timestamp when this scenario record was most recently updated. Enables change detection, incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer, and audit trail for scenario revisions.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 (Quality Management Systems)

description: Indicates whether this scenario overrides standard MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) constraints for procurement and production orders. True = MOQ constraints relaxed for scenario modeling purposes (e.g., testing unconstrained supply response).
reference: SAP S/4HANA (MM); SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning)

description: Comma-separated list or descriptive text of DC locations included in this network design scenario (e.g., 'Chicago IL, Dallas TX, Atlanta GA'). Captures the geographic footprint assumption for network_design scenario type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product if structured location data is required]

Assumed OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) percentage for manufacturing lines within this scenario. Drives production capacity calculations and throughput assumptions. Lower OEE assumptions model downside capacity risk; higher values model optimized production performance.
reference: Aveva/Wonderware MES (Performance Analytics); SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning)

description: Free-text notes entered by the supply chain planner documenting key assumptions, data quality caveats, or analytical observations for this scenario. Supports S&OP meeting preparation and scenario documentation.
Time granularity of the planning periods within this scenario. Weekly buckets are used for near-term tactical planning; monthly for mid-range S&OP; quarterly for strategic horizon.
Last date of the planning horizon covered by this scenario. Together with planning_horizon_start_date, defines the full temporal scope of the scenario.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Demand Planning)

description: Total number of months covered by the planning horizon for this scenario. Typical IBP horizons range from 3 months (tactical) to 36 months (strategic network design).
First date of the planning horizon covered by this scenario. Defines the temporal scope of the supply-demand balancing analysis.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Demand Planning)

description: Replenishment signal generation method applied in this scenario. MRP (Material Requirements Planning) is driven by BOM explosion; kanban uses pull signals; reorder_point triggers at stock threshold; vendor_managed inventory (VMI) delegates to supplier; consignment uses supplier-owned stock.
reference: SAP S/4HANA (MM; PP); SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning)

description: Method used to calculate safety stock parameters within this scenario. Statistical methods use demand variability and lead time; fixed_days uses a target days-of-supply; min_max uses floor/ceiling thresholds; dynamic adjusts based on demand sensing signals.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning; Inventory Optimization)

description: Detailed narrative description of the scenario's assumptions, business context, and analytical purpose. Captures the rationale for the what-if analysis or planning hypothesis being tested.
Human-readable name of the planning scenario (e.g., 'FY25 Q2 Baseline', 'Hurricane Season Downside', 'DC Network Consolidation v2'). Used in S&OP presentations and IBP planning workbooks.
Externally-known business identifier for the scenario, used in S&OP meeting decks, planning reports, and cross-functional communications. Format: SCN-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.

Current lifecycle state of the IBP scenario within the S&OP approval workflow. Draft indicates work-in-progress; in_review is under cross-functional review; approved is consensus-accepted; rejected was not accepted; archived is retired from active planning.

Classification of the planning scenario by its analytical purpose within the IBP/S&OP process. Baseline represents the consensus plan; upside/downside represent demand or supply risk bounds; what-if tests specific assumptions; network_design evaluates DC network configuration changes.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: Numeric version counter for the scenario, incremented each time the scenario is revised or re-run. Enables version-controlled what-if analysis and comparison of scenario iterations within the same S&OP cycle.
Operational system of record from which this scenario was originated or imported. Enables data lineage tracking across the supply chain planning technology stack.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: Indicates whether this scenario incorporates supply-side constraints such as raw material availability, manufacturing capacity limits, or MOQ restrictions. True = constrained supply plan; False = unconstrained demand-driven plan.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (PP)

description: Target fill rate or case fill rate (%) assumed in this scenario for customer service level commitments. Drives safety stock calculations and replenishment signal generation. Typically expressed as OTIF (On Time In Full) or fill rate targets per SLA agreements.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning; Inventory Optimization); FDA FSMA

description: Indicates whether 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) partners are included in the distribution network assumptions for this scenario. True = 3PL capacity is modeled alongside owned DC network.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder (Transportation Management); SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning)

description: Indicates whether toll manufacturing capacity is included in the supply assumptions for this scenario. True = toll manufacturing is modeled as an available production source, relevant for capacity expansion and cost-comparison scenarios.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (PP)

description: Estimated total landed cost for the supply network under this scenario, including manufacturing COGS, inbound freight, warehousing, and outbound distribution costs. Key financial output metric for network design and strategic scenario comparison. Expressed in scenario currency.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); Oracle Cloud ERP (Financials)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the supply plan record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the supply chain manager or S&OP lead who approved this supply plan version. Required for IBP governance and approval workflow audit trail.
Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level supply planning aligns production, inventory, and distribution with the brand’s marketing objectives and sales targets.
Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or 3PL node in the supply network for which this plan defines planned receipts and shipments.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation of a production plan to an internal cost center for monthly financial reporting and variance analysis.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Posting of production costs from the supply plan to the appropriate GL account for accounting.
The scenario identifier from SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning) under which this supply plan was generated. Enables traceability back to the source IBP planning run and scenario.
Reference to the supply chain planner or IBP user responsible for authoring and owning this supply plan. Supports planner attribution and audit trail.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); Workday HCM

description: Reference to the specific version snapshot of this supply plan, enabling cycle-over-cycle comparison and plan-vs-plan variance analysis.
Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility associated with this supply plan record. Used for SKU/plant-level planning granularity.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis of each supply plan by profit center to support P&L statements.

Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Supply Plan aligns production with a specific New Product Development project; planners need the project ID to sync supply with product launch roadmap.

Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Supply planning must honor contracted volumes; linking supply_plan to sales_contract enables the Contract‑Based Production Planning process.
Reference to the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which this supply plan is generated. Drives replenishment signals and production scheduling at the SKU level.

Indicates whether supply allocation decisions are required for this SKU/DC due to constrained supply. When true, the supply plan drives allocation logic in the order management and trade management systems.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA SD (Sales and Distribution)

description: Timestamp when the supply plan was formally approved by the designated S&OP approver. Null if the plan has not yet been approved. Part of IBP governance audit trail.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Projected on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the end of the planning horizon after accounting for planned production, receipts, and shipments. Indicates supply-demand balance position.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Inventory Optimization

description: Indicates whether the planned production quantity for this SKU involves co-packing or toll manufacturing arrangements with a third-party contract manufacturer, affecting supply lead times and MOQ constraints.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP (Production Planning); TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Timestamp when this supply plan record was first captured in the data platform. Used for audit trail and IBP governance.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 22000 Food Safety Management Systems

description: Total planned demand quantity for the SKU over the planning horizon as consumed from the demand plan. Represents the demand signal used for supply-demand balancing in IBP.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Indicates whether the demand quantity in this supply plan was derived from SAP IBP Demand Sensing (short-horizon statistical signal) rather than the standard statistical forecast. Affects plan accuracy benchmarking.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning) Demand Sensing

description: The last date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the end boundary for planned production, receipts, and shipment quantities.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: The first date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the start boundary for planned production, receipts, and shipment quantities.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Total supply lead time in calendar days for the SKU from production order release to DC receipt. Used in replenishment signal timing and safety stock calculations.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Standard production lot size for the SKU at the plant, used to round planned production quantities to feasible manufacturing batch sizes in the constrained supply plan.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP (Production Planning); Aveva/Wonderware MES

description: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) constraint applied to this SKU in the supply plan. Ensures production and procurement orders meet supplier or manufacturing minimum thresholds.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management); SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning)

description: Identifies the tier of the supply network node (plant, primary DC, secondary DC, 3PL, cross-dock) for which this supply plan record is generated. Supports network design analytics.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning)

description: Free-text notes or commentary entered by the planner or approver describing key assumptions, constraints, or exceptions relevant to this supply plan version. Supports IBP governance documentation.
On-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the start of the planning horizon. Used as the baseline for supply-demand balance position calculations.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management)

description: The real-world business event timestamp when this supply plan version was generated or published in the IBP system. Distinct from the audit record creation timestamp.
Externally-known business identifier for the supply plan, used in IBP, ERP, and cross-functional S&OP communications. Follows the SP-{alphanumeric} naming convention.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Current lifecycle state of the supply plan within the IBP approval workflow. Drives downstream replenishment signal release and production scheduling actions.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Classification of the supply plan indicating whether it reflects capacity-constrained production, unconstrained demand, consensus S&OP output, baseline reference, or simulation scenario.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Total planned production quantity for the SKU at the plant over the planning horizon, expressed in the SKU's base unit of measure. Key volume metric driving MES scheduling and capacity allocation.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA PP (Production Planning); Aveva/Wonderware MES

description: Total planned inbound receipts quantity for the SKU at the DC over the planning horizon, including transfers from plants and external supplier deliveries. Key volume metric for supply-demand balancing.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Total planned outbound shipments quantity for the SKU from the DC over the planning horizon. Drives replenishment signals and allocation decisions when supply is constrained.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Identifies the IBP/S&OP planning cycle (e.g., monthly-2024-06) to which this supply plan belongs. Enables cycle-over-cycle comparison and governance tracking.
Free-text reason provided by the approver when a supply plan is rejected in the IBP approval workflow. Null when plan_status is not 'rejected'. Supports governance audit trail.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Indicates whether the replenishment signal derived from this supply plan has been released to the ERP or WMS for execution. Tracks plan-to-execution handoff status.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management)

description: Inventory rotation policy applied to this SKU in the supply plan. FIFO (First In First Out) and FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard in food and beverage to minimize waste and ensure food safety compliance.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); ISO 22000 Food Safety Management Systems; GFSI

description: Minimum buffer inventory quantity for the SKU at the DC/plant, set to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Drives replenishment trigger signals in IBP.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Inventory Optimization

description: Remaining shelf life in days for the SKU as applied in this supply plan. Governs FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation policy and constrains planned shipment sequencing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM (Quality Management); FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); ISO 22000

description: Operational system of record from which this supply plan record originated. Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; Oracle Cloud ERP

description: Indicates whether this supply plan record reflects a constrained supply scenario where planned production or receipts are insufficient to fully satisfy planned demand. Triggers allocation decision workflows.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Net supply-demand balance position for the SKU over the planning horizon (planned supply minus planned demand). Positive values indicate surplus; negative values indicate a supply gap requiring allocation decisions.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Base unit of measure for all quantity fields in this supply plan record (e.g., CS=Case, EA=Each, KG=Kilogram). Aligns with the SKU's base UOM in SAP S/4HANA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|OZ — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management); ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: Timestamp when this supply plan record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks revisions and supports audit trail for IBP governance.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Sequential integer version number of this supply plan snapshot within a planning cycle. Increments with each revision, enabling plan-vs-plan variance analysis.
Business-facing identifier for the plan version, used to reference this version from supply_plan and demand_plan records. Enables cross-product linkage during the FK resolution step.
Identifier of the individual who approved or rejected this plan version. Required for S&OP governance and audit trail.
Identifier of the SAP IBP scenario (e.g., base case, upside, downside) associated with this plan version. Enables what-if scenario comparison across S&OP iterations.
Reference to the immediately preceding plan version within the same planning cycle. Enables direct plan-vs-plan variance analysis and version lineage tracing for IBP governance.
Identifier of the supply planner who created or owns this plan version. Supports accountability and audit trail for IBP governance.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); Workday HCM (Core HR)

description: Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Each Supply Plan Version reflects the version of the product roadmap from an R&D project; linking enables version‑controlled supply forecasting.

business_glossary_term: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle ID
description: Reference to the S&OP / IBP planning cycle that produced this version. Links the version to the formal monthly or weekly IBP governance cycle.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: Indicates whether supply allocation across customers or channels is required in this plan version due to constrained supply. Triggers allocation management processes in SAP IBP or S/4HANA.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (SD)

description: Current workflow state of this plan version within the S&OP governance process. Draft versions are in progress; pending_review awaits sign-off; approved versions are the active baseline; superseded versions have been replaced by a newer version.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 (Quality Management Systems)

description: Date and time when this plan version was formally approved by the designated approver. Null if not yet approved. Critical for S&OP governance audit trail.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 (Quality Management Systems)

description: Distribution channel scope covered by this plan version (e.g., all channels, retail only, foodservice, direct-to-consumer, export). Supports channel-specific supply planning and trade management alignment.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud)

description: Indicates whether co-packing (contract manufacturing) volumes are included in the planned production and receipts quantities for this version. Relevant for toll manufacturing and co-packer network planning.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (MM)

description: Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this plan version belongs (e.g., P01 through P13 for 13-period calendars). Aligns supply plan versions with financial reporting periods.
Fiscal year to which this plan version primarily belongs (e.g., FY2025). Used for financial alignment of supply plans with budgeting and EBITDA reporting cycles.
Weighted mean absolute percentage error (WMAPE) or equivalent forecast accuracy metric used as a planning assumption for this version. Informs safety stock and buffer inventory parameters.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Demand Planning)

description: The last date of the planning horizon covered by this version. Together with horizon_start_date, defines the full temporal scope of the supply plan.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: The first date of the planning horizon covered by this version. Defines the temporal scope of the supply plan snapshot.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: Indicates whether this is the currently active (latest approved) version of the supply plan for the given planning cycle. Only one version per planning cycle should be active at any time.
Flags this version as the official baseline plan for the planning cycle, used as the reference point for variance analysis and performance measurement against actuals.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: The exact date and time when this plan version snapshot was created in the system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the version record and supports audit trail for IBP governance.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 22000 (Food Safety Management Systems)

description: Externally-known business identifier for the supply plan (e.g., SP-2024-000123). Used in cross-functional communications, ERP references, and EDI exchanges.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (PP)

description: Geographic or organizational scope of this plan version. Indicates whether the version covers the full global network, a regional subset, a national market, the DC network, or a single plant.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: Classification of the supply plan by planning horizon and purpose. Operational plans cover short-term execution; tactical cover mid-term; strategic cover long-range network design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operational|tactical|strategic|contingency|baseline — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: Frequency cadence of the planning cycle that generated this version (e.g., monthly S&OP, weekly demand sensing refresh). Drives scheduling of IBP governance reviews.
Date and time when this plan version was published to downstream execution systems (SAP S/4HANA, MES, WMS). Null if not yet published. Supports SLA tracking for plan-to-execution lead time.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (PP)

description: Indicates whether this plan version has been published and communicated to downstream stakeholders (e.g., manufacturing, procurement, logistics). Published versions trigger execution workflows.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (PP)

description: Explanation provided by the approver when rejecting this plan version. Null if the version was not rejected. Supports governance audit trail and continuous improvement of the S&OP process.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 (Quality Management Systems)

description: Target fill rate or OTIF (On Time In Full) service level percentage used as a planning input for this version (e.g., 98.5%). Drives safety stock calculations and replenishment signal parameters.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Inventory Optimization)

description: Operational system of record from which this plan version was originated or extracted (e.g., SAP_IBP, JDA_BLUE_YONDER). Supports data lineage and Silver layer provenance tracking.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: Indicates whether this plan version was generated under known supply constraints (e.g., raw material shortage, capacity bottleneck, supplier disruption). Triggers allocation and prioritization workflows.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (PP)

description: Net supply-demand balance at the version level (total planned supply minus total demand). Positive values indicate surplus; negative values indicate a supply gap requiring resolution.
Aggregate consensus demand volume across all SKUs and channels captured in this plan version. Enables plan-vs-plan demand variance analysis across S&OP cycles.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Demand Planning)

description: Aggregate planned production volume across all SKUs and plants captured in this plan version. Key volume metric for S&OP executive review and capacity utilization analysis.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (PP)

description: Aggregate planned inbound receipts (from suppliers, co-packers, and toll manufacturers) across all SKUs and DCs captured in this plan version. Supports supply-demand balancing at the version level.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (MM)

description: Aggregate planned outbound shipment volume across all SKUs and DCs in this plan version. Used for OTIF target-setting and distribution network capacity planning.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Transportation Management)

description: Date and time when this plan version record was last modified. Supports change tracking and data lineage in the Silver layer lakehouse.
Human-readable label for this version (e.g., 'Baseline', 'Consensus', 'Final', 'Pre-S&OP', 'Executive Review'). Used in reporting and S&OP presentations to distinguish versions.
Indicates whether this plan version has been locked and is no longer editable. Locked versions serve as immutable reference points for variance analysis and regulatory audit purposes.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 22000 (Food Safety Management Systems)

description: Free-text notes from the planner describing key assumptions, constraints, or changes incorporated in this version (e.g., 'Adjusted for Q3 promotional uplift', 'Reflects raw material shortage at Plant A').
Sequential integer version number within a planning cycle (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Increments each time a new snapshot is created for the same planning cycle, enabling plan-vs-plan variance analysis.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder (Supply Planning)

description: Unit of measure applied to all volume quantity fields in this plan version (e.g., CS for cases, EA for eaches, KG for kilograms). Ensures consistent interpretation of volume metrics across planning systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); SAP S/4HANA (MM); GS1 Standards

description: Primary key for planning_exception

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Exception Management process records which customer account's demand caused a planning exception, supporting root‑cause analysis and service level reporting.
Reference to the Distribution Center or planning location node where the supply-demand imbalance or constraint violation was detected.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capture the cost impact of planning exceptions in the associated cost center for exception reporting.
Reference to the supply planner responsible for reviewing and resolving this exception. Drives daily planner workbench assignment and exception-based management workflows.
Reference to the specific SKU affected by this supply planning exception. Identifies the finished good, semi-finished, or raw material involved in the imbalance.

business_glossary_term: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle ID
description: Reference to the IBP/S&OP planning cycle run during which this exception was generated. Links the exception to the specific planning iteration for traceability.

Sales or distribution channel associated with the demand driving this exception. Retail: traditional grocery/mass/club. Foodservice: restaurants and institutional. DTC: direct-to-consumer. Ecommerce: online retail. Export: international distribution.
Timestamp when this exception record was first written to the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 22000; FSMA; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

description: Total demand quantity (in planning UOM) for the affected SKU at the affected location within the exception time bucket, as calculated by the IBP or MRP run. Represents the demand side of the supply-demand imbalance.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: The date and time when the supply planning exception was first detected and raised by the MRP or IBP balancing run. Represents the principal business event time for this record.
Target date by which the planner must resolve or escalate the exception to prevent downstream supply chain impact. Drives SLA compliance tracking on the planner workbench.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Free-text description of the reason this exception was escalated beyond the standard planner workbench. Populated only when is_escalated is True. Supports root cause analysis and S&OP review.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: End date of the planning time bucket in which the supply-demand imbalance or constraint violation occurs. Together with start date defines the affected planning window.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Start date of the planning time bucket (week, period, or day) in which the supply-demand imbalance or constraint violation occurs. Used for time-phased exception analysis.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Externally-visible business identifier for the supply planning exception, used in planner workbench, exception reports, and cross-system communication (e.g., ERP, IBP). Format: EXC-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Current lifecycle state of the supply planning exception within the planner workbench workflow. Open: newly raised, awaiting review. In_review: planner actively working resolution. Resolved: corrective action applied. Escalated: elevated to senior planner or S&OP team. Closed: no further action required.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Classification of the supply planning exception category. Shortage: supply insufficient to meet demand. Excess: supply exceeds demand beyond safety stock. Late delivery: inbound supply arrives after required date. MOQ violation: order quantity below Minimum Order Quantity. Shelf life risk: inventory at risk of expiry before consumption. Allocation breach: customer or channel allocation limit exceeded.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; FSMA

description: Estimated percentage point reduction in order fill rate attributable to this exception if unresolved. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 5.25 = 5.25% fill rate reduction). Supports customer service level impact quantification.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Fiscal period (P01–P13) in which the exception time bucket falls, aligned to the company's fiscal calendar. Enables exception volume and severity reporting by financial period for S&OP and IBP cycle reviews.
Fiscal year in which the exception time bucket falls. Used for year-over-year exception trend analysis and annual S&OP performance reporting.

Net quantity gap between supply and demand (supply_qty minus demand_qty) for the affected SKU and location in the exception bucket. Negative value indicates shortage; positive value indicates excess. Core metric for exception prioritization.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether the exception was automatically resolved by the planning system without planner intervention (True) or required manual planner action (False). Supports automation rate KPI tracking and continuous improvement.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether this exception has been escalated to a senior supply planner, S&OP team, or management for resolution (True) or remains at standard planner level (False).
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Minimum Order Quantity constraint applicable to the affected SKU from the relevant supplier or manufacturing plant. Populated when exception_type is moq_violation to quantify the constraint breach.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle Cloud Procurement; FSMA

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Impact Flag
description: Indicates whether this exception is projected to negatively impact On Time In Full (OTIF) delivery performance to customers if not resolved (True). Used to prioritize exceptions with direct customer service level consequences.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; FDA FSMA

description: Free-text notes entered by the supply planner documenting analysis, context, interim actions, or rationale for resolution decisions. Supports knowledge transfer and audit trail.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Planning horizon bucket in which the exception falls. Short_term: 0–4 weeks (operational). Mid_term: 5–13 weeks (tactical). Long_term: 14+ weeks (strategic). Determines urgency and escalation path within the S&OP process.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Date and time of the MRP or IBP planning run that generated this exception. Enables traceability of exceptions to specific planning engine executions and supports run-over-run comparison.

Unit of measure in which demand_qty, supply_qty, and imbalance_qty are expressed for this exception (e.g., CS=Case, EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Litre, GAL=Gallon, MT=Metric Ton, PAL=Pallet). Aligns with SAP IBP planning UOM configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|PAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP; SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 80000

description: System-generated or planner-entered recommended corrective action to resolve the exception (e.g., 'Expedite PO #12345', 'Reallocate 500 CS from DC-West', 'Reduce production run by 200 MT', 'Approve substitution to SKU-98765'). Drives exception-based management workflows.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Free-text description of the actual corrective action applied by the planner to resolve the exception. Captured at resolution time for audit trail and continuous improvement analysis.

Categorical classification of the resolution method applied to close the exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: expedite|reallocation|substitution|demand_reduction|production_increase|accepted_risk|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Date and time when the exception was marked as resolved or closed by the planner. Used to calculate resolution cycle time and SLA adherence metrics.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Inventory rotation policy applicable to the affected SKU at the affected DC. FIFO (First In First Out): oldest stock consumed first. FEFO (First Expired First Out): stock closest to expiry consumed first — standard for perishable food and beverage products. LIFO (Last In First Out): rarely used in food and beverage.
reference: FSMA; FDA 21 CFR; GFSI; ISO 22000; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Configured safety stock quantity for the affected SKU at the affected DC at the time the exception was raised. Used to assess whether the imbalance breaches the safety stock buffer and to calibrate exception severity.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Business impact severity level assigned to the exception, used to prioritize planner workbench queue. Critical: immediate risk to customer service or food safety. High: significant supply disruption within the planning horizon. Medium: manageable imbalance requiring near-term action. Low: informational, minor deviation.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; GFSI

description: Number of days of remaining shelf life for the at-risk inventory lot at the time the exception was raised. Populated when exception_type is shelf_life_risk. Supports FEFO rotation policy enforcement and food safety compliance.
reference: FSMA; FDA 21 CFR; GFSI; ISO 22000; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Minimum acceptable remaining shelf life (in days) configured for the SKU at the DC, below which a shelf_life_risk exception is triggered. Defined per SKU-DC combination in the planning system.
reference: FSMA; FDA 21 CFR; GFSI; ISO 22000; SAP IBP

description: Native exception identifier or alert number from the originating system of record (e.g., SAP IBP alert ID, JDA exception number). Enables cross-system traceability and deduplication.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Operational system of record that generated or originated this supply planning exception. Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Silver layer.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer; SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder

description: Total available supply quantity (in planning UOM) for the affected SKU at the affected location within the exception time bucket, including on-hand inventory, open purchase orders, and planned production orders.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this exception record, including status changes, planner notes, or resolution actions. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 22000; FSMA; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

business_glossary_term: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for the S&OP/Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle record. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete, recurring cross-functional planning event with a defined lifecycle (draft → in-progress → closed).

Name or employee identifier of the individual accountable for facilitating and driving the S&OP/IBP cycle to completion. Typically the Supply Chain Planning Director or S&OP Process Manager. Serves as the primary point of contact for cross-functional escalations.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A IBP; SAP IBP — Cycle Ownership

description: Total number of cross-functional participants who attended the S&OP/IBP cycle meetings (demand review, supply review, pre-S&OP, and executive review combined). Used as a process maturity indicator and governance compliance metric.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A IBP — Meeting Governance

description: Aggregate manufacturing capacity utilization percentage (0–100) for the planning period as assessed during the supply review of this S&OP cycle. Derived from the constrained supply plan vs. available production capacity. Values above 85% typically trigger capacity escalation discussions.
reference: SAP IBP — Capacity Planning; Aveva/Wonderware MES — OEE Analytics

description: Free-text narrative summarizing the key cross-functional decisions, trade-offs, and commitments agreed upon during this S&OP/IBP cycle. Captures the rationale for scenario selection, supply gap resolutions, and any deviations from the financial operating plan.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A IBP — Decision Documentation; SAP IBP

description: Unit of measure for the consensus demand volume approved in this S&OP cycle (e.g., CS = cases, EA = each, KG = kilograms). Ensures consistent interpretation of volume figures across demand, supply, and finance teams. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP — UOM; GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standards

description: Total consensus demand volume (in standard cases or agreed unit of measure) approved by cross-functional teams during this S&OP cycle. Represents the agreed-upon demand signal used to drive supply planning, procurement, and manufacturing scheduling for the planning period.
reference: SAP IBP — Consensus Demand; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Financial revenue target (in reporting currency) aligned and approved during this S&OP/IBP cycle. Represents the commercial commitment reconciled between the demand plan and the financial operating plan. Used for EBITDA tracking and financial close alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Profitability Analysis; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: Date and time when this S&OP cycle record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, compliance reporting, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 22000 — Record Keeping; FSMA — Traceability Records

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this S&OP cycle record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency global operations.
reference: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: Date and time when this S&OP/IBP cycle was formally closed and the approved consensus plan was locked for execution. Marks the end of the active planning window. Used for cycle duration KPI measurement and audit compliance.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Cycle Closure; Oliver Wight IBP

description: Free-text field for additional commentary, risk flags, assumptions, or contextual notes recorded by the S&OP cycle owner or cross-functional team. May include references to external market events, supply disruptions, regulatory changes, or strategic initiatives impacting the planning cycle.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A IBP — Assumptions Documentation

description: Externally-known business identifier for the S&OP/IBP cycle, formatted as SOP-YYYY-MM-NNN (e.g., SOP-2024-10-001). Used in cross-functional communications, meeting agendas, and audit trails to uniquely reference a planning cycle. Maps to SAP IBP Planning Cycle ID.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Cycle; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Date and time when this S&OP/IBP cycle was formally opened and made active in the planning system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp marking the start of the cycle's active planning window. Used for SLA tracking and cycle duration analytics.

Current lifecycle state of the S&OP/IBP cycle. Drives workflow routing, access controls, and downstream planning system locks. Draft: cycle initiated but not yet active. In Progress: milestone reviews underway. Pending Approval: awaiting executive sign-off. Approved: consensus plan ratified. Closed: cycle archived. Cancelled: cycle voided.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Cycle Status; Oliver Wight IBP Maturity Model

description: Classification of the planning cycle cadence and scope. Determines which cross-functional teams participate and which decisions are in scope. Monthly S&OP covers full supply-demand balancing; weekly demand review focuses on near-term demand sensing; executive IBP covers strategic financial reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monthly_sop|weekly_demand_review|executive_ibp|supply_review|pre_sop|demand_review — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP — IBP for S&OP; Oliver Wight Class A S&OP Framework

description: Version identifier of the demand plan (from SAP IBP or JDA/Blue Yonder) that was reviewed and approved during this S&OP cycle. Enables traceability between the S&OP cycle record and the specific demand plan version used as input to supply planning and financial reconciliation.
reference: SAP IBP — Plan Version Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Scheduled or actual date of the Demand Review milestone within this S&OP cycle. During the demand review, commercial and demand planning teams align on the unconstrained consensus demand forecast, incorporating statistical forecasts, market intelligence, and promotional uplifts.
reference: SAP IBP — S&OP Demand Review; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Scheduled or actual date of the Executive S&OP or IBP review meeting where senior leadership approves the consensus plan, resolves escalated supply-demand gaps, and aligns on financial commitments and strategic trade-offs.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A IBP; SAP IBP — Executive IBP Review

description: Status of the financial reconciliation between the consensus volume plan and the financial operating plan (budget/latest estimate) at the close of this S&OP cycle. Aligned: volume plan supports financial targets. Gap Identified: volume shortfall vs. financial plan. Escalated: gap referred to CFO/leadership. Resolved: gap closed via scenario or plan adjustment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Profitability Analysis; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials; Oliver Wight IBP Financial Integration

description: The fiscal period (month number 1–13 depending on calendar) within the fiscal year to which this S&OP cycle belongs. Enables alignment of planning cycle outcomes with period-close financial reporting and EBITDA reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Fiscal Period; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: The fiscal year to which this S&OP cycle belongs, as defined in the corporate financial calendar (e.g., 2024). Used for financial reconciliation, budget alignment, and year-over-year planning comparisons.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Fiscal Year Variant; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: Target days of supply (DOS) for finished goods inventory approved during this S&OP cycle. Reflects the inventory optimization policy balancing service level targets, working capital constraints, and shelf life (FEFO) requirements for the planning period.
reference: SAP IBP — Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Inventory Planning

description: Indicates whether this S&OP cycle's consensus plan has been locked for execution (True), preventing further modifications to the approved demand and supply plan. Locking triggers downstream replenishment signals, procurement purchase orders, and manufacturing schedules.
reference: SAP IBP — Plan Lock; JDA/Blue Yonder Planning Freeze

description: Number of new product development (NPD) SKUs included in the demand and supply plans reviewed during this S&OP cycle. Tracks the volume of innovation pipeline items requiring dedicated supply chain readiness assessment and launch planning.
reference: SAP IBP — New Product Introduction; PLM Integration

description: Number of open action items carried forward from this S&OP cycle at the time of cycle closure. Tracks cross-functional accountability and process discipline. A high count may indicate unresolved supply-demand gaps or escalation items requiring follow-up in the next cycle.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A S&OP — Action Item Tracking

description: Number of months covered by the planning horizon reviewed in this S&OP cycle. Typically 18–24 months for strategic IBP cycles and 3–6 months for operational S&OP cycles in the Food and Beverage industry. Drives the time fence for supply-demand balancing decisions.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Horizon; Oliver Wight Class A IBP

description: Last calendar date of the planning horizon governed by this S&OP cycle. Together with planning_period_start_date, defines the full time fence for supply-demand balancing decisions made in this cycle.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Horizon; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: First calendar date of the planning horizon governed by this S&OP cycle. Defines the start boundary for demand, supply, inventory, and financial plans reviewed and approved within this cycle. Typically the first day of the planning month or week.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Horizon; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Scheduled or actual date of the Pre-S&OP (reconciliation) meeting where supply, demand, and finance teams resolve gaps between the demand plan and constrained supply plan before escalating unresolved issues to the executive S&OP review.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A S&OP; SAP IBP — Pre-S&OP Step

description: Percentage of total consensus demand volume attributable to trade promotions and promotional uplifts in the planning period reviewed during this S&OP cycle. High promotional volume percentages increase demand variability and require additional safety stock and supply flexibility.
reference: SAP IBP — Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder TPM Integration

description: Monetary gap (in reporting currency) between the consensus revenue plan approved in this S&OP cycle and the financial operating plan (budget or latest estimate). Positive values indicate revenue shortfall vs. financial plan. Key input for executive escalation and corrective action planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Variance Analysis; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: Indicates whether safety stock parameters were formally reviewed and updated during this S&OP cycle (True) or carried forward unchanged from the prior cycle (False). Safety stock reviews are triggered by significant changes in demand variability, lead times, or service level targets.
reference: SAP IBP — Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Inventory Planning

description: Name or label of the supply-demand scenario approved by the executive team during this S&OP cycle (e.g., 'Base Case', 'Upside Demand', 'Supply Constrained'). Identifies which planning scenario was selected as the consensus plan for execution.
reference: SAP IBP — Scenario Planning; Oliver Wight IBP Scenario Management

description: Classification of the approved planning scenario for this S&OP cycle. Base Case: most likely outcome. Upside: optimistic demand/supply scenario. Downside: pessimistic scenario. Supply Constrained: demand exceeds available supply. Risk Adjusted: incorporates identified supply chain risk mitigations.
reference: SAP IBP — Scenario Planning; Oliver Wight Class A IBP

description: Target customer service level (fill rate or OTIF percentage) approved for the planning period during this S&OP cycle. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.50 = 98.5%). Drives safety stock parameters and replenishment signal calibration across the DC network.
reference: SAP IBP — Service Level Optimization; SLA Framework

description: Operational system of record from which this S&OP cycle record was sourced or initiated. SAP_IBP: SAP Integrated Business Planning. JDA_BLUE_YONDER: JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning. ORACLE_ERP: Oracle Cloud ERP. MANUAL: manually entered outside a planning system.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder; Oracle Cloud ERP

description: Volumetric gap (in consensus demand UOM) between the unconstrained consensus demand plan and the constrained supply plan identified during this S&OP cycle. Positive values indicate supply shortfall; negative values indicate supply surplus. Key input for executive escalation and scenario planning.
reference: SAP IBP — Supply-Demand Balancing; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Scheduled or actual date of the Supply Review milestone within this S&OP cycle. Supply planning teams assess capacity constraints, raw material availability, MOQ compliance, and FIFO/FEFO inventory rotation to produce a constrained supply plan.
reference: SAP IBP — S&OP Supply Review; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Date and time when this S&OP cycle record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer and audit trail requirements for planning governance.
reference: ISO 22000 — Record Keeping; SAP IBP — Change Log

description: System-generated unique identifier for the account record.

Foreign key linking to customer.bill_to_location. Business justification: Account billing address fields are duplicated; linking to bill_to_location centralizes address data and removes redundant columns.

Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Credit information is detailed in credit_profile; moving credit_rating and payment_terms to credit_profile normalizes credit data.

Foreign key linking to customer.edi_trading_partner. Business justification: EDI trading partner configuration belongs to a B2B account; adding FK enables direct association without creating a bidirectional link.
Identifier of the internal sales representative managing the account.
Identifier of the loyalty program to which the account belongs.

Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Preferred Supplier Assignment process links each key account to its primary supplier for contract negotiation and service level management.

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: REQUIRED: Assign a default price list to each customer account for pricing engine calculations; standard practice in F&B account‑level pricing.
Identifier of the sales territory responsible for the account.
Human‑readable name of the account (e.g., retailer name, consumer name).
External business number used to identify the account in contracts and transactions.
Current operational status of the account.
Category that defines the nature of the account holder.
Revenue range band for the account (e.g., <$1M, $1‑5M, $5‑10M, >$10M).
Primary sales channel through which the account purchases products.
Date‑time when the account record was first created.
Date when the account agreement ends or is scheduled to terminate (null for open‑ended).

Must comply with FDA FALCPA (Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act) and FSMA requirements. Used in consumer marketing and retail planogram placement.

description: Total approved annual marketing investment budget allocated to the brand, expressed in the functional currency. Includes above-the-line (ATL) advertising, below-the-line (BTL) shopper marketing, digital, and trade promotion spend. Confidential financial data managed in Oracle Cloud ERP Financials and SAP CO cost centers.
reference: Oracle Cloud ERP Financials; SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; EBITDA Reporting Framework

description: Classification of the brand within the corporate brand architecture hierarchy. 'masterbrand' = umbrella brand covering multiple product lines; 'sub_brand' = brand operating under a masterbrand; 'endorsed' = independent brand with parent brand endorsement; 'standalone' = fully independent brand; 'private_label' = retailer-owned brand manufactured by Food Beverage under co-packing or toll manufacturing arrangements.
reference: Brand Architecture Framework; Nielsen/IRI Brand Hierarchy Standards

description: Unaided or total brand awareness percentage among the target consumer segment, expressed as a value between 0.00 and 100.00. Sourced from Nielsen/IRI consumer panel studies or commissioned brand health tracking research. Key input to brand equity score calculation and marketing investment justification.

description: Primary consumer product category to which the brand belongs (e.g., 'Snacks', 'Beverages', 'Dairy', 'Packaged Foods', 'RTD Coffee', 'RTE Cereals'). Aligns with Nielsen/IRI/SPINS syndicated data category taxonomy for market tracking and ACV/TDP reporting. description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the brand, used across SAP S/4HANA, Oracle ERP, and trade systems. Serves as the natural key for cross-system integration and EDI transactions. Example: 'SNKBR-001', 'BEV-RTD-02'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Brand Master; GS1 GTIN Standards

description: Official commercial name of the brand as registered and used in consumer-facing marketing, packaging, and trade communications. description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual marketing budget (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Ensures correct financial reporting and multi-currency consolidation in Oracle Cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA FI.

description: Indicates whether the brand carries a clean label certification or commitment (no artificial colors, flavors, preservatives, or GMO ingredients). description: Timestamp when the brand record was first created in the enterprise data platform (Databricks Silver Layer). Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management.

description: Brand identifier as recorded in Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud / Marketing Cloud). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse brand master and Salesforce campaign and consumer engagement records. Enables brand-level marketing performance attribution.
